TXS 0506+056 blazar is more massive as compared to BL Lacertae. Keeping this in mind allows us to qualitatively compare the DM density spikes and LOS integrals from these two blazars.

sources.summary: The paper links TXS0506+056 to the IceCube neutrino and tests whether blazar jet models can explain its photon/neutrino behavior, with hadronic contributions limited by X-ray and TeV constraints.

quotes.quote: In September 2017, the IceCube Neutrino Observatory recorded a very-high-energy neutrino in directional coincidence with a blazar in an unusually bright gamma-ray state, TXS0506+056 . Blazars are prominent photon sources in the universe because they harbor a relativistic jet whose radiation is strongly collimated and amplified. High-energy atomic nuclei known as cosmic rays can produce neutrinos; thus the recent detection may help identifying the sources of the diffuse neutrino flux and the energetic cosmic rays.

We propose the hybrid model displayed in hybridfit , in which the bulk of photon emission is of leptonic origin, and hadronic contributions are as strong as permitted by the X-ray data. In addition, the outflow of matter and radiation in the blazar should not be so powerful that the surrounding matter is blown away, otherwise the activity of the central black hole would be quenched, which for continuous and isotropic emission leads to the so-called Eddington limit. Modeling the flare on the basis of an increase in the particle-acceleration power alone will invariably require a jet power that is in excess of the Eddington luminosity by several orders of magnitude as discussed in the Supplementary Information , see Supplementary Figure 1. It is known that so-called proton synchrotron models may alleviate the tension with the Eddington limit , but they do not simultaneously reproduce the SED of TXS0506+056 and the energy and flux of neutrino emission, see Supplementary Figure 4; similar arguments apply to models with higher maximum proton energy, see Supplementary Figure 2.

sources.summary: The paper models TXS 0506+056 as a neutrino-associated blazar whose jet-sheath environment can reproduce multiwavelength emission, VHE spectral steepening, and proton-accelerated neutrino production.

quotes.quote: A neutrino with energy of 290 TeV, IceCube-170922A, was detected in coincidence with the BL Lac object TXS 0506+056 during enhanced gamma-ray activity, with chance coincidence being rejected at 3 level. We monitored the object in the very-high-energy (VHE) band with the MAGIC telescopes for 41 hours from 1.3 to 40.4 days after the neutrino detection. Day-timescale variability is clearly resolved.

Model description Hereafter we model the broadband EM and neutrino emission from TXS 0506+056, building on the jet-sheath scenario of Ghisellini:2004ec,Tavecchio:2014eia . These earlier studies focused on the leptonic emission and/or the neutrino emission and lacked explicit treatment of hadronic radiative processes. Here we present the first extension of the jet-sheath model to account for all relevant hadronic processes, as well as a first comparison of the model with multi-messenger observations of a particular object.

The combined MWL and neutrino observations of TXS 0506+056 can be interpreted consistently in terms of electrons and protons that are accelerated in the same region of the jet and interact with external low-energy photons, originating from the slower sheath surrounding the faster jet spine. Such jet-sheath structure is independently well motivated.

sources.summary: The paper treats TXS 0506+056 as the blazar associated with IceCube-170922A, but finds it quiescent during the 2014-2015 neutrino flare while comparing its gamma-ray behavior with PKS 0502+049.

quotes.quote: Recently the IceCube collaboration and 15 other collaborations reported the spatial and temporal coincidence between the neutrino event IceCube-170922A and the radio-TeV activity of the blazar TXS 0506+056. Their further analysis on 9.5 years of IceCube data discovered neutrino flare between September 2014 and March 2015, when TXS 0506+056 is however in ``quiescent" state. We analyze the Fermi-LAT data in that direction, and find another bright GeV source PKS 0502+049, which is at an angle of 1.2^ from TXS 0506+056, with strong activties during the neutrino flare. No other bright GeV source was detected in the region of interest.

The TXS 0506+056 is a blazar active in the optical-TeV energy range. Though its properties is found typical among the bright Fermi-LAT blazars , it has very strong GeV activities among BL Lacs and is located at a declination toward which the IceCube has highest sensitivity . It is the first time of finding the association between neutrino and point source with such a high significance.

sources.summary: The paper treats TXS 0506+056 as a blazar neutrino-source candidate whose single-event flare association is vulnerable to Eddington bias, while additional events allow better time-averaged and flare flux estimates.

quotes.quote: Several associations between individual high-energy events and potential electromagnetic counterparts have been suggested, the most significant being the detection of a single high-energy event in coincidence with a very high-energy gamma-ray flare from the blazar TXS 0506+056 txsmmpaper . A subsequent search for additional neutrino emission from this source revealed a flare of 13 5 lower energy events several years earlier txsneutrinopaper .

The neutrino source candidate TXS 0506+056 was initially found through the coincidence of a single high-energy neutrino event and a gamma-ray flare txsmmpaper . When restricting the multiwavelength analysis to the properties of the observed gamma-ray flare, the neutrino luminosity thus cannot be estimated reliably and specific emission models should not be ruled out based on their low predicted neutrino flux (see, e.g., gao2018, keivani2018 ). The appropriate trial factor here is not only the number of similar sources, but rather the rate density of similar blazar flares, and hence might be even larger than the effective density of BL Lac objects shown in Fig. fig:comparison . There is evidence that additional neutrino events have been detected from TXS 0506+056 txsneutrinopaper . In this case the time-averaged source flux, as well as the neutrino flux during the 2014/2015 flare, can be measured accurately based on the large number of detected events.

sources.summary: The paper identifies TXS 0506+056 as a luminous flaring blazar linked to IceCube neutrinos, multiwavelength follow-up, TeV emission, and possible cosmic-ray acceleration.

quotes.quote: This blazar, TXS 0506+056, had been relatively poorly studied until now, although it was identified as the highest energy gamma ray source detected by EGRET from any blazar with two photons above 40 GeV . The set of observations triggered by the September 22 neutrino has yielded a treasure trove of multiwavelength data that will allow us to probe the physics of the first cosmic ray accelerator. An optical telescope eventually measured its distance , which was found to be 4 billion light-years. Its large distance points to a special galaxy, which sets it apart from the ten-times-closer blazars such as the Markarian sources that dominate the extreme gamma-ray sky observed by NASA's Fermi satellite.

TXS 0506+056 was originally flagged by the Fermi and Swift satellite telescopes. Follow-up observations with the MAGIC air Cherenkov telescope identified it as a rare TeV blazar with the potential to produce the very high energy neutrino detected by IceCube.

It is important to realize that nearby blazars like the Markarian sources are at a redshift that is ten times smaller, and therefore TXS 0506+056, with a similar flux despite the greater distance, is one of the most luminous sources in the Universe.

sources.summary: The paper treats TXS 0506+056 as a BL Lac blazar counterpart to IC-170922, with gamma-ray activity near the neutrino event and earlier VHE neutrino excess.

quotes.quote: On September 2017 a first significant association (at the level of 3 ) was announced: the gamma-ray flaring blazar of the BL Lac class, TXS 0506+056, was identified as a likely e.m. counterpart to the IceCube event IC-170922 2018Sci...361.1378I . Furthermore, from the analysis of archival data, an excess of VHE neutrinos from the direction of the same source has been also detected in 2014/2015 2018Sci...361..147I . TXS 0506+056 has thus suggested as the first 1 extragalactic neutrino point-like source ever detected.

The e.m follow-up triggered by the GCN Notice and the GCN Circular announcing the identification of an EHE neutrino event by IceCube GCN21916 allowed the identification of the blazar BL Lac TXS 0506+056 (also known as 5BZB J0509+0541 2015Ap&SS.357...75M ) as the likely counterpart of the IceCube event 2018Sci...361.1378I . 0.5 0.5 Using GRID data with energies above 400 MeV in a time interval of three days close to the neutrino event T _0 , we obtained a better positional agreement of the detection with the TXS 0506+059 source, contained within the IC-170922 error box (see Fig. fig6 , left panel ), which thus confirms the gamma-ray activity observed from the source during this period 2017ATel10791....1T, 2018Sci...361.1378I . As reported in 2018Sci...361.1378I , the source has been active in gamma-rays since several months before Sept. 2017. Figure fig6 , right panel , shows the gamma-ray lightcurve above 100 MeV from the beginning of August till the end of Sept. 2017, estimated on the TXS 0506+056 position.

sources.summary: The review identifies TXS 0506+056 as the likely IceCube neutrino-source blazar, noting its 2017 GeV/VHE flare, archival 2014-2015 neutrino excess, measured neutrino fluence, and quiescent multi-wavelength state.

quotes.quote: This picture changed with the detection of the 290 TeV neutrino IceCube-170922A from a direction consistent with the blazar TXS 0506+056 IceCube18a on September 22, 2017. The blazar was in an extended GeV ray flaring state in September -- October 2017 (see Fig. TXS0506lightcurve ), as detected by Fermi LAT, and was subsequently also detected in VHE rays by MAGIC. This single neutrino, however, only had a 50 likelihood of actually being of astrophysical origin (due to its moderate energy), thus, by itself, providing only marginal evidence for the association. Furthermore, being only one single event, it allowed for the calculation of only flux upper limits (see Fig. TXS0506SED ).

In an archival search for additional neutrino events from the direction of TXS 0506+056, however, the IceCube collaboration found evidence for an excess of 13 5 astrophysical high-energy muon-neutrinos from that location during an extended 5 month long period in 2014 -- 2015 IceCube18b , henceforth termed the ``neutrino flare'' (see Fig. TXS0506nuflare ). This provided the first strong hint for TXS 0506+056 being a source of high-energy neutrinos, and allowed for the first calculation of a measured high-energy neutrino flux from an astrophysical source, corresponding to an all-flavour fluence (after correcting for neutrino oscillations) of 4.2^ +2.0 _ 1.4 10^ 3 erg cm ^ 2 with a spectrum between 32 TeV and 3.6 PeV fitted by a power-law with spectral index = 2.1 0.3 . Most notably, the ray and multi-wavelength flux of TXS 0506+056 during the time of this neutrino flare showed no evidence of enhanced activity.

sources.summary: The paper reclassifies TXS 0506+056 as a masquerading BL Lac, intrinsically an FSRQ, and links its neutrino association, multiwavelength flaring, and blazar-sequence outlier status.

quotes.quote: We present evidence that TXS 0506+056, the first plausible non-stellar neutrino source, despite appearances, is not a blazar of the BL Lac type but is instead a masquerading BL Lac, i.e., intrinsically a flat-spectrum radio quasar with hidden broad lines and a standard accretion disk. This re-classification is based on: (1) its radio and O II luminosities; (2) its emission line ratios; (3) its Eddington ratio. We also point out that the synchrotron peak frequency of TXS 0506+056 is more than two orders of magnitude larger than expected by the so-called ``blazar sequence'', a scenario which has been assumed by some theoretical models predicting neutrino (and cosmic-ray) emission from blazars.

Fermi LAT observations have revealed that TXS 0506+056 was in a flaring state at the time of the IceCube alert, with a 0.1-300 GeV flux higher by a factor 6 than the average reported in the 3LAC catalogue Fermi3LAC,icfermi . Follow-up observations have led to the detection of the source with the MAGIC and VERITAS telescopes at energies > 100 GeV, as well as in the X-ray, optical, and radio bands, by Swift /XRT, NuSTAR , ASAS-SN, VLA, and various other facilities icfermi,veritas18,Alb18,Ansoldi18 . Subsequent analysis of archival IceCube data has revealed 13 5 muon neutrinos in excess of background expectations arriving from the same region of the sky on a time-scale of 4-5 months in 2014--2015, which constitutes 3.5 evidence for neutrino emission from the direction of TXS 0506+056 iconly . A dissection of the region around the neutrino position has further shown that TXS 0506+056 is the only counterpart of all the neutrino emission in the region and therefore the most plausible first high-energy neutrino source Padovani_2018 .

sources.summary: The paper models TXS 0506+056 as a blazar with two emission zones, using BLR photons to enhance neutrino production while fitting its multiwavelength flare emission.

quotes.quote: A high-energy muon neutrino event, IceCube-170922A, was recently discovered in both spatial and temporal coincidence with a gamma-ray flare of the blazar TXS 0506+056. It has been shown, with standard one-zone models, that neutrinos can be produced in the blazar jet via hadronic interactions, but with a flux which is mostly limited by the X-ray data. In this work, we explore the neutrino production from TXS 0506+056 by invoking two physically distinct emission zones in the jet, separated by the broad line region (BLR). Using the Doppler-boosted radiation of the BLR as the target photon field, the inner zone accounts for the neutrino and gamma-ray emission via p interactions and inverse Compton scattering respectively, while the outer zone produces the optical and X-ray emission via synchrotron and synchrotron self-Compton processes.

TXS 0506+056 is classified as a BL Lac object based on the low equivalent width (EW) of three very weak emission lines stickel91, stocke91 identified in its optical spectrum paiano18 . However, this should not be interpreted as evidence for the lack of a BLR, since the radiation of the latter could be overwhelmed by the intense non-thermal emission from the jet 1998ApJ...506..621G, 2012MNRAS.420.2899G, 2013MNRAS.431.1914G . Interestingly, a recent paper by 2019MNRAS.484L.104P pointed out that TXS 0506+056 is in fact a masquerading BL Lac object, with a hidden BLR with luminosity 5 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 and a standard accretion disk.

sources.summary: VERITAS followed TXS 0506+056 after the IceCube neutrino alert, detecting lower VHE flux than MAGIC and noting a significant neutrino-gamma-ray correlation.

quotes.quote: TXS 0506+056 is currently one of the most important objects in multi-messenger astronomy. Prior to the 2017-18 season, this Fermi LAT detected blazar was on the VERITAS discovery target list because of its hard MeV-GeV spectrum. It appears in both the 2FHL ( > 50 GeV) and 3FHL ( > 10 GeV) catalogs, and it was relatively bright (F( > 50 GeV) 4 Crab) for a non-VHE-detected object. However, because its redshift ( z = 0.337 ) was not yet measured and its synchrotron peak location ( 10^ 15.3 Hz) was unremarkable, it was a low-priority object, and only 1 h of VERITAS data were taken in 2016.

On Sept. 22, 2017, IceCube detected a 290-TeV neutrino from a direction consistent with the ray blazar, leading to a global follow-up campaign . Prompt observations of TXS 0506+056 with VERITAS and MAGIC initially did not yield detections. However, follow-up observations eventually revealed the blazar to be in an elevated ray emission state.

sources.summary: TXS 0506+056 is treated as a BL Lac blazar associated with IceCube neutrino events; the paper monitors gamma-ray flaring and compares expected neutrino variability.

quotes.quote: A multi-wavelength follow-up campaign associated this neutrino event with a gamma-ray flaring state of the BL Lac TXS 0506+056 located at z =0.3365. From the same position of the sky a muonic neutrino excess is observed in a time window of 110 days around 13^ th of December 2014. These observations together suggest TXS 0506+056 as a possible neutrino emitter.

We report here a short and long term gamma-ray monitoring of this source and we compare it with other blazars selected following the criteria of being spatially correlated with an astrophysical muonic neutrino event observed by IceCube. A detailed study of source duty cycles, as well as the characterization of most significant flares is obtained through the Fermi-LAT data. We show the expected neutrino flux variability with different time binning assuming the gamma-rays observed by Fermi-LAT produced through pion cascades. These expectations are then compared with the IceCube discovery flux level searching for the optimal time bin in a multi-messenger context.

sources.summary: The review describes TXS 0506+056 as a blazar linked to IC-170922A and a 2014-2015 neutrino outburst, making it convincing evidence for a high-energy neutrino and hadronic cosmic-ray source.

quotes.quote: The picture changed in 2017 with the discovery of a 290 TeV muon-track neutrino (IC-170922A) from the direction of the blazar TXS 0506+056, which exhibited a period of enhanced ray emission at the time of the neutrino, observed by Fermi-LAT. The correlation of the high-energy neutrino and the ray flare had a significance of 4.1 , 3 after trial correction, and provides strong indication that the blazar was responsible for the neutrino event . The large coverage in electromagnetic observations initiated after this discovery also includes a rise in the TeV emission observed days later by the MAGIC telescopes and allows for a modelling of the combination of the broad-band electromagnetic spectrum and the neutrino.

The combination of these two observations provide convincing evidence for TXS 0506+056 being a source of high-energy neutrinos, thereby not only identifying a first source contributing to IceCube's diffuse neutrino flux but also nailing down the first source of extragalactic hadronic cosmic rays and hadronic cosmic rays in general without relying on spectral modelling. Multi-wavelength data on this earlier event is sparse and only data from full-sky instruments continuously running are available. Interestingly, in archival Fermi-LAT data no enhanced ray emission was seen to accompany the 2014/2015 neutrino outburst.
With the discovery of TXS 0506+056 as the first source of high-energy neutrinos, blazars have been established to accelerate hadronic cosmic rays.

sources.summary: The paper discusses TXS 0506+056 as a blazar with neutrino flares, using a plasmoid-in-AGN-jet model whose parameters are compatible with its detections but not a full SED fit.

quotes.quote: This assumption is supported by general evidence of up to PeV high-energy-neutrinos of astrophysical origin observed by icecube2013evidence_for_HENu of which a neutrino of 290 TeV could be associated with the flare of the gamma-ray blazar TXS 0506+056 with a significance of 3 by combining data of the IceCube neutrino observatory and Fermi-LAT ( icecube2018multimessenger_PKS_NEHuGamma ). This detection initiated a dedicated search for neutrino clustering in the 10 years of IceCube data around the position of TXS 0506+056, with the result that there was an enhanced flux of neutrinos in a half-year period from September 2014 to March 2015, with a statistical significant of 3 of being incompatible with the background hypothesis. It is interesting to note that this potential flare is not accompanied with a gamma-ray flare.

The second concerns the evidence for a neutrino flare of the source TXS 0506+056 in 2014/2015 that lasted around 100 days and has a significance of 3 of being an astrophysical neutrino flare from the direction of TXS 0506+056 icecube_txs2018 . This detection - as opposed to the flare in 2018 icecube2018multimessenger_PKS_NEHuGamma lacks a flare at gamma-ray energies and production close to the jet base would be a solution even here. However, that flare lasted about 6 months, so the injection scenario needs to be different from what is discussed here.

Conclusively, our model parameters are compatible to the TXS 0506+056 detections yet the full modelling of the time evolution of the TXS SED goes beyond the scope of this paper and will be subject to future work.

sources.summary: The paper models TXS 0506+056 as a blazar and possible supermassive black hole binary whose precessing jet could explain recurring neutrino flares and future gravitational-wave signatures.

quotes.quote: The recent detection of possible neutrino emission from the blazar TXS 0506+056 was the first high-energy neutrino associated with an astrophysical source, making this special type of active galaxies promising neutrino emitters. The fact that two distinct episodes of neutrino emission were detected with a separation of around 3 years suggests that emission could be periodic. Periodic emission is expected from supermassive binary black hole systems due to jet precession close to the binary's merger. Here we show that if TXS 0506+056 is a binary source then the next neutrino flare could occur before the end of 2021.

The blazar TXS 0506+056 produced a detectable neutrino signature both in 2014/2015 icecube_txs2018 and then in September 2017 icecube_txs_fermi2018 . We now examine the possibility that TXS 0506+056 is a supermassive black hole binary system in which the two observed neutrino emission episodes are due to the precession of the relativistic outflow from the black holes. We can use observations in the context of this scenario to make predictions on upcoming flares, and even the time of the binary merger.

sources.summary: The paper models TXS 0506+056 as a blazar with an orphan 2014-2015 neutrino flare, explaining its neutrino and multiwavelength emission through an inner-outer blob scenario.

quotes.quote: For the first time, a high-energy ( 290 TeV ) muon neutrino event, IceCube-170922A, was detected in both spatial and temporal coincidence with the ray flare of the known blazar TXS 0506+056 2018Sci...361.1378I at the significance level of 3 , triggering extensive studies on the neutrino--blazar association 2018ApJ...863L..10A,2018ApJ...864...84K, 2018MNRAS.480..192P, 2018ApJ...866..109S, 2018arXiv180711069Z, 2019PhRvD..99j3006B, 2019MNRAS.483L..12C, 2019NatAs...3...88G, 2019PhRvD.100j3002L, 2019PhRvD..99f3008L, 2019MNRAS.484L.104P, 2019MNRAS.483L.127R, 2019ApJ...886...23X, 2020PASJ...72...20C . More interestingly, 2018Sci...361..147I investigated the archive data of IceCube in the direction of the same blazar, and discovered a 3.5 excess of 13 5 high-energy neutrino events, also known as 2014-2015 (hereafter 14-15) neutrino flare, in the period between 2014 September and 2015 March. It is worth noting that no evidence of multi-wavelength activity is found during that time (i.e., ``orphan'' neutrino flare), and the neutrino flux is about 5 times higher than the average ray flux, probably implying a strong absorption of GeV photons by intense X-ray radiation field 2018arXiv180900601W, 2019ApJ...881...46R . Such a phenomenon poses a great challenge to the conventional one-zone models.

To summarize, we studied the multi-messenger emissions of blazar TXS 0506+056 in the period of the 2014-2015 neutrino flare and blazar 4FGL J0955.1+3551 during the conjectural emission period of the neutrino event IceCube-200107A with the inner--outer blob model. Considering that certain dissipative process occurs at the jet base, an inner blob may form within the hot corona around the SMBH and produce a neutrino flare via the p interactions with the X-ray radiation of the corona, while the non-flare-state multi-wavelength flux of the blazar measured in the same period is ascribed to an outer blob which is far from the SMBH. We found that, on the premise that the multi-wavelength SED being generally reproduced, the 14-15 neutrino flare of TXS 0506+056 could be interpreted in a wide range of parameter space, showing the feasibility that the multi-messenger emission of TXS 0506+056 in the period of the 14-15 neutrino flare can be interpreted in the same physical picture of that for IceCube-170922A.

sources.summary: The paper treats TXS 0506+056 as the strongest IceCube blazar association, tied to IceCube-170922A, archival neutrino excess, and complex activity states.

quotes.quote: This is the blazar TXS 0506+056, also known as 5BZB J0509+0541, located in the relatively small uncertainty region of the neutrino IceCube-170922A, detected when the source was undergoing a flare. This result was further strengthened by the discovery of an excess of several lower energy neutrinos from the same direction in a subsequent analysis of IceCube archival data neutrino,Dissecting . This so-called ``neutrino flare'' occurred when TXS 0506+056 was in a period of low activity, suggesting that the relationship between astrophysical neutrinos and emission is not straightforward.

The most robust statistical evidence so far for an association between astrophysical neutrinos and blazars remains the case of TXS 0506+056 Aartsen2018,neutrino,Dissecting . This IBL/HBL type blazar is located well within the 0.5 ^ (90 containement) error region of the 0.3 PeV event IceCube-170922A that was detected in correspondence with enhanced activity from the source. This already significant result was further strengthened by the detection of a 3.5 excess from the same direction found in a subsequent search of IceCube archival data neutrino,Dissecting . The corresponding 13 5 neutrinos were detected between September 2014 and March 2015, when TXS 0506+056 was not undergoing strong activity, implying that the relationship between astrophysical neutrinos and emission is likely complex.

sources.summary: The paper studies TXS 0506+056 with VLBI, linking its parsec-scale jet components, gamma-ray activity, and core brightening to the IceCube-170922A neutrino association.

quotes.quote: The most prominent candidate so far is the blazar TXS 0506+056, which is found to be associated with the neutrino event IceCube-170922A. Furthermore, the IceCube reported an excess of neutrinos towards TXS 0506+056 between September 2014 and March 2015, even though this association needs additional investigation, considering the presence of a nearby ray source, the quasar PKS 0502+049. Motivated by this, we studied the parsec-scale structures of TXS 0506+056 and PKS 0502+049 through radio interferometry at 8 and 15 GHz. We identified twelve jet components in TXS 0506+056 and seven components in PKS 0502+049. The most reliable jet components show superluminal speeds ranging from 9.5c to 66c in the case of TXS 0506+056, and from 14.3c to 59c for PKS 0502+049, which were used to estimate a lower (upper) limit for the Lorentz factor (jet viewing angle) for both sources.

Moreover, the formation epochs of the jet components C9 and C10 in TXS 0506+056 are compatible with the occurrence of the IceCube-170922A event, suggesting a possible connection between the radio core activity and the production of the detected 290-TeV neutrino. A systematic and strong increase of the flux density of the parsec-scale core of TXS 0506+056 seen after 2017 (see fig:TXS_PKS_flux_figure ) also argues in favour of this possibility.

sources.summary: The paper describes TXS 0506+056 as an IBL-like BL Lac linked to IceCube-170922A, with hard, relatively stable NuSTAR spectra across many observations.

quotes.quote: TXS 0506+056 TXS 0506+056 is a relatively bright BL Lac object with an IBL-like SED. This blazar became famous and widely studied after its association in September 2017 with the high-energy neutrino IceCube-170922A IceCube,neutrino,Dissecting,Ansoldi2018 . Before the occurrence of this event no redshift estimation was available for this object. Shortly afterward obtained a high quality optical spectrum at the 10.4 m Gran Telescopio Canarias, which revealed very weak emission lines at the redshift of z=0.3365 .

showed that this source is in fact a masquerading BL Lac, that is a blazar that is intrinsically a FSRQ where the disk and broad-line emission is present but completely diluted by the strong non-thermal radiation from the jet. As part of the intense follow-up campaign aimed at studying in detail the likely electromagnetic counterpart to IceCube-170922A \ observed this blazar 18 times between 2017 and 2021. Our analysis does not show any correlation between different parameters, e.g. versus flux.

sources.summary: The paper uses neutrino and gamma-ray observations of blazar TXS 0506+056 to constrain dark-matter scattering in its central spike and host halo.

quotes.quote: On 22 September 2017, the IceCube neutrino observatory detected a neutrino event with an energy of 290 TeV, consistent with the direction of the gamma-ray blazar TXS 0506+056, located at a distance of 1421 Mpc  . The neutrino alert from IceCube triggered an observation campaign ranging from radio to gamma-ray telescopes  . In particular, Fermi-LAT observed an excess of gamma rays from the direction of TXS 0506+056 following the IceCube alert, with more than 5 significance and reaching energies up to 300 GeV. On the other hand, the initial observations of TXS 0506+056 by ground based gamma-ray telescopes after the IceCube alert only lead to an upper bound on the gamma-ray flux.

High-energy neutrinos and photons have been detected from the blazar TXS 0506+056, with fluxes which are well compatible with astrophysical models. This indicates that the fluxes are not significantly attenuated by putative interactions of neutrinos or photons with the dark matter particles existing between their production point and the Earth. In this work we have investigated the possible attenuation of the fluxes due to the dark matter spike around the black hole of TXS 0506+056.

sources.summary: The paper treats TXS 0506+056 as a blazar neutrino-source comparison, noting prior evidence, a time-integrated excess contribution, and a much greater distance than NGC 1068.

quotes.quote: To date the most compelling evidence of a source of high-energy cosmic neutrinos was reported following the space and time coincidence of a high-energy IceCube neutrino with the gamma-ray flaring blazar TXS 0506+056 . TXS 0506+056 has recently been shown to host a standard accretion disk and a dusty torus, which has the peculiarity to be also an emitter of high-energy radiation and, possibly, cosmic rays . Additionally, a recent IceCube study suggests a correlation of IceCube neutrinos with a catalog composed of 110 known gamma-ray emitters at a significance of 3.3 . The main contributors are the active galaxy NGC 1068 and the blazars TXS 0506+056, PKS 1424+240 and GB6 J1542+6129.

Besides NGC 1068, the other two objects contributing to the excess are the blazars PKS 1424+240 and TXS 0506+056, for which we find potential neutrino emission with local significance of 3.7 and 3.5 , respectively. We emphasize that the significance of TXS 0506+056 reported here relates to a time-integrated signal, whereas previous analyses have provided evidence for transient emission .

Nevertheless, despite the intrinsic uncertainty in estimating the density, the nearness of NGC 1068 compared to TXS 0506+056, which is approximately 100 times farther away, indicates that there are at least two populations of neutrino sources that differ in both density and luminosity by orders of magnitude.

sources.summary: The paper discusses TXS 0506+056 as a flaring blazar associated with IceCube alerts, possible masquerading FSRQ behavior, optical and radio variability, and jet structures relevant to neutrino production.

quotes.quote: Fermi LAT, as well as MAGIC follow-up observations , confirmed the presence inside the error region of about 0.5^ from the IceCube event direction of a flaring blazar, TXS 0506+056, located at a redshift of 0.33 with a chance probability of 3 . The picture appeared immediately complex for TXS 0506+056, previously classified as high-frequency peaked BL Lacertae (HBL BL Lac) HBL and low-frequency peaked LBL are the blazars with the frequency of the synchrotron bump of the SED >10^ 15 Hz or <10^ 14 Hz, respectively. , possibly being a `masquerading' FSRQ . FSRQs are relatively luminous LBL blazars with strong, optical-UV emission lines in addition to the non-thermal continuum, a signature of the Broad Line Region (BLR) outside the jet, likely photo-excited by a radiatively efficient accretion disk around a supermassive black hole (SMBH).

While both TXS 0506+056 flares do not pass the 5 significance discovery threshold, some interesting observations were made in coincidence with the 2017 and 2014-2015 observations by IceCube. E.g. the MASTER global optical telescope network reported that TXS 0506+056 was in a quiet state 73 s after the IceCube 2017 event, but 2 hr after it the optical flux increased at 50 level on top of the background, namely the biggest variation they recorded since 2005 . Additionally, during the 100 d neutrino-flare period across 2014-2015, another longer flare with about 3.5 significance was observed. VLBA 15 GHz observations in 2009-18 on the structure of the jet of TXS 0506+056, showed a curved jet structure, possibly a precessing jet with 10 yr period, with the 2017 alert in the bright precession phase or a cosmic collider of 2 jets .

sources.summary: The paper discusses TXS 0506+056 as a blazar associated with high-energy neutrino events, including IceCube and Baikal-GVD detections and electromagnetic activity.

quotes.quote: The first association of an individual astrophysical object with a high-energy neutrino was found for the blazar TXS 0506+056 IC170922A . On September 22, 2017, the IceCube neutrino obsesrvatory detected a probable muon neutrino with an energy of 290 TeV from the direction of this blazar -- the neutrino alert IC170922A. It was followed by a large flash from TXS 0506+056 observed simultaneously in the entire electromagnetic spectrum -- from radio to gamma rays multi_TXS2018 . Moreover, this event was supplemented by about a 3.5 excess of lower-energy neutrinos from the direction of TXS 0506+056 found by IceCube in their earlier data sample from 2014-2015.

The cascade with the highest energy, detected by Baikal-GVD below the horizon so far, arrived from the direction of the well-known blazar TXS 0506+056 GVD-TXS2022 . The blazar TXS 0506+056 is characterized by a bright compact parsec-scale emission, but appears to be otherwise completely ordinary Kovalev-2020 . We estimated the probability of the cascade event with an energy of above 200 TeV, induced by a neutrino inside the GVD volume, of having astrophysical origin and the likelihood of its association with TXS 0506+056.

sources.summary: The paper treats TXS 0506+056 as a blazar candidate source of repeated neutrino flares, applying a jet-precession model in which an inspiraling supermassive binary black hole at its core could explain the flare timing.

quotes.quote: Ever since the detection of a 300 TeV neutrino from the direction of the blazar TXS 0506+056 at a 3 level by IceCube, and a coincident detection of a GeV gamma-ray flare by Fermi Large Area Telescope , this source became one of the main candidates for the source of cosmic rays (CRs) and neutrinos. A blind analysis of 10 yr data of IceCube from the same direction revealed another neutrino flare in 2014/2015 with 10 neutrinos at 10 TeV at a 3.5 level . However, gamma-ray data at that time indicated that the source was in a quiescence mode. In September 2022, IceCube reported the detection of another 170 TeV neutrino from the direction of the blazar , but with no associated gamma-ray detection.

In the following, the jet precession model is extended further to mass ratios up to and including unity by allowing contributions of the second spin and the orbital angular momentum and applied on TXS 0506+056. Though the neutrino event detected by the Baikal Collaboration has a high chance of being of astrophysical origin, it remains unclear if it originated from this source, as it has a huge uncertainty range (being a cascade event). This is why it is investigated with this updated model, whether all four distinct neutrino flares could originate from TXS 0506+056, if it has an inspiraling SMBBH at its core.

sources.summary: The review links TXS 0506+056 to multiple neutrino associations, including IceCube event IC170922, a Baikal-GVD cascade event, and earlier flare analyses.

quotes.quote: TXS 0506+056. The association of this blazar with the event IC170922 ( 290 TeV) started the history of observational associations between blazars and neutrinos. Both this neutrino event and this blazar were included in the sample of Ref. . At the end of 2022, the Baikal-GVD experiment reported a coincidence of the cascade event GVD210418CA with the same blazar.

TXS 0506 + 056. Although active galactic nuclei have been proposed as potential sources of high-energy neutrinos long before the discovery of the latters, the most serious attention to the possible connection between neutrinos and blazars was caused by the publication of two IceCube papers dedicated to the same source, a fairly ordinary blazar TXS 0506 + 056. Ref. announced the observation of a gamma-ray flare of this blazar a few days after detecting a high-energy neutrino from the direction of this source in September 2017.

sources.summary: The paper discusses TXS 0506+056 as a BL Lac associated with IceCube neutrinos, using its redshift, luminosity, and baryonic loading to test blazar-sequence neutrino models.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, after the neutrino detection different ray telescopes have detected a flaring state of the source TXS 0506+056 IceCube:2018dnn , which lies in a direction consistent with that of the IceCube event. black This correlation in space and time increased the probability of this event being of astrophysical origin, excluding the atmospheric hypothesis at the 3.5 level. black The aforementioned astrophysical source is considered to be a BL Lac, located at redshift z_ .8 TXS =0.3365 0.0010 , with a luminosity of L_ 1.3 10^ 47 erg/s during the weeks before and after the neutrino event IceCube:2018dnn .

The luminosity averaged over all historical data is L_ 2.8 10^ 46 erg/s IceCube:2018dnn , as marked in the right panel of distro . From here on we will use this time-averaged luminosity to study the characteristics of TXS 0506+056 within the blazar population.

Note that the TXS 0506+056 lies on the verge between low-luminosity sources, which dominate the diffuse neutrino flux, and the flux-limited high-luminosity sources; it is therefore a rather special case in the context of our model.

sources.summary: The paper adds TXS0506+056 as a benchmark BL Lac neutrino candidate, noting its high state during IceCube detection, prior neutrino excess, and multiwavelength/TeV relevance.

quotes.quote: During our analysis a new source, namely TXS0506+056, has been added because of its position inside the angular uncertainty of a muon track event detected by IceCube. TXS0506+056, was in a high-state during the neutrino event and we will consider it as benchmark to check the properties of the other sources of the sample during the related neutrino detection.

In fact TXS0506+056 fully complies with our selection criteria (a source belonging to the 2FHL catalogue in the error circle of an IceCube event). Since this is the most plausible association observed so far, in our study we can use TXS 0506+056 as a benchmark case to discuss the other potential candidates (IceCube Collaboration at al. 2018). We note that the archival search of the past IceCube data revealed 13 5 low energy muon neutrinos coming from the same region of TXS 0506+056 in the sky on a time scale of five months (IceCube-Collaboration 2018). These events were not present on the past lists released by the Collaboration.

sources.summary: VERITAS observed TXS 0506+056 after the IceCube alert, detected VHE gamma-ray emission in the full data set, and characterized the blazar's spectrum and neutrino association.

quotes.quote: While no significant very-high-energy (VHE; E > 100 GeV) emission was observed from the blazar by VERITAS in the two-week period immediately following the IceCube alert, TXS 0506+056 was detected by VERITAS with a significance of 5.8 standard deviations ( ) in the full 35-hour data set. The average photon flux of the source during this period was (8.9 1.6) 10^ 12 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , or 1.6 of the Crab Nebula flux, above an energy threshold of 110 GeV, with a soft spectral index of 4.8 1.3 .

The initial reconstructed direction of the neutrino 2017GCN.21916....1K was consistent with that of the gamma-ray blazar TXS 0506+056, which was observed to be in a high gamma-ray emission state by Fermi LAT 2017ATel10791....1T . Follow-up observations of the neutrino/blazar position led to the first detection of this blazar in the VHE range by the MAGIC telescopes 2017ATel10817....1M .

The potential association with an astrophysical neutrino event made TXS 0506+056 a source of particular interest, and further studies of its high-energy emission are required to explore the possible physical connection between the neutrino and gamma-ray emissions. For this purpose, VERITAS performed observations of the blazar between 2017 September and 2018 February which are presented here.

sources.summary: The paper treats TXS 0506+056 as an IceCube-associated quasar/blazar with broad multiwavelength activity, then analyzes its GeV light curve, finding a strong flare about six times its 8-year average.

quotes.quote: On 2017 September 22 the IceCube neutrino observatory detected a track-like, very-high-energy event (IceCube-170922A) that is spatially associated with TXS 0506+056, a quasar at a redshift of z=0.3365 . This source is characterized by the increased acitivies in a very wide energy range (from radio to TeV) during these days.

Temporal Behaviors TXS 0506+056 The weekly ray light curve of TXS 0506+056 is presented in the left panel of Fig. fig:0506wlc . Before MJD 57855, the source maintains at a relatively quiescent state, with only a few bins whose fluxes reach roughly three times of the 8-year averaged flux, 9.8 10^ 8 ph cm^ 2 s^ 1 . However, since then, a strong ray flare has appeared, with a peak flux of (5.8 0.6) 10^ 7 ph cm^ 2 s^ 1 which is nearly 6 times of the 8-year averaged flux. The corresponding peak apparent ray luminosity of the bin is 1.5 10^ 47 erg s^ 1 , adopting a redshift of 0.3365 2018ApJ...854L..32P .

sources.summary: The paper models the 2014-15 IceCube neutrino flare from TXS 0506+056, favoring an inefficient stationary soft-X-ray target-field scenario and separating the LAT gamma rays from the neutrino mechanism.

quotes.quote: Recently, neutrino astrophysics experienced a further boost when the BL Lac object TXS 0506+056 was found within the 50 containment region of the single IceCube event IceCube-EHE-170922A IceCube1 , and simultaneously in an elevated flux state in the Fermi LAT energy range IceCube1 at the time this event was detected. Furthermore, an extended neutrino excess (designated the ``neutrino flare'' in the following) consisting of 13 neutrino events detected in a 110 ^ 35 _ 24 days period in September 2014 -- March 2015 was found from the same source IceCube2 . This has motivated the suggestion that TXS 0506+056 is the source of these detected neutrinos IceCube1 , implicitly assuming a causal connection between the neutrino and ray emission.

We thus conclude that a scenario in which an external soft X-ray photon field provides the targets for photo-pion reactions producing the IceCube neutrinos of TXS 0506+056 in an environment where dominantly Compton scattering supports the pair cascading, appears feasible. Eq. taupglimitb and Eq. taugglimitb imply in this case still very inefficient neutrino production, and a corresponding pair cascade flux that lies significantly below the gamma-ray flux level observed by the LAT during the neutrino flare (see Figs. fig:4 - fig:8 ). Further high energy radiation processes are therefore needed to explain the observed gamma-ray SED, which is unlikely proton-initiated, during the neutrino flare.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3FGL J0509.4+0541 as a bright gamma-ray blazar coincident with a neutrino position, also listed in 3FHL/2FHL and measured at redshift about 0.3365.

quotes.quote: The gamma-ray source at Dec= +5 69 , RA= 77 36 lies well within the 50 neutrino position uncertainty region, at a distance of 0 1 from the best fit neutrino position (see the gamma-ray counts map in Fig. fig:counts ). The source is listed in the 3FGL as well as 3FHL as 3FGL J0509.4+0541 and 3FHL J0509.4+0541, respectively 2015ApJS..218...23A,2017ApJS..232...18A . The 3FGL catalog is based on gamma-ray data in the energy range from 100 MeV to 300 GeV, whereas the 3FHL catalog is focused on the energy range from 10 GeV to 2 TeV.

We note that is also in the 2FHL 2FHL catalog based on gamma-ray data from 50 GeV to 2 TeV, identifying it already as a potential target for very-high-energy gamma-ray emission. is among the brightest 4.4 (5.9 ) sources in the 3FGL (3FHL) in terms of gamma-ray energy flux within the energy bounds of the corresponding catalog see also 2018arXiv180704461P .
The redshift of was measured to be z = 0.336 by and later confirmed by at z = 0.3365 0.0010 .

sources.summary: The paper uses TXS 0506+056 as a flaring blazar associated with IceCube 170922A, modeling its multiwavelength SED, neutrino emission, and polarization to test leptonic/hadronic scenarios.

quotes.quote: The recent IceCube 170922A event was reported to be coincident with the blazar TXS 0506+056 flaring state IceCube18a,IceCube18b . Following the neutrino alert, the blazar was detected in multi-wavelength campaigns including very-high-energy rays IceCube18a, MAGIC18, 2018ApJ...861L..20A . All wavelengths exhibit strong variability, with a 7 optical polarization degree during flares reported by the Kanata Telescope IceCube18a . Theoretically, many models have been proposed to explain the SED of the TXS 0506+056 flare and the IceCube-170922A event e.g., Cerruti19,Gao19,Keivani18,MAGIC18,Reimer18 .

To facilitate realistic predictions of the high-energy polarization, we take the recent TXS 0506+056 observation IceCube18a to derive the general physical parameters and particle distributions via one-zone spectral modeling. While we do not aim at detailed fitting, the derived parameters can help to illustrate their effects on the high-energy SED and spectral polarization.

sources.summary: The white paper highlights TXS 0506+056 as a candidate neutrino blazar whose SED, neutrino flare, and MeV polarization can test leptohadronic models.

quotes.quote: Abstract: The possible association of the blazar TXS 0506+056 with a high-energy neutrino detected by IceCube holds the tantalizing potential to answer three astrophysical questions: 1. Where do high-energy neutrinos originate? 2. Where are cosmic rays produced and accelerated? 3. What radiation mechanisms produce the high-energy rays in blazars?

There is general agreement that the broadband SED of the candidate neutrino blazars TXS 0506+056 and PKS 1424 418 can be explained with a leptohadronic model in which the leptonic component dominates the GeV band and the hadronic component leads to cascading emission that gets eventually released in the MeV and hard X-ray regime Gao17,Keivani18,Murase18,Gao19,Reimer18 . The down-cascading of gamma-ray photons below the GeV range may explain the mysterious neutrino excess (designated the `neutrino flare') of 13 neutrino events detected by IceCube between September 2014 and March 2015, which has been independently 0.65 font=footnotesize associated with 3 significance with TXS 0506+056 during an extended GeV-faint state Icecube18b .

sources.summary: The paper highlights TXS 0506+056 as a redshift 0.336 neutrino blazar whose IceCube alert triggered multiwavelength follow-up and VHE gamma-ray detection by MAGIC and VERITAS.

quotes.quote: The Neutrino blazar TXS 0506+056 is a gamma-ray emitting blazar located at redshift 0.336 paiano2018 . This object was only one of the many gamma-ray emitting blazars detected by Fermi LAT at gamma rays, at least until the 22nd September 2017, when IceCube reported the detection of a neutrino from a region compatible with that of TXS 0506+056 (GCN/AMON Notice dated 22 September 2017 20:55:13 UTC). The alert was immediately followed by multi-wavelength and multi-messenger observations summarized in icecube2018 .

The analysis of Fermi LAT data collected after the alert and in the precedent years revealed that TXS 0506+650 was in an active gamma-ray state since several months at the moment of the alert. Moreover, the emission reaches the VHE gamma-ray band, as announced by MAGIC and confirmed by the VERITAS collaboration. This exceptional event officially marked the beginning of the era of extragalactic multi-messenger astronomy with neutrinos.

sources.summary: The paper discusses TXS 0506+056 as a flaring active galaxy linked to an IceCube neutrino alert, MAGIC VHE detection, earlier neutrino excess, and complex jet system.

quotes.quote: TXS 0506+056 On the 22^ nd of September 2017, a muon-neutrino induced signal in IceCube with a reconstructed energy of 290 TeV was sent out as an alert, prompting a global response at all wavelengths from radio to VHE gamma-ray . This neutrino was quickly associated to the flaring active galaxy TXS 0506+056, with a detection at VHE from the MAGIC telescope. Although the association of the neutrino event with this galaxy is not overwhelmingly statistically significant, it is strengthened by the observation of an earlier period (between 2014 and 2015) when 13 5 neutrino events in excess of the background expectation were seen from the direction of the source .

Several authors have pointed out the difficulty of resolving the measured gamma-ray and X-ray emission with the implied neutrino flux from TXS 0506+056, in particular during the 2014/15 event (see e.g. ). In scenarios where the gamma-rays and neutrino emission are generated in the same zone by the same process, cascading results in gamma-ray emission that overshoots the observations. That the situation in TXS 0506+056 is more complex than typical assumptions is hinted at by recent radio VLBI observations, which suggest that this system contains two supermassive black holes producing jets that occasionally interact .

sources.summary: The paper treats TXS 0506+056 as a blazar with evidence for high-energy neutrino emission, noting its limited contribution to the total astrophysical flux and prior ANTARES study.

quotes.quote: Neutrino astronomy has recently entered an exciting period with the discovery of an isotropic high-energy cosmic neutrino flux reported by the IceCube Collaboration , followed by the first evidence of neutrino emission from an astrophysical source, the blazar TXS 0506+056 . These observations represent a major breakthrough in the field, thus further investigations are strongly motivated. Indeed, the origin of most of the observed neutrino flux remains unknown.

The neutrino flux of TXS 0506+056 can only account for less than 1 of the total observed astrophysical flux . Moreover, recent searches for neutrino emission from the directions of blazars from the 2nd Fermi-LAT AGN catalogue performed by the IceCube Collaboration indicated that blazars contribute less than about 40 80 (30 ) to the total observed neutrino flux assuming an unbroken power-law spectrum (E_ ) E_ ^ with = 2.0 ( =2.5 ). The region around TXS 0506+056 was studied also by the ANTARES Collaboration using data collected from 2007 to 2017 .

sources.summary: The paper presents TXS 0506+056 as a multi-messenger blazar associated with IceCube event IC170922A, with VERITAS detections and later deep monitoring at lower flux.

quotes.quote: TXS 0506+056 is an important object for multi-messenger astronomy (see and references therein). It was observed to have an elevated VHE and MeV-GeV gamma-ray flux in spatial and temporal coincidence ( 3 ) with the IceCube high energy neutrino event IC170922A. This could indicate that blazar jets accelerate cosmic rays to at least several PeV, and are hence a source of VHE cosmic rays.

The initial VERITAS follow-up observations of the neutrino/blazar ( 35 h from Sept. 2017 to Feb. 2018) led to a soft-spectrum ( 4.8 ), VHE detection (5.8 ), albeit at lower VHE flux than detected by MAGIC. VERITAS has since carried out deep observations of TXS 0506+056 and an associated MWL campaign . A weak excess (3.4 ) is observed in 61 h collected from Oct. 2018 to Feb. 2021.

sources.summary: TXS 0506+056 is discussed as a blazar associated with IC 170922A; the model predicts only about 0.0012 muon plus antimuon events in seven days after that neutrino detection.

quotes.quote: An illustrative example is the 2017 multi-wavelength flare of TXS 0506+056 that has been associated with the high-energy neutrino IC 170922A icecube2018a . The lack of X-ray observations prior to that epoch makes difficult a detailed study of the long-term behaviour of this source in neutrinos 2020ApJ...891..115P .

Only 3 sources from our sample are positionally consistent with astrophysical muon neutrino track events detected by IceCube, namely TXS 0506+056, 1ES 0229+200 and PKS 1502+106. /XRT data are available close to the arrival time of the high-energy neutrino only in the case of TXS 0506+056 and IC 170922A  IceCube:2018cha . Our prediction in terms of muon plus antimuon neutrinos after the neutrino detection is 0.0012 events in 7  d. The estimated average rate of muon and antimuon neutrinos from the SED modelling of the 2017 flare is N _ _ + _ 0.1  yr ^ 1   Keivani2018,2020ApJ...891..115P . Adopting this rate, we find 0.0019 events for the same time interval. The two predictions are similar even though the underlying models of electromagnetic emission are different, because the maximum neutrino flux in the model of is also limited by the X-ray flux.

sources.summary: TXS 0506+056 is the recovered flaring blazar counterpart: the paper notes its 2017 neutrino coincidence, a separate 2014-15 neutrino flare, model consistency, and ASAS-SN optical flaring.

quotes.quote: In 2017, the gamma-ray blazar TXS 0506+056 was observed in spatial coincidence with a high-energy neutrino during a period of electromagnetic flaring IceCube:2018dnn . A search for neutrino clustering from the same source revealed an additional neutrino flare in 2014-15 ice2018_txsflare , during a period without any significant electromagnetic flaring activity Fermi-LAT:2019hte . Theoretical models have confirmed that conditions in the source are consistent with the detection of one neutrino after accounting for Eddington bias Strotjohann:2018ufz . However, explaining the ``orphan'' neutrino flare in 2014/15 proved to be difficult Reimer:2018vvw,Rodrigues:2018tku .

We also examined the ASAS-SN light curves of every Fermi 4FGL Blazar fermi2020_4fgl, fermi2020_dr2 within the footprint of a neutrino alert. We do not find any flaring activity coincident with the arrival of the corresponding neutrino, except for the previously-reported ASAS-SN observations of TXS 0506+056 IceCube:2018dnn . This light curve is shown in the top panel of Figure fig:lightcurves_blazars , with the source exhibiting an optical flare at the time of the neutrino detection.

sources.summary: The paper treats TXS 0506+056 as a likely TeV-PeV neutrino emitter: it cites IceCube event and flare correlations, notes one-zone modeling tension, and identifies it as a BL Lac source.

quotes.quote: The two most likely TeV -- PeV neutrino emmiters are TXS 0506+056 and NGC 1068 . The TXS 0506+056 is found to have a 3 (global significance) correlation with a 300 TeV IceCube neutrino event . Furthermore, a neutrino flare was detected in the direction of TXS 0506+056 between 2014 and 2015, with a global confidence of 3.5 . These two measurements are statistically independent, and a simple combination of the two results yields a significance of 4.8 . However, multi-wavelength observations seem to not favor the possibility that the neutrinos are from TXS 0506+056 in a one-zone model, although more complicated two-zone models can solve this problem .
Note that the 3FHL source catalog does not contain the BL Lac source TXS 0506+056 (the exclusion of TXS 0506+056 in the analysis of the 4FGL sources has a negligible effect on the results).

sources.summary: The paper models TXS 0506+056 during a 2018 VHE flare, linking its SED to leptonic, hadronic-cascade, and cosmogenic components while finding little gamma-ray/neutrino-flare correlation.

quotes.quote: The MAGIC collaboration has recently analyzed data from a long-term multiwavelength campaign of the ray blazar TXS 0506+056. In December 2018, it was flaring in the very-high-energy (VHE; E>100 GeV) ray band, but no simultaneous neutrino event was detected. We model the observed spectral energy distribution (SED), using a one-zone leptohadronic emission. We estimate the neutrino flux through the restriction from observed X-ray flux on the secondary radiation due to hadronic cascade, initiated by protons with energy E_p 0.1 EeV.

We highlight our modeling as an important step in the course of study of TXS 0506+056, viz., (i) a neutrino flare (archival data) was observed in 2014-2015, but no gamma-ray activity, (ii) then an increased gamma-ray activity and simultaneous detection of a neutrino event in 2017 (iii) finally, no neutrino detection but increased gamma-ray activity in 2018. From the study of phase (iii), we show that indeed there is little correlation between gamma-ray and neutrino flares.

sources.summary: The paper treats TXS 0506+056 as an IceCube neutrino blazar whose central black hole has a dense dark-matter spike, using its neutrino emission and path to constrain neutrino-dark matter scattering.

quotes.quote: Neutrino emission in coincidence with gamma rays has been observed from the blazar TXS 0506+056 by the IceCube telescope. Neutrinos from the blazar had to pass through a dense spike of dark matter (DM) surrounding the central black hole. The observation of such a neutrino implies new upper bounds on the neutrino-DM scattering cross section as a function of the DM mass.

A more model independent strategy is to use the fact that a 290 TeV neutrino, known as event IC-170922A, has been observed by the IceCube experiment and was identified as coming from the blazar TXS 0506+056  . Ref. \ set limits on the DM- scattering cross section using the fact that the neutrino had to pass through cosmological and galactic DM between the blazar and the Earth. In this work, we derive stronger limits, using the fact that the neutrino also had to traverse the dense DM spike surrounding the supermassive black hole powering TXS 0506+056.

sources.summary: The review presents TXS 0506-056 as a blazar neutrino source with redshift 0.3365, gamma-ray flaring, and an IceCube 290 TeV neutrino used to constrain Lorentz-invariance violation.

quotes.quote: Indeed the only two extragalactic objects that have been identified as neutrino sources so far, the supernova SN1987A and the blazar TXS 0506-056, have been used to such effect. The supernova SN1987A was the first celestial object to be detected in neutrinos, along with optical, X-ray and radio observations . The other object is the blazar TXS 0506-056, which has been identified as a neutrino source as well as an optical, gamma ray and radio source . These two objects provide an actual example on how the ideas mentioned in the previous paragraph can be adapted to set strong limits on the scale of LIV effects.

The second object that has been used to extract limits on LIV is the blazar TXS 0506-056. This blazar has a redshift z = 0.3365 0.0010 and it is known to present flaring episodes, as Figure fig:txs-flare illustrates for the case of gamma rays. On September 22nd 2017 IceCube detected a neutrino with an estimated energy of 290 TeV from the direction of this object (vertical red dashed line in Figure fig:txs-flare ) in coincidence with a gamma-ray flaring period of several weeks.

sources.summary: The paper treats TXS 0506+056 as a transient UHECR-source candidate, uses literature broad-line-region parameters to shift source-environment constraints, and concludes it is strongly disfavored as a UHECR accelerator.

quotes.quote: In addition to the classic candidates for UHECR sources, the dashed boxes show two transient possibilities, TXS 0506+056 TXSObservation and TDE AT2019dsg Stein+20 based respectively on the multimessenger studies in Cerruti+18,Keivani+18,Liu+18,Murase:2018iyl,Gao+18,MAGIC18,Xue+19,Zhang+19 and Stein+20,Liu+20,Murase:2020lnu,Winter+20,Cendes+21 . Their large ranges reflect both the uncertainties in the interpretation of the observations and the potentially large inherent range of conditions.

For example, based on results of Liu+18 for the broad-line region of TXS 0506+056, n_0 10^ 4.7 . For this value, the posterior distribution would be obtained from the one for n_0 = 1 by sliding it downward and to the right parallel to the ``Hillas rails" by 10^ 4.7 and 10^ +4.7 , respectively. If this n_0 estimate and the box in the B-L plane attributed to TXS 0506+056 are valid, TXS 0506+056 would be strongly disfavored as a source of UHECRs.
Our results are in good agreement with Keivani+18 who conclude that multimessenger data make it unlikely for TXS-0506+056 to be a UHECR accelerator.

sources.summary: The proceedings describe TXS 0506+056 as a redshift-0.33 flaring blazar linked to IceCube alerts, optical and radio variability, and a possibly precessing jet.

quotes.quote: Fermi LAT, as well as MAGIC follow-up observations  , confirmed the presence inside the error region of about 0.5^ from the IceCube event direction of a flaring blazar, TXS 0506+056, located at redshift of 0.33 with a chance probability of 3 . The picture appeared immediately complex for TXS 0506+056, previously classified as high-frequency peaked BL Lacertae (HBL BL Lac) HBL and low-frequency peaked LBL are the blazars with the frequency of the synchrotron bump of the SED >10^ 15  Hz or <10^ 14  Hz, respectively. , possibly being a `masquerading' FSRQ.

While both TXS 0506+056 flares do not have the significance of discovery, some interesting observations were made in coincidence with the 2017 and 2014-2015 observations by IceCube. E.g. the MASTER global optical telescope network reported that TXS 0506+056 was in a quiet state 73 s after the IceCube 2017 event, but 2 hr after it the optical flux increased at 50 level on top of the background, namely the biggest variation they recorded since 2005 . Additionally, during the 100 d neutrino-flare period across 2014-2015, another longer flare with about 3.5 significance was observed. VLBA 15 GHz observations in 2009-18 on the structure of the jet of TXS 0506+056, showed a curved jet structure, possibly a precessing jet with 10 yr period, with the 2017 alert in the bright precession phase or a cosmic collider of 2 jets  .

sources.summary: The paper finds repeated flares in J0509.4+0542, notes its IC170922A neutrino association, and discusses an alternative interpretation involving nearby J0505.3+0459.

quotes.quote: By visually inspecting these light curves we identified two repeated patterns of flares in J0505.3+0459 and J0509.4+0542 that are listed at the bottom of Table tab:res and are shown in Figs. fig:0505p1 and fig:0509p1 . The pattern in J0509.4+0542 does not follow the dependencies of the flux on the time scale (or ) transformation we considered in section sec:meth .

J0509.4+0542 is the first blazar where a high-confidence association of a multi-band flare with a neutrino detection was discovered Aartsen2018a . It is associated with the E = 264 TeV neutrino IC170922A, which arrived on MJD=58018.9. This is currently the most extensively studied connection between a neutrino and an AGN e.g., and references therein Halzen2022 , and the most reliable to date.
However, an alternative interpretation has been proposed for this set of neutrino events previously associated with J0509.4+0542.

sources.summary: TXS 0506+056 is presented as a BL Lac/temporary FSRQ-like blazar, the first high-significance cosmic-neutrino source candidate, with gamma-ray and neutrino-flare behavior.

quotes.quote: TXS 0506+056 is a BL Lac object suggested to have at least temporarily intrinsic properties of a FSRQ, Padovani2019 at a redshift z = 0.3365 0.0010 Paiano2018 , associated with the Fermi LAT source 4FGL J0509.4+0542. This source is a TeV http://tevcat.uchicago.edu/?mode=1&showsrc=309 emitter. A multimessenger campaign, triggered by the coincident observation of a gamma-ray flare and the 290 TeV IceCube neutrino IC-170922A (assuming _ ^ 2.19 neutrino spectrum), identified TXS 0506+056 as the possible source of high-energy neutrinos at a 3 significance level ICTXS2018a . With this, TXS 0506+056 became the first identified source of high-energy cosmic neutrinos at such high significance.

Going back to the archival data, the IceCube Collaboration found a 160-day long neutrino flare in 2014/2015 from the direction of TXS 0506+056 at a significance level of 3.5 ICTXS2018b . Despite being more dominant than IC-170922A, this neutrino flare happened during a low-gamma state of TXS 0506+056 Garrappa2019 .

sources.summary: The paper presents TXS 0506+056 as a BL Lac/neutrino-emitting AGN whose repeated neutrino events may be explained by a precessing inner jet and supermassive binary black hole model.

quotes.quote: Precessing jets as potential neutrino emitters britzentxs have reported evidence that the neutrino emission observed from TXS 0506+056 might be related to a collision of jetted material. In their model, a special viewing angle of the precessing inner jet plays a major role in obtaining the right circumstances for neutrino generation. The timescale inferred from the observed precession signatures is of the order of 10 yr.

Most recently, further neutrino emission has been observed from the direction of TXS 0506+056 on April 18, 2021 by Baikal-GVD baikal . On September 18, 2022, the IceCube Collaboration indicated that another neutrino arrived from the direction of TXS 0506+056 tjus . Both events support the prediction for repeated neutrino-emission based on the precession model for this source and a supermassive binary black hole model britzentxs, debruijn .

sources.summary: The paper treats TXS 0506+056 as a blazar neutrino-source candidate, noting IceCube associations, broadband modeling, and evidence for a broad-line region typical of FSRQs.

quotes.quote: Regarding these individual associations, the highest significance thus far has been obtained for the case of blazar TXS 0506+056, a BL Lac object for which neutrino emission has been established at the 3.5 level. This includes two observations: 1) a 290 TeV neutrino was detected by IceCube in 2017 in spatial coincidence with the source and in temporal coincidence with a six-month gamma-ray flare TXS_MM ; and 2) archival IceCube data shows an excess of neutrinos with energies of tens of TeV around the source's position during 2014-15, although this coincided with a low state in gamma rays TXS_orphanflare . Furthermore, after ten years of observations, the second hottest spot in the IceCube sky is coincident with the position of TXS 0506+056 Aartsen:2019fau , which can be regarded as additional evidence in support of this blazar as a neutrino source candidate.

For the 2017 flare of TXS 0506+056, some models suggest that the high-energy gamma rays co-emitted with the neutrinos interact through pair production with lower-energy photons in the jet, generating electromagnetic cascades whose energy is ultimately emitted by the source in the X-ray range e.g. Cerruti:2018tmc,Gao:2018mnu,Keivani:2018rnh,Sahu:2020eep . It is worth noting that while the very-high-energy gamma rays produced through hadronic processes cascade down to the X-ray regime, in the GeV gamma-ray range the source is optically thin in these models, which allows them to explain the bright state in GeV gamma rays observed during neutrino emission. The peak of this GeV emission, as well as the peak of the optical emission, are typically dominated by leptonic processes, while a potential hadronic signature is limited to the X-ray and, potentially, TeV ranges. As shown by Padovani:2019xcv , data show that TXS 0506+056 possesses a broad line region (BLR) surrounding the active black hole, which is a feature typical of flat-spectrum radio quasars (FSRQs). This lead to the reclassification of TXS 0506+056 as a masquerading BL Lac.

sources.summary: The paper characterizes TXS 0506+056 as a neutrino-associated TeV blazar and reports optical variability, blue-when-brighter behavior, and constraints on its emission-region size.

quotes.quote: TXS 0506 + 056 is registered as a blazar in the Texas Survey of Radio Sources catalog 1996AJ....111.1945D . The first detection of a high-energy neutrino event from a blazar was reported from TXS 0506 + 056 on 22 September 2017 by the IceCube collaboration and was coincident in direction and time with a ray flare 2018Sci...361..147I . Prompted by this discovery, an investigation was carried out of 9.5 years of IceCube neutrino observations to search for excess emission at the position of the blazar, and an excess of high-energy neutrino events between September 2014 and March 2015 at energies around 290 TeV at a 3.5 level was indeed detected 2018Sci...361.1378I .

During our observations, we consistently observed that TXS 0506 + 056 followed the BWB trend. This suggests that the increasing flux can be attributed to jet synchrotron emission, very possibly indicating an enhancement in particle acceleration efficiency 2015MNRAS.451.3882A . This BWB trend is seen in Figure fig:SED_variation which illustrates spectral steepening as the magnitude increases.

sources.summary: The paper models boosted relic-neutrino flux from cosmic-ray protons in blazar TXS 0506+056, assumes a 10 kpc host-galaxy path, and derives an overdensity upper limit near 10^10.

quotes.quote: In particular, we will calculate the flux of relic neutrinos boosted by cosmic ray protons in the Milky Way and the flux of relic neutrinos boosted by cosmic ray protons in the blazar TXS 0506+056, which is believed to be a proton accelerator from its detected emission in high-energy neutrinos . We will place an upper limit on the local, or TXS 0506+056 present relic neutrino overdensity from a non-observation of a particular spectral feature on top of the measured atmospheric and astrophysical high energy neutrino fluxes, or from an excess of events w..t experimental limits on cosmogenic neutrinos.

For TXS 0506+056, we calculate the high-energy proton flux following , with a cut-off at 1.7 10^ 9 GeV , and rescale it by 1/d^ 2 , where d=1835.4 Mpc. We assume that protons scatter off relic neutrinos over an effective distance of D_ eff =10 kpc in the host galaxy of TXS 0506+056, which lies under the mean free path of these protons for the relic neutrino overdensities considered in this work.

For these masses, we also find an upper limit on the neutrino overdensity of TXS 0506+056 of _ 10^ 10 , stronger than the bound from KATRIN. We also note that TXS 0506+056 is unlikely to be the only neutrino-emitting quasar.

sources.summary: The paper describes TXS 0506+056 as a blazar spatially associated with IceCube neutrino IC-170922A, gamma-ray brightening, ANTARES follow-up, and rapid optical state changes seen by MASTER.

quotes.quote: Following the alert, Fermi-LAT and MAGIC detected an increased gamma-ray flux from the known blazar TXS 0506+056 compatible with the direction of the IceCube neutrino alert. The probability of such an association reaches a 3 level. The TXS 0506+056 direction was also investigated by ANTARES in steady and time-dependant modes with 11 years of data .

The most famous example is the association between the high-energy neutrino IC170922 and the flaring blazar TXS 0506+056 . The discovery of an optical changing state in TXS 0506+056 by MASTER related to the neutrino is particularly interesting, at a 50 significance level. This blazar was detected by MASTER to be in the off-state after one minute and then switched to the on-state no later than two hours after the event.

sources.summary: The paper describes TXS 0506+056 as a blazar neutrino source, reclassified as a masquerading BL Lac with FSRQ-like emission lines.

quotes.quote: One alert event and its multi-wavelength follow-up observations identified the first non-stellar neutrino source: the blazar TXS 0506+056  . Blazars are one of the most known powerful non-transient sources and the identification of TXS 0506+056 as a neutrino source motivates the connection of IceCube alert events and blazars.

One major goal is to identify objects that are of a similar nature to TXS 0506+056. Even though TXS 0506+056 was first classified as a BL Lac, closer investigation of the source's spectrum has revealed emission lines common for Flat Spectrum Radio Quasars (FSRQs)  . This indicates that the object has a broad line region and its broad emission lines are outshone by its powerful jet  . Hence, TXS 0506+056 is not an ordinary BL Lac, but a masquerading BL Lac   .

sources.summary: The paper describes TXS 0506+056 as an active-galaxy neutrino source identified by a 2017 multimessenger event and archival burst, with harder burst-like emission and unresolved modeling challenges.

quotes.quote: After accumulating a decade of data with a detector with gradually improved sensitivity, the first high-energy neutrino sources emerged in the neutrino sky: the active galaxies NGC 1068, NGC 4151, PKS 1424+240 , and TXS 0506+056 . The observations point to the acceleration of protons and the production of neutrinos in the obscured dense cores surrounding the supermassive black holes at their center, typically within a distance of only 10 100 Schwarzschild radii . TXS 0506+056 had previously been identified as a neutrino source in a multimessenger campaign triggered by a neutrino of 290 energy, IC170922, and by the independent observation of a neutrino burst from the same source in archival IceCube data in 2014 .

Instead, modeling the multiwavelength spectrum of TXS 0506+056 represents an unmet challenge. The source is different: it emits neutrinos in bursts with Eddington luminosity characterized by a harder neutrino spectrum, close to E^ 2.2 . The gamma-ray obscured spectra at the time of neutrino production may indicate that the neutrinos are produced in the vicinity of the core as is the case for NGC 1068 but in association with catastrophic events hinted at by the observation of a strong optical flare in 2017 .

sources.summary: The paper models TXS 0506+056 as a flaring blazar associated with a high-energy neutrino, favoring a hybrid leptonic scenario with subdominant hadronic neutrino emission.

quotes.quote: Detection of the \ neutrino coincident with the flaring blazar , the first and only 3 high-energy neutrino source association to date, offers a potential breakthrough in our understanding of high-energy cosmic particles and blazar physics. We present a comprehensive analysis of \ during its flaring state, using newly collected , , and \ data with \ observations and numerical models to constrain the blazar's particle acceleration processes and multimessenger (electromagnetic and high-energy neutrino) emissions. Accounting properly for electromagnetic cascades in the emission region, we find a physically-consistent picture only within a hybrid leptonic scenario, with \ produced by external inverse-Compton processes and high-energy neutrinos via a radiatively-subdominant hadronic component. 1 We derive robust constraints on the blazar's neutrino and cosmic-ray emissions and demonstrate that, because of cascade effects, the 0.1--100 keV emissions of \ serve as a better probe of its hadronic acceleration and high-energy neutrino production processes than its GeV--TeV emissions. If the \ neutrino association holds, physical conditions in the \ jet must be close to optimal for high-energy neutrino production, 1 and are not favorable for ultra-high-energy cosmic-ray acceleration.

sources.summary: The paper links TXS 0506 + 056 to IceCube neutrino activity and reports WISE mid-infrared variability, including late brightening concurrent with the 2017 ray flare.

quotes.quote: The electromagnetic follow-up campaigns associated the event with the flaring ray blazar TXS 0506 + 056 Padovani2018 . Investigation of the archival data of the IceCube instrument revealed an excess of high-energy neutrinos in the direction of TXS 0506 + 056 compared to the atmospheric background between 2014 September and 2015 March sci2 possibly also associated with the blazar Padovani2018 .

TXS 0506 +056 shows significant MIR variability during year-long time scale (Fig. fig:lc ) with more than a factor of 2.5 peak-to-peak variation in both bands. A brightening can be seen in the last two mission phases, which is concurrent with the ray flare started in April 2017 and peaked around the IceCube 170922A event. No WISE observation is available during the previous excess neutrino event, however TXS 0506 + 056 became brighter in MIR after that period.

sources.summary: The paper models TXS 0506+056 during the IceCube-170922A episode, explaining its broadband emission and expected neutrino rate with a lepto-hadronic jet scenario.

quotes.quote: The observation of IceCube-170922A event from the direction of TXS 0506+056 when it was in its enhanced ray emission state offers a unique opportunity to investigate the lepto-hadronic processes in blazar jets. Here, the observed broadband emission of TXS 0506+056 is explained by boosted synchrotron/synchrotron self Compton emission from the jet whereas the ray data observed during the neutrino emission- by inelastic interactions of the jet-accelerated protons in a dense gaseous target. The proton energy distribution is E_ p ^ 2.50 , calculated straightforwardly from the data obtained by Fermi-LAT and MAGIC and if such distribution continues up to E_ ,p =10 PeV, the expected neutrino rate is as high as 0.46 events during the long active phase of the source or 0.15 if the activity lasts 60 days.

In this interpretation, the energy content of the protons above > GeV in blazar jets can be estimated as well: the required proton injection luminosity is 2.0 10^ 48 erg s^ 1 exceeding 10^3 times that of electrons 10^ 45 erg s^ 1 which are in equipartition with the magnetic field. As the required parameters are physically realistic, this can be an acceptable model for explanation of the neutrino and ray emission from TXS 0506+056.

sources.summary: The paper uses TXS 0506+056 as a blazar neutrino-source case: IceCube reported a coincident event and archival excess, ANTARES found no evidence, and KM3NeT/ARCA sensitivity is discussed.
