It features a highly disturbed structure [arXiv:1409.1239] and is actively interacting with a nearby companion galaxy, IZw 18C [arXiv:1501.06709, arXiv:1602.04772].

sources.summary: IZw 18 has triangular H I aligned with its optical body, an extended southern feature, gas linked to companion IZw 18C, and high-resolution peaks at optical star-forming knots.

quotes.quote: IZw 18 (also known as UGCA 166, Mrk 0116 or SBS 0930+554) From Lelli et al. (2012a; their Fig. 2, 3 and 4) and Lelli, Verheijan Fraternali (2014a, b; their Fig. C.16, and Fig. 1, 2), with the VLA. The intermediate resolution (20 x 20 ) H 1 image shows single-peaked "triangle-shaped" emission (PA _ HI 45 ^ ), with a Southeast extension that curves due South, along D 14 kpc. The main body of the H 1 emission is aligned with the optical morphology (PA _ opt 45 ^ ). The peak of the H 1 coincides with the position of the brightest (Northwest) star formation knot in the optical image.

The latter emission is likely associated with the companion galaxy, IZw 18C, with which it is interacting, at a distance of D 18 kpc. The kinematical axis (PA _ kin 50 ^ ) is roughly aligned with both the (main) H 1 and optical emission. The large Southern extension is at an almost constant velocity. At high resolution (5 x 5 ), the H 1 emission is resolved into two peaks, positionally coincident with the two brightest star formation knots in the optical image. There is also a halo of extended emission to the Northwest, englobing the interacting companion, IZw 18C.

sources.summary: The paper describes IZw18 as an intriguing blue dwarf analogue of high-redshift galaxies with extremely low metallicity and an intense ionizing/FUV radiation field.

quotes.quote: Since its discovery Zwicky1966 , the HII galaxy I Zwicky 18 (hereafter IZw18, also known as Mrk 116), is one of the most intriguing blue dwarf galaxies in the local Universe. Zwicky1966 described it as a system made by two compact galaxies, recognized now as two compact star forming regions associated with HII regions usually indicated as the NW and SE components Davidson1989 . It is located at a distance of 18.2 Mpc Aloisi2007 with a corresponding angular scale of 88 pc/ . It is considered an analogue of high-redshift galaxies and references therein Lebouteiller2013 and after the first studies Sargent1970,Searle1972,Searle1973 , it continues to keep attraction thanks to its extremely low metallicity: Z/Z _ 0.026 Kunth1986,Pagel1992,Vilchez1998,Izotov1999,Izotov1999b,Lebouteiller2013,Kehrig2016 .

The interstellar radiation field is very intense: in the SE knot we obtained very high values for the ionization parameter as high as U 10^2 , and FUV fluxes G/G_0 10^ 3.5 . These high values for U , are an additional indication of the underlying high ionizing flux in IZw18, given the large uncertainties associated with the ionization parameter estimates see Ucci2017b . The prominent ionizing flux in the SE knot in our maps, can be in fact associated with the presence of a clumpy HII component, consisting of numerous knots containing blue stars Dufour1996 . We confirmed the extreme poor metal content of IZw18: log( Z/Z_ ) = -1.32 0.01 (i.e., 12+log(O/H) = 7.37 0.01, corresponding to 1/20 solar).

sources.summary: The paper contrasts I Zw 18 with more massive very young galaxies, emphasizing its extreme gas fraction, isolation, low mass, and possible non-interaction trigger.

quotes.quote: Therefore, the normal metallicities of VYGs at given mass suggests that VYGs do not resemble objetcs like I Zw 18. Although we find that the gas mass fractions of VYGs with H i detections is very high, I Zw 18 has an even more extreme f_ gas value. The H i mass of I Zw 18 is 50 times higher than its stellar mass, and the total neutral gas mass fraction is f_ gas = 0.98 . To compute the gas mass fraction of I Zw 18, we adopted M_ H i = 1.25 10^8 M _ Engelbracht+08, Thuan+16 and M_ = 2.6 10^ 6 M _ Izotov+2014_SDSS_SFing .

But the stellar mass of I Zw 18 is 40 times lower than the lowest masses of our VYG sample, so it is difficult to make a meaningful comparison between this galaxy and the VYGs. Furthermore, while our VYGs tend to have close companions, I Zw 18 appears to be a very isolated system. Indeed, using the same approach described in Sect. sec:environment:local to investigate the local environment of our VYG sample, and assuming m_ , I Zw 18 =16.4 as the extinction-corrected Petrosian magnitude in the band of I Zw 18, we find that there is no other galaxy brighter than m_r = m_ , I Zw 18 + 1 up to 200 kpc from I Zw 18. This suggests that interactions with nearby galaxies cannot be the mechanism triggering the burst of SF in I Zw 18, and other processes must be invoked to explain the SF activity in this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper presents I Zw 18 as a nearby low-metallicity blue compact dwarf with strong nebular He II regions aligned with an ultraluminous X-ray source.

quotes.quote: With a metallicity of 12+Log(O/H) 7.1-7.2 , I Zw 18 is a canonical low metallicity blue compact dwarf (BCD) galaxy. A growing number of BCDs, including I Zw 18, have been found to host strong, narrow-lined, nebular He 2 ( 4686) emission with enhanced intensities compared to H (e.g. \ >1 ). We present new observations of I Zw 18 using the Keck Cosmic Web Imager. These observations reveal two nebular He 2 emission regions (or He 3 regions) northwest and southeast of the He 3 region in the galaxy's main body investigated in previous studies. All regions exhibit \ greater than 2 .

The two newly resolved He 3 regions lie along an axis which intercepts the position of I Zw 18's Ultra-luminous X-ray (ULX) source. We explore whether the ULX could power the two He 3 regions via shock activity and/or beamed X-ray emission. We find no evidence of shocks from the gas kinematics.

sources.summary: The review treats IZw 18 as a debated young-galaxy candidate with extremely metal-poor H II regions, weak CO emission, possible ongoing formation, and evidence for prior enrichment.

quotes.quote: Following the definition in Sect. 5.1, IZw 18 might be another young galaxy, although this is matter of debate because of the presence of evolved stars . It also hosts H ii regions of extremely low metallicity, 12 + log(O/H) = 7.17 0.03 in the northwestern and 7.18 0.03 in the southeastern component . Showing extremely weak CO emission , the galaxy may still be in the process of formation . IZw 18 is with D 18 Mpc ( M_ V 15 ^ M . 0; NED) much closer to us than SBS 0335--052. No red giant is found in measurements 1--2 magnitudes deeper than the expected tip of the red giant branch.

Many stars appear to be younger than half a billion years, a time during which up to three bursts of star -formation can be deduced, interrupted by more quiescent periods lasting 1--2 10 ^ 8 yr. Far UV (917--1188 ) absorption lines do not indicate the existence of dominant galaxy-wide in- or outflows. Based on HST far UV absorption line data, ref. investigated the metallicity of the massive H i envelope of IZw 18. It is as in SBS 0355--052 not zero, but roughly two thirds of that derived from the H ii regions. They argue that at least a part of this metallicity must have been generated prior to the present starburst, suggesting the existence of an older stellar generation.

sources.summary: The paper studies IZw18 as an extremely metal-poor blue compact dwarf, using MEGARA H-line kinematics to map its main body and halo gas structure and dynamics.

quotes.quote: This study is focused on I Zwicky 18 (hereafter IZw18), which is identified as a blue compact dwarf (BCD) galaxy e.g., petrosianterferometric,tikhonov2007red,ramos2011spatial located approximately 18 Mpc away 2017A&A...602A..45L . IZw18 has attracted attention due to its extremely low metallicity in its ionized gas (12+log(O/H)=7.11 approx 3 solar; e.g., ), which places IZw18 among the most metal-poor star-forming systems in the local Universe. Such low metallicity suggests the presence of chemically unevolved stellar populations and makes IZw18 one of the prime candidates for investigating galaxy formation and evolution processes happening in the primordial universe. IZw18 has a highly irregular morphology, a characteristic common among BCDs, which further enhances its scientific interest.

This study offers an in-depth analysis of the kinematic behavior of ionized gas in IZw18, a galaxy notable for its extremely low metallicity and proximity, utilizing data from MEGARA/GTC. We explored the structure and dynamics of the galaxy through H line profiles, applying single and double Gaussian component fittings to create detailed maps of luminosity, velocity, and velocity dispersion in the main body (MB) and Halo regions. Additionally, we retrieved integrated spectra from various galactic regions to achieve a greater signal-to-noise (S/N) ratio. In the MB, a rotational pattern is evident, yet a secondary, more complex kinematic pattern emerges from the double-component fitting, further enriched by the identification of a very broad component.

sources.summary: FUSE observations of I Zw 18 measure neutral-gas column densities and find the H I oxygen abundance consistent with its H II regions, with possible nitrogen and argon ionization effects.

quotes.quote: FUSE observations of the H I interstellar gas of I Zw 18 FUSE observations of I Zw 18 A. Lecavelier des Etangs 1 J.-M. D esert 1 D. Kunth 1 A. Vidal-Madjar 1 G. Callejo 1,2 R. Ferlet 1 G. H ebrard 1 V. Lebouteiller 1 A. Lecavelier des Etangs, lecaveli@iap.fr Institut d'Astrophysique de Paris, CNRS, 98 bis Bld Arago, F-75014 Paris, France LERMA, Observatoire de Paris - Section de Meudon, 5 Place Jules Janssen 92195 Meudon cedex, France Received 28 May 2003 / Accepted 20 September 2003 We present the analysis of FUSE observations of the metal-deficient dwarf galaxy . We measured column densities of H i , N i , O i , Ar i , Si ii , and Fe ii . The O i /H i ratio ( (O i /H i )= 4.7^ +0.8 _ 0.6 ) is consistent with the O/H ratio observed in the H ii regions (all uncertainties are 2- ). If the oxygen is depleted in the H i region compared to the H ii regions, the depletion is at most 0.5 dex.

\ (Mrk 116) is a blue compact dwarf galaxy with the smallest known abundance of heavy elements as measured from nebular emission lines. The observed emission lines originate from a pair of bright H ii regions in which a strong burst of star formation is taking place. In these H ii regions, the oxygen abundance is measured to be only 1/50 the solar value (Izotov et al.  ). The interstellar gas ionized by recently formed massive stars is surrounded by an envelope of neutral hydrogen (H i ).

sources.summary: The paper presents HST/NICMOS near-infrared imaging of I Zw 18, finding cool stellar populations, an intermediate-age component, carbon-star candidates, and evidence against a purely young-galaxy interpretation.

quotes.quote: We report results from near-infrared imaging of I Zw 18, the most metal-poor galaxy in the local universe with NICMOS on board the Hubble Space Telescope (HST). Observations were obtained in the broad F205W filter ( K) and in the medium-wide bands F171M and F180M, the latter which cover strong molecular absorption features in cool stars. The new data, together with previously obtained HST/NICMOS images in the F110W ( J) and F160W ( H) filters, provide a census of the cool stellar population in this chemically unevolved galaxy. We find that stars as old as 1 Gyr are required to explain the observed colour-magnitude diagrams. Combining broad and medium-wide band photometry, we have classified the observed stars.

The global properties of I Zw 18, i.e., extremely low nebular oxygen abundance, 12+ ( O/H ) = 7.18 (Izotov and Thuan ; just a few percent of the solar abundance), very blue colours, and a large H i reservoir, have been interpreted as evidence that this galaxy may be genuinely young, presently forming its very first generation of stars. If its star formation history really is restricted to the last few 10 Myr, the existence of I Zw 18 demonstrates that the formation of galaxies may be continuing also at the present cosmic epoch. Speaking against the youth hypothesis is the discovery of a population of faint red stars in I Zw 18, both in the optical (Aloisi et al. ) and in the near-infrared ( Ostlin ).

sources.summary: The paper describes IZw18 as a very metal-poor blue compact dwarf and uses deep HST Cepheid observations to constrain its distance and star-formation history.

quotes.quote: In the framework of an ongoing ACS@HST project (HST program 10586, PI: Aloisi) we have obtained deep multi-color imaging of the very metal-poor Blue Compact Dwarf galaxy IZw18. The data were acquired in time-series fashion to allow the identification of Classical Cepheids (CCs). The main aim of this project is to constrain both the distance and the Star Formation History of the galaxy. However, as a byproduct these data also provide new insights into the properties of CCs at very low metallicities. We have identified 24 candidate CCs in IZw18.

The Blue Compact Dwarf galaxy IZw18 is one of the most intriguing objects in the Local Universe. It has the lowest nebular metallicity of all known star forming galaxies Z=1/30-1/50 Z _ (depending on the metallicity scale), and it has long been regarded as a possible example of a galaxy undergoing its first burst of star formation, hence a local analog of primordial galaxies in the distant Universe. Previous HST observations with WFPC2 and NICMOS (Aloisi et al., 1999, Ostlin et al., 2000) detected AGB stars tracing an underlying population about 0.5 Gyr old. However, Izotov Thuan, (2004, IT04) failed to reveal the galaxy RGB tip on their ACS@HST data, thus suggesting that IZw18 does not host an old population. Since current estimates for the distance of IZw18 are very uncertain, it may be possible that IZw18 RGB tip was not detected because the galaxy is farther away than previously thought. We have used our ACS@HST deep time-series data of IZw18, to measure the galaxy distance with the CCs.

sources.summary: The paper models IZw18 as an extremely metal-poor dwarf, testing bursty star-formation histories and showing that AGB yield choices strongly affect predicted nitrogen abundances.

quotes.quote: The model reproduces the main features of the gas-rich dwarf galaxy IZw18 and assumes two bursts of star formation separated by 300 Myr of quiescence. Only the evolution after the second burst is shown. The oxygen is almost unaffected (most of the elements are produced by massive stars). Looking at the evolution of C/O and N/O, the difference between the AGB and non-AGB models, and the disagreement between the non-AGB model and the observations (represented by shaded areas in Fig. 1), are striking.

The evolution of log (C/O) and log (N/O) for the IZw18 gasping models is shown in Fig. 1 (right side). The dependence of log (C/O) on the assumed nucleosynthetic prescriptions is rather small ( 0.1 dex at most), whereas the difference in the nitrogen production is very remarkable. In particular, MM02 already produce nitrogen in a primary way in massive stars, so that the predicted N/O is larger than for the other sets of yields in the first tens of Myr.

sources.summary: The paper describes I Zwicky 18 as an extremely metal-poor blue compact dwarf whose HST photometry reveals stellar populations from a few Myr to possibly 13 Gyr.

quotes.quote: All our results suggest that IZw18 is not atypical compared to other BCDs. galaxies: evolution --- galaxies: stellar content --- galaxies: dwarfs --- galaxies: individual (IZw18) I Zwicky 18 (also Mrk 116 or UGCA 166, hereafter IZw18) is the most intriguing Blue Compact Dwarf (BCD) galaxy in the local Universe. It became famous right after its discovery, when measured from its emission-line spectrum an oxygen abundance [ O/H ] = --1.14 dex, only 7 of the solar value, indicating an almost unprocessed gas content. The first studies on its color and composition sargent70,searle72,searle73 emphasized its almost primordial evolutionary status. IZw18 shows very blue colors, U B =-0.88 and B V =-0.03 vanzee98 , suggesting the presence of a very young stellar population, with a current star formation rate (SFR) much higher than the past mean value. The total mass of IZw18 from the rotation curve at a radius of 10 ^ 12 ^ is estimated to be 10^8 M _ e.g., davidson85,petrosian97,vanzee98 , where the neutral gas corresponds to 70 of the total mass, but only 10^7 M _ of HI is associated with the optical part of the galaxy e.g., lequeux80,vanzee98 .

IZw18 contains stars in all the evolutionary phases visible at its distance: massive and intermediate mass stars on the MS, blue and red supergiants, AGB and TP-AGB stars, carbon stars, RGB stars. Hence, it hosts stars of all ages, from a few Myr up to possibly 13 Gyr.

sources.summary: The paper describes I Zw 18 as an extremely metal-poor blue compact dwarf with a compact rotating gas disk, high central mass concentration, and likely interaction or merger-driven starburst.

quotes.quote: I Zw 18 is a prototype Blue Compact Dwarf (BCD), characterized by a strong starburst and extremely low metallicity ( Z 0.02 Z_ ). It has long been considered a candidate young galaxy in the Local Universe, but recent studies indicate the presence of old stars. We analysed archival VLA observations of the 21 cm line and found that the associated to the starburst region forms a compact fast-rotating disk. The column densities are very high, up to 50-100 M _  pc ^ 2 ( 0.6-1.2 10^ 22 atoms cm ^ 2 ). The rotation curve is flat with a steep rise in the inner parts, indicating the presence of a strong central concentration of mass.
There is also extended emission towards the outlying stellar complex I Zw 18 C and a 13.5 kpc tail. An interaction/merger between gas-rich dwarfs is the most likely explanation for the starburst.

sources.summary: The paper derives I Zw 18's SFH from HST/ACS CMDs, finding it is not newly formed, contains old stars, and has intense recent star formation explaining its blue, gas-rich appearance.

quotes.quote: We have derived the star formation history (SFH) of the blue compact dwarf galaxy I Zw 18 through comparison of deep HST/ACS data with synthetic color magnitude diagrams. A statistical analysis was implemented for the identification of the best-fit SFH and relative uncertainties. We confirm that I Zw 18 is not a truly young galaxy, having started forming stars earlier than 1 Gyr ago, and possibly at epochs as old as a Hubble time. In I Zw 18' s main body we infer a lower limit of 2 10^ 6 M_ for the mass locked-up in old stars. I Zw 18' s main body has been forming stars very actively during the last 10 Myr, with an average star formation rate (SFR) as high as 1 M_ /yr (or 2 10^ 5 M_ yr^ 1 pc^ 2 ).

The high current SFR can explain the very blue colors and the high ionized gas content in I Zw 18, resembling primeval galaxies in the early Universe. Detailed chemical evolution models are required to quantitatively check whether the SFH from the synthetic CMDs can explain the low measured element abundances, or if galactic winds with loss of metals are needed.

sources.summary: The paper describes IZw18 as an extremely metal-poor nearby star-forming galaxy whose nebular HeII emission may require unusual very hot, nearly metal-free ionizing stars.

quotes.quote: We performed new IFS observations of IZw18 using the PMAS IFU R05 on the 3.5m telescope at CAHA (fig. kehrig.izw18.fig3 ; K15). IZw18 is a nearby SF galaxy, well known for its extremely low Z (1/40) Z _ e.g., jvm98 , and it is considered an excellent local analog of primeval systems. Our IFS data reveal for the first time the entire nebular HeII 4686-emitting region (fig. kehrig.izw18.fig4 ) and corresponding total HeII-ionizing photon flux [ Q(HeII) _ obs ] in IZw18.

IZw18, as the most metal-poor HeII-emitter in the local Universe, is an ideal object to perform this study. Our observations combined with stellar model predictions point out that conventional excitation sources (e.g. single WRs, shocks, X-ray binaries) cannot convincingly explain the total Q(HeII) _ obs derived for IZw18 e.g., mm05,crowther06,L14 . Other mechanisms are probably also at work. If the HeII-ionization in IZw18 is due to stellar sources, these might be peculiar very hot stars.

sources.summary: The paper includes IZw 18 as an XMP with a companion galaxy, an adopted H I mass consistent with Cormier et al., and a lower plotted SFR in that comparison.

quotes.quote: The Dwarf Galaxy Survey (DGS; Madden et al. 2013) contains 18 sources in common with the original XMP sample, only three of which possess CO observations that yield H _2 mass upper limits (Cormier et al. 2014): SBS 0335-052, IZw 18 and VII Zw 403 (UGC 6456). The integrated measurements for these XMPs are included in the subsequent analysis, where _ H_2 (Spitzer, APEX Atacama Pathfinder Experiment , Mopra 22-m and IRAM Institut de Radioastronomie Millim etrique 30-m data), _ HI (see Madden et al. 2013 for details) and _ SFR (Spitzer data) are sampled within r_ dust 50 -- 90 or r_ CO 10 -- 30 (the dust and CO aperture, respectively).

Two of these sources, the XMPs SBS 0335-052 and IZw 18 (see Filho et al. 2015 and references therein for details; see also the Appendix), are documented interacting galaxies, and UM 461 is suspected to be interacting (Doublier et al. 1999). These sources could represent a transient, perhaps less advanced phase of the interaction process, whereby the HI (and possibly the H _2 ) content is high, but they have not begun forming stars at the appropriate rate.

IZw 18 also possesses a companion galaxy (Dufour, Esteban Casta neda 1996). The total HI mass (log(M _ HI /M _ ) 8.0) adopted by Cormier et al. (2014) for IZw 18 is consistent with the value adopted in the present work (Filho et al. 2013 and references therein). However, the location of IZw 18 on the Cormier et al. (2014) KS plot (Fig. 1a) is over an order of magnitude lower in _ SFR than the estimation in the present work (Fig. 1b), due to the difference in the probed area (4.8 kpc ^2 versus 1.2 kpc ^2 ), and adopted SFR (10 ^ 2.3 M _ yr ^ 1 versus 10 ^ 1.7 M _ yr ^ 1 ), which in Cormier et al. (2014) is sampled in the thermal infrared regime.

sources.summary: The review presents IFS observations of IZw18, describing it as an extremely metal-poor high-ionization starburst with mapped emission-line structure and excitation variations around its NW component.

quotes.quote: We performed new IFS observations of IZw18 using the PMAS IFU on the 3.5m telescope at CAHA (see K15, K16). IZw18 is a high-ionization galaxy which is among the most metal-poor ( Z 1/40 Z _ ; e.g. Pagel et al. 1992; V lchez Iglesias-P aramo 1998) starbursts in the local Universe. This makes IZw18 an excellent analog for primeval systems.

Fig. izw18.fig1 reveals that the spatial distribution of the emission in H , HeII 4686 and [ OIII ] 4363 is peaked towards the NW component while the [ OII ] 3727 emission reaches its maximum at the SE component. Also, by inspecting the maps of [ OIII ] 5007/H , [ NII ] 6584/H and [ OIII ] / [ OII ] , there is a clear tendency for the gas excitation to be higher at the location of the NW knot and thereabouts. The spatial distribution of the abundance indicator R _ 23 is found to be relatively flat without any significant peak (see the R _ 23 map in Fig. izw18.fig1 ). However, the ionization parameter diagnostic [ OIII ] / [ OII ] does not show a homogeneous distribution with the highest values of [ OIII ] / [ OII ] found within the NW knot, as mentioned above.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IZw18 among the lowest-metallicity observed galaxies and compares its derived sulphur abundance for southeast and northwest regions with literature values, finding agreement for the northwest region.

quotes.quote: Some very well studied objects, among which are included the two lowest metallicity observed galaxies: IZw18 and SBS0335-052E, have data from two different sources, which can serve to estimate the magnitude of the internal errors in this kind of observations.

On the other extreme, the lowest sulphur abundances are found for objects in the \ Gal sub-sample, with the lowest values, of about only 2 of the solar one, found for SBS0035-052E and IZw18. In these two cases the comparison with the abundances derived by the authors of the articles of reference is difficult. For IZw18SE 1993ApJ...411..655S , give 12+log(S/H)= 5.55 0.06, that should be compared to our derived value of 5.70 0.04. However, it is not clear which electron temperature is used to derive the S ^ ++ /H ^+ ionic ratio and no value of T _e ( [ SIII ] ) is given.

For IZw18NW, the value of 12+log(S/H) given by 1993ApJ...411..655S , and the one derived in the present work are fully consistent within the errors. This is probably due to the fact that our value of T _e ( [ SIII ] ) (19400 K) is very close to their value of T _e ( [ OIII ] ) (19600 K).

sources.summary: The paper describes I Zw 18 as a nearby metal-poor dwarf with young star clusters and diffuse emission, arguing that low-temperature hot gas can power its ionization and X-ray luminosity.

quotes.quote: \ is one of the nearest ( d_ I Zw 18 =18.2 Mpc) and most metal-poor ( Z_ I Zw 18 0.02Z_ ) dwarf galaxies. It contains two large \ regions ionized by star clusters with ages between a few to a hundred megayears Hunter1995, Papaderos2002, Contreras2011, Rickards2021 . \ is filled with diffuse \ emission. From the observed luminosity in the 4686 line, Kehrig2015 estimate a rate of \ ionizing photons as Q( He ^+) 50 [ s ^ 1 ] .

However, according to our model the plasma temperature in \ is significantly lower. Using the apec model with 1.16 MK and Z=0.02 Z_ , the absorption-corrected X-ray luminosity of diffuse gas in \ is L_ X ^ I Zw 18 (0.3-10 keV ) 10^ 39 erg s ^ 1 , which is in agreement with our model predictions. The extended X-ray radiation detected by Chandra is likely only a small fraction of the warm-hot gas filling this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper uses IFU and KCWI data for J0934+5514, handles saturated optical lines, and derives its OII ratio from the KCWI spectrum.

quotes.quote: For the remaining galaxies of the sample (J0036-3333, J0127-0619, J0337-0502, J0405-3648, J0934+5514, and J0144+0453), we used integral field spectroscopy data, when available, or long-slit spectroscopy, instead of SDSS. Specifically, we used VLT/VIMOS integral field unit (IFU) from james09 for J0127-0619, MMT Blue Channel Spectrograph spectra from senchyna19a for J0144+0453, Keck/KCWI IFU spectra from rickardsvaught21 for J0934+5514, and VLT/MUSE IFU spectra for the remaining three galaxies.

Concerning the IFU data available for J0021+0052, J0036-3333, J0127-0619, J0337-0502, J0405-3648, J0934+5514, J1044+0353 and J1418+2102, we extracted a spectrum from a 2.5" aperture centred at the same coordinates of COS observations to match the COS aperture (see also berg22 and Paper V arellano-cordova22 ). Specifically, the integrated VIMOS spectrum of J0127-0619 is obtained combining the high-resolution blue and orange grisms, covering the wavelength range 4150-7400 \ with a spectral resolution R 1150-2150 (see james09 for more details). The KCWI spectrum of J0934+5514 is in the wavelength range 3500-5600 \ at a median spectral resolution of R 3600 .

We did not fix the 4959,5007 fluxes because in the KCWI data of J0934+5514 and SDSS data of J1253-0312 the 5007 line is saturated. For these objects we obtained an estimate of the 5007 by applying the fixed line ratio of 3 with respect to 4959 osterbrock89 . We notice that for J1253-0312 also the \ line is clipped, so we discarded its flux. Concerning 1907,9, we constrained the line ratios 1907/ 1909 to vary up to 1.6 osterbrock89 , to avoid non-physical values. Our procedure allows us to fit this doublet with two well-separated Gaussians, since the distance between the centroids of the two emission lines of the doublet is fixed. Conversely, the resolution of the COS/G185M ( R 20000 @2100 ) does allow us to resolve the \ doublet even after the 6-12 native pixel binning (i.e., 40-80 km/s), as long as the width of the fitted emission lines is smaller than half of their wavelength separation (i.e., 300 km/s). Among the galaxies with significant \ emission ( S/N>3 ), the latter condition is not satisfied in J1044+0353 and J1418+2102, because of the lower resolution of COS/G140L. Concerning 3727,29 doublet, blended in SDSS, MOD and MMT data, the distribution of flux between these two Gaussians is not reliable enough to derive an accurate line ratio. Therefore, the \ ratio is derived only for the KCWI data of J0934+5514 (the doublet is not covered by the wavelength range of MUSE).

sources.summary: The paper presents STIS UV imaging and spectra of starburst galaxy I Zw 18, identifying two WC Wolf-Rayet clusters, noting its very low-metallicity blue compact nature, and finding H I concentrated in the southeastern half.

quotes.quote: We present UV images and spectra of the starburst galaxy I Zw 18, taken with the Space Telescope Imaging Spectrograph. The high spatial resolution of these data allows us to isolate clusters containing Wolf-Rayet stars of the subtype WC. Our far-UV spectra clearly show C 4 1548,1551 and He 2 1640 emission of WC stars in two clusters: one within the bright (NW) half of I Zw 18, and one on the outskirts of this region. The latter spectrum is unusual, because the C 4 is seen only in emission, indicating a spectrum dominated by WC stars. These data also demonstrate that the H 1 column in I Zw 18 is strongly peaked in the fainter (SE) half of I Zw 18, with a column depth far larger than that reported in previous analyses.

I Zw 18 (Mrk 116) represents an important local fiducial in our understanding of galaxy formation and evolution. It is a blue compact galaxy with the smallest known abundance of heavy elements in its ionized gas ( 2 of solar; V i lchez Isglesias-P a ramo 1998 and references therein).

sources.summary: The paper presents I Zw 18 as a two-knot, low-SFR star-forming dwarf with young stellar populations, plus a strong X-ray point source and fainter extended emission.

quotes.quote: I Zw 18 is composed of two bright knots of star formation separated by 5 and referred to as the brighter northwest (NW) and fainter southeast (SE) components Papaderos2002 . Star formation proceeds differently in I Zw 18 as compared to SBS 0335 052. There are no SSCs in I Zw 18 and the derived SFR is one order of magnitude smaller ( 0.04 M _ yr ^ 1 ) than in SBS 0335 052. Papaderos2002 and Hunt2003a , using deep optical and near-infrared imaging, have put an upper limit of 500 Myr for the age of I Zw 18.

The point source is spatially coincident with the bright NW component of I Zw 18. The faint extended component appears to lie along the main body of I Zw 18 with some X-ray emission centered on the northwest component and some in between the northwest and southeast optical peaks. Bomans2002 in a preliminary reduction of the same Chandra data also found the X-ray emission of I Zw 18 to be composed of a strong point source and fainter extended emission, although we fail to recover the SW and NE extensions these authors claim to see.

sources.summary: The paper includes I Zw 18 in a Chandra dwarf-starburst comparison and reports no diffuse X-ray detection, attributing the non-detection partly to short exposure, small volume, and low metallicity.

quotes.quote: We present a comprehensive comparison of the X-ray properties of a sample of eight dwarf starburst galaxies observed with Chandra (I Zw 18, VII Zw 403, NGC 1569, NGC 3077, NGC 4214, NGC 4449, NGC 5253, He 2--10). In Paper I we presented in detail the data reduction and analysis of the individual galaxies. For the unresolved X-ray sources we find the following: point sources are in general located close to bright \ regions, rims of superbubbles, or young stellar clusters.

No diffuse X-ray emission is detected in I Zw 18 and VII Zw 403. The integration times of both objects are the lowest of all the galaxies in the sample and the detection limits of the unabsorbed luminosities are of order 2-4 10^ 38 \ (see Paper I). The 5--10 times smaller size of I Zw 18 and VII Zw 403 (see Table tab:sample_props_opt ), however, reduces the volume of these galaxies up to three orders of magnitude. For this reason, the hot gas might be below the detection threshold if it has similar properties to that found in the rest of the sample.

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes I Zw 18 for helium abundance work, finding increased temperature and abundance while noting it fails quality criteria and lies below the regression line.

quotes.quote: I Zw 18 showed a temperature increase (10,700 to 17,300 K) and a resulting increased abundance. This difference in temperature is significant, and results in an increase in the helium abundance of about 1.5 sigma. Note, however, that I Zw 18 was not included in the original ``high quality'' dataset of Paper II because it did not meet two of the criteria (EW(H ) and redshift), so this is likely due more to the quality of the spectrum and the looser constraint on the derived temperature. Nonetheless, the newly derived temperature is much more in line with that expected from the temperature derived from observations of the [ O III ] emission lines.

I Zw 18 was not analyzed in os04 both because of its low equivalent width for H ( EW(H )=135<200 ) making it susceptible to large corrections for underlying absorption and because of it's radial velocity making it susceptible to contamination by Galactic Na I absorption. I Zw 18 is below the regression line and, being the lowest metallicity point, it would lower the intercept and raise the slope.

sources.summary: The paper studies I Zw 18 as the lowest-metallicity blue compact dwarf known, whose X-ray emission is dominated by one luminous binary now seen at its highest flux.

quotes.quote: I Zw 18 is a nearby BCD with the lowest known metallicity and the highest X-ray luminosity (Kaaret et al. \ 2011). The X-ray emission from I Zw 18 is dominated by a single X-ray binary and observations reported to date have shown a maximum luminosity below the Eddington luminosity of a 20 M_ compact object. Here, we report on a measurement of the X-ray spectrum of I Zw 18 that reveals the highest flux yet observed.
This extraction region is larger than the extent of I Zw 18 and therefore includes all X-ray emission from the galaxy.

The Chandra X-ray Observatory observed I Zw 18 for 41 ks on 2000 February 8 and imaging and spectroscopic results were reported by Bomans02 and Thuan04 . The imaging shows that the X-ray emission was dominated by a single point source with at least 96 of the flux.

sources.summary: The paper identifies I Zw 18 as one of the most metal-deficient BCDs, with very low cold dust content but a modeled dust-to-gas ratio consistent with other estimates.

quotes.quote: Spectral energy distributions (SEDs) in these ranges are dominated by dust emission resulting from reprocessing of UV radiation from young massive stars, in particular in the most metal-deficient BCDs I Zw 18 and SBS 0335 052E W07,H04 .

The two most-metal deficient galaxies, I Zw 18 and SBS 0335 052E are among the galaxies of the Herschel sample with the lowest cold dust mass (Table tab1 ). In Fig. fig10 a, they have low M_ cd / M_* mass ratios of 10 ^ 4 , which are lower by a factor of 10 than the average value for the sample. This, however, does not deviate more than by 1 from other sample galaxies.

The lowest global dust-to-gas mass ratios are derived for the two lowest-metallicity BCDs, I Zw 18 and SBS 0335 052E, shown in Fig. fig13 a. They are in nice agreement with dust-to-gas mass ratios obtained for these galaxies by RR14 and for some other most-metal poor galaxies with 12 + logO/H 7.6 this paper, LF98,E08 .

sources.summary: The paper uses I Zw 18 as an extreme BCD with an extended nebular halo, showing how composite stellar and nebular profiles affect inferred Sersic parameters.

quotes.quote: Another case is represented by I Zw 18 , an extremely metal-poor BCD that is immersed within an extended nebular halo contributing 1/3 of its total R band luminosity P02 . As shown in PapOst12 , the R band profile of this galaxy (SBP1) reflects the superposition of two nearly exponential luminosity components differing in their scale length and central surface brightness _0 , namely the steep inner component that is primarily due to stellar emission and dominates down to 24.5 , and the surrounding shallower nebular envelope. The best-fitting for the combined SBP depends on the limiting surface brightness, being 1.2 for a \ 24.5 \ and reaching an 2 for a \ 29 \ (dashed gray curve in Fig. IZw18 ).

In the latter case, a marginally resolved distant morphological analog of I Zw 18 (e.g., a high-sSFR compact galaxy or quasar surrounded by an extended Ly envelope) could, purely on the basis of its high , be misclassified as an evolved ETG. Repeated modeling of SBP1 2 with \ (Fig. IZw18 ) reveals a far weaker dependence of the \ solution on : even for a \ 29 , the inferred reaches maximal values of 1.7 and 2.5 for SBP1 and SBP2, respectively. These lower 's better reflect the fact that 2/3 of the optical luminosity of I Zw 18 originates from the steeper stellar emission-dominated inner exponential component.

sources.summary: The paper presents IZw18 as a prototype extremely metal-poor blue compact dwarf whose recent stellar mass formation and limited ancient population make it a VYG proxy.

quotes.quote: Moreover, as shown in the comprehensive analysis of all available data for the prototype XMP blue compact galaxy IZw18 and its fainter companion IZw18C by PO12 , despite the detection with HST of red giant branch stars, the conclusion that the main stellar mass was formed during the very recent cosmological epoch ( 1 Gyr) remains valid.

We mention also the results of the HST data analysis for the system of IZw18 and IZw18C by Annibali2013 . They confirm the detection of the substantial stellar population with ages of 1 Gyr. Based on this fact, the authors conclude that IZw18 is not a truly young galaxy forming its first generation of stars. However, we use in this paper the relaxed definition of Very Young Galaxy as suggested by Tweed18 . Within this framework, with the current limits to their ancient star formation history and the mass of stars with ages of a few to ten Gyrs, IZw18 and IZw18C have properties consistent with their being VYGs.

sources.summary: The review uses IZw18 as an extremely metal-poor, dust-poor local galaxy where static neutral gas produces deep Lyman-alpha absorption instead of the expected emission.

quotes.quote: A good lesson was provided by the extremely low-metallicity, dust-poor local galaxy IZw18, where bright \ emission was expected and where the HST revealed a deep absorption instead . We understand now from the UV metal lines that the neutral gas is static in IZw18, which disables the \ escape. We have to take into account these effects when interpreting high- z samples, especially in experiments where only emission is targeted.

The extremely metal-poor IZw 18 is one of the surprising examples of a deep absorption. This finding illustrates the importance of H i kinematics for the \ escape , which we described in Section sec:Lya . The absence of \ emission in a subset of BCGs is an important realization for high- z surveys, where the \ technique will certainly be missing such galaxy populations.

sources.summary: The paper studies extremely metal-poor galaxy IZw18, finding its HMXB varies modestly, cannot supply the observed He II ionization budget, and is offset from the He II peak.

quotes.quote: Here we present a new study on the effects that X-ray sources have on the He ii ionization in the extremely metal-poor galaxy IZw18 (Z 3 Z _ ), whose X-ray emission is dominated by a single high-mass X-ray binary (HMXB). This study uses optical integral field spectroscopy, archival Hubble Space Telescope observations, and all of the X-ray data sets publicly available for IZw18. We investigate the time-variability of the IZw18 HMXB for the first time; its emission shows small variations on timescales from days to decades.

The best-fit models for the HMXB X-ray spectra cannot reproduce the observed He ii ionization budget of IZw18, nor can recent photoionization models that combine the spectra of both very low metallicity massive stars and the emission from HMXB. We also find that the IZw18 HMXB and the He ii emission peak are spatially displaced at a projected distance of 200 pc. These results reduce the relevance of X-ray photons as the dominant He ii ionizing mode in IZw18, which leaves uncertain what process is responsible for the bulk of its HeII ionization.

sources.summary: The paper uses I Zw 18 as a metal-poor galaxy with a central ULX, adopting its empirically constrained UV-to-X-ray SED while noting debate over whether the ULX supplies He-ionising photons.

quotes.quote: For example, argued that the observations of the low metallicity galaxy I Zw 18 used by for the scaling, show an insufficient contribution of the ULX in this galaxy to \ ionising photons.

A galaxy SED including an ULX -- I Zw 18 In addition to the above SEDs, which primarily focus on the X-ray part of the spectrum and describe ULXs, we have also used a SED of the well-known metal-poor galaxy I Zw 18, which harbours a ULX source in the center of its main \ region, I Zw 18-NW. Detailed multi-wavelength observations covering numerous emission lines and tracers from different ISM phases are available for this region. have studied this object in depth and demonstrated the importance of the ULX for the understanding of this giant \ region. Their SED (standard, \ = 4 10^ 40 ), including stellar and X-ray emission constructed using multiple, empirically adjusted components and an accretion disk model (diskbb) for the X-ray source, is shown in Fig. \ fig_norms and denoted by LEB subsequently. As discussed in , the SED was empirically adjusted at energies close the He ^+ ionisation potential. Since this is a ``combined'' and empirically-constrainted galaxy SED, covering the entire spectral range (from UV to X-rays) or relevance here, the X-ray contribution is kept fixed.

sources.summary: The paper describes I Zw 18 as an extreme dwarf galaxy with very low line ratios, extended He II emission, disfavored HMXB ionization, and high-ionization conditions analogous to high-redshift galaxies.

quotes.quote: Compared to the values of C3He2-O3He2 spanned by the local dwarf galaxies, our high-redshift composite spectrum is offset to the lower-left region, close to the two well-known dwarf galaxies with the lowest / \ ratios, SBS 0335-052 and I Zw 18. Our high-redshift composite spectrum overlaps better with the region spanned by the z 2 - 4 \ emitters. The offset from the region spanned by most dwarf galaxies might suggest that reionization-era galaxies generally have harder ionizing radiation and are capable of producing more \ ionizing photons ( >54.4 eV) than those typical dwarf galaxies. The two extreme dwarf galaxies, SBS 0335-052 and I Zw 18, might be more similar to the high-redshift galaxies and could provide some hints at the \ ionizing sources. In particular, the extended \ 4686 emission has already been observed in these two galaxies Kehrig2015,Kehrig2018,RickardsVaught2021 .
For example, ionization from high-mass X-ray binaries is also disfavored by comparing the observed X-ray luminosity to \ luminosity in SBS 0335-052 Kehrig2018 and I Zw 18 Kehrig2021 .

The position of the composite spectrum on the UV line-ratio diagnostic diagram overlaps the regions spanned by the local extreme dwarf galaxies, I Zw 18 and SBS 0335-052, and z 2 - 4 \ emitters, suggesting high ionization mechanisms. It may be possible to study these lower redshift extreme galaxies as analogs of ``normal'' star-forming high-redshift galaxies, This can provide clues to the ionization mechanisms in high-redshift galaxies.

sources.summary: The review treats IZw18 as a blue compact, extremely metal-poor galaxy whose HST color-magnitude diagrams reveal both recent star formation and an old stellar population, constraining chemical-evolution models.

quotes.quote: The important implications that CMDs can have on chemical evolution models of fairly distant galaxies can be examplified by discussing the interesting case of IZw18. Until very recently, this blue compact galaxy was considered the prototype of a really young system, experiencing now its first burst of star formation. This youth was considered the best (possibly the only) way to explain its extremely low metallicity (Z=1/50 Z _ ). A few years ago, Hunter Thronson (1995) and Dufour et al. (1996) obtained HST-WFPC2 images of IZw18, allowing for the first time to resolve a few hundreds of its individual stars.

Both diagrams show two groups of stars: a brighter, bluer group, corresponding to stars of 12 M _ , born during a recent burst occurred around 12-15 Myr ago (the exact values depending of course on the adopted distance), and a faint, red group, corresponding to stars with mass lower than 3 M _ and age between 0.3 and 15 Gyr. Hence, an old stellar population definitely exists in IZw18 and rules out the hypothesis of one single and recent SF burst (see Aloisi et al. 1999 and Ostlin 2000 for details).

sources.summary: The paper rederives helium abundance parameters for I Zw 18 using Cloudy-based temperature corrections, finding T(He ii) below T(O iii) and using that result in the primordial-helium sample.

quotes.quote: In sections 4 to 7 we redetermine the He ^+ /H ^+ values for I Zw 18, NGC 2363, Haro 29, and SBS 0335--052, based on the observations of izo97 and izo99 and on photoionization models for these objects. These four objects were selected from their sample based on the following criteria: the two objects with the lowest heavy element abundances and the two objects with the smallest observational errors pei01 . We derive He ^+ /H ^+ , N_e (He  ii ), T (He  ii ), and (3889) self-consistently based on all the observed He  i line intensities, with the exception of those strongly affected by underlying absorption. By combining the He ^+ /H ^+ values of these four objects with that of NGC 346 we derive a new Y_p (nHc) in section 8.

From photoionization models of I Zw 18 based on Cloudy lur01 we find that: ICF( He ) = 1.00 , (3889) = 0.010 0.005 , and t^2 (O  iii ) = 0.013 0.006 . We consider t^2 (O  iii ) as a lower limit to t^2 (see equations et2  and  et22t32 ). From the formalism of section  stemp together with =0.265 and T (O  iii ) = 19,060  K izo99 we obtain t^2= 0.024 0.006 and consequently that T (He  ii ) is 10.2 smaller than T (O  iii ).

sources.summary: The paper summarizes prior chemo-dynamical modelling of IZw18, describing it as the locally most metal-poor galaxy, probably young in those models, and useful for interpreting NGC1569.

quotes.quote: In the following, we briefly summarize the main results presented in a series of papers (Recchi et al. 2001; 2002; 2004) where we have analyzed the dynamical and chemical evolution of IZw18, the most metal-poor galaxy locally known. Although these models were tailored on this specific object, they give a hint on the enrichment of a galaxy in the first hundreds of Myrs of evolution, and in addition, IZw18 will become useful in our discussion. Either by assuming a gasping SF scenario or a bursting one, no stars older than 300--500 Myr are required in order to reproduce the characteristics of IZw18. In summary, a significant contribution of intermediate-mass stars is decisive in the chemical and dynamical evolution of IZw18, but no old stars are required.

From a chemodynamical point of view, IZw18 is therefore a young galaxy. This sets a robust constraint on the age of this galaxy (of the order of a few hundred Myrs) and lets them conclude that IZw18 is a bona fide young galaxy (at least young in a cosmological sense), in agreement with the chemodynamical calculations and at variance with recent claims that most of the Blue Compact Dwarf galaxies (including IZw18) host a very old (with an age of several Gyrs) population of stars (e.g. Ostlin 2000).

sources.summary: The paper reviews IZw18 as an extremely metal-poor blue compact dwarf whose age remains debated, with deeper HST photometry increasingly supporting older stellar populations and planned Cepheid/RGB distance tests.

quotes.quote: IZw18 has been recurrently claimed to be a young galaxy, but stars of increasingly older ages are found every time deeper magnitude levels are reached with high-resolution photometry: from the original few Myrs to, possibly, several Gyrs. The Blue Compact Dwarf galaxy IZw18 is the most metal poor star-forming galaxy ever discovered, with a metallicity between 1/30 and 1/50 of solar, depending on the adopted solar value (e.g., , ). IZw18 is not only extremely metal poor, but also very blue and full of gas, three properties that originally suggested it to be forming now its first stars and make it the system in the local Universe most similar to primeval galaxies.

A result implying that stars at least 500 Myr old are present in the galaxy, and confirmed by , who independently measured the same AGB stars on near-infrared HST-Nicmos images. As shown by the isochrone labels in Fig. fig:cmd ( b ), this would imply an age significantly older than 2 Gyr, possibly 10 Gyr or more. The combination of this new photometry with the archival data will allow us to derive deeper CMDs to better search for RGB stars.

sources.summary: The paper treats I Zw 18 as the lowest-metallicity benchmark for estimating primordial helium, using its two regions and measured helium abundance to illustrate the Bayesian method.

quotes.quote: The best studied example is I Zw 18 (Pagel 1992, Skillman Kennicutt 1993, Izotov, Thuan, Lipovetsky, 1997), with an average estimated helium abundance from five independent measurements Y=0.230 0.004 (Olive, Skillman, Steigman 1997), and a metallicity 1/50th of solar. In the analysis below, we will use samples drawn from the complete sample of 62 individual regions (including two in I Zw 18) compiled by Olive, Skillman, Steigman (1997) from the data in Pagel (1992), Izotov, Thuan, Lipovetsky, (1994, 1996), and Skillman (1994, 1997). This sample represents all published Y measurements of regions with O/H 1.5 10^ 4 and N/H 1.0 10^ 5 (for comparison (O/H) _ = 8.5 10^ 4 and (N/H) _ = 1.1 10^ 4 ).

As an illustrative exercise it is useful first to consider a sample of just one galaxy, the lowest metallicity galaxy I Zw 18 where Y has been measured independently in two distinct regions, yielding an average helium abundance Recently, the smallness of the error bars associated with one of the two regions in I Zw 18 has been questioned due to underlying stellar absorption (Skillman, Terlevich Terlevich 1997). Of course, too much weight should never be given to any one single observation. Y_1,Y_2 = .230 .004 .

sources.summary: The review presents IZw18 as the lowest-metallicity ionized-gas blue compact galaxy known, then argues photometry and resolved stars indicate it is old rather than genuinely young.

quotes.quote: IZw18 The blue compact galaxy IZw18 (also known as Markarian 116) was first described by Zwicky in 1966. IZw18 is the galaxy with the lowest known metallicity as derived from the ionised gaseous component. Its oxygen abundance is only 1/50 of that of the Sun. Thus, often being referred to as ``the most metal-poor galaxy known'', it is still two orders of magnitude more metal rich than the most metal-poor stars in the Milky way.

This suggests that IZw18 could be hosted by a low surface brightness galaxy. Thus, although not foolproof, photometry (of individual stars and surface photometry) now indicates that the galaxy is in fact old, although Izotov et al. (1999a) in a last attempt to resurrect its youth, suggested that the distance of IZw18 has been severely underestimated.

sources.summary: The paper identifies variable stars in IZw18, including Classical Cepheids, and uses Cepheid-based distances consistent with the RGB tip to argue that the galaxy is older and farther away than previously thought.

quotes.quote: Variable stars have been identified for the first time in the very metal-poor Blue Compact dwarf galaxy IZw18, using deep multi-band (F606W, F814W) time-series photometry obtained with the Advanced Camera for Surveys (ACS) on board the Hubble Space Telescope (HST). We detected 34 candidate variable stars in the galaxy. We classify three of them as Classical Cepheids, with periods of 8.71, 125.0 and 130.3 days, respectively, and other two as long period variables with periodicities longer than a hundred days.

The Cepheid-based distance estimates are fully consistent within the errors with the distance modulus of 31.30 0.17 mag, obtained by aloisi07 from a totally independent distance indicator, namely, the RGB Tip of IZw18. Classical Cepheids and the RGB Tip of IZw18 both consistently indicate that the galaxy is much further away than previously thought, in turn supporting the suggestion by aloisi07 that IZw18 is at least as old as about 2 Gyrs.

sources.summary: The review highlights I Zw 18 as an extremely metal-poor BCD with little old stellar mass, disputed neutral-gas oxygen abundances, and a distinctive ionized-to-neutral gas ratio.

quotes.quote: Nevertheless, only 22 of the total stellar mass in I Zw 18 could be contributed by older stars which in itself makes this object peculiar per se (Hunt et al. \ 2003).

1 Results are shown in Fig. fig:compa1 , where the abundances of N, O, Ar, and Fe in the neutral gas of BCDs are compared to the abundances in the ionized gas of the H \ regions. The same dataset was used by Aloisi et al. \ (2003) and Lecavelier et al. \ (2004) to investigate I Zw 18 (data points (1) and (1*) respectively). Lecavelier et al. \ find O /H =-4.7^ +0.8 _ 0.6 which is consistent with the O/H ratio observed 0.6 in the H \ regions (all uncertainties are 2- ) while Aloisi et al. \ report a significantly different value with O /H =-5.4 0.3 , a discrepancy with the former result that awaits for clarification.

In I Zw 18, _ H I 0 (following the result of Lecavelier et al. \ 2004) translates into a fraction f_n of 5 . The large difference in behavior between f_n and _ H I in these 2 galaxies is due to the fact that the ratio of ionized gas mass over neutral gas mass is much larger in I Zw 18 than in Pox 36.

sources.summary: The study includes I Zw 18 as a regularly rotating dwarf, citing prior work showing a compact gas disk, steep inner rotation, flat outer rotation, and substantial baryonic mass.

quotes.quote: Nine galaxies in our sample (50 ) have a regularly rotating disk: NGC 1705, NGC 2366, NGC 4068, NGC 4214, NGC 6789, UGC 4483, I Zw 18, I Zw 36, and SBS 1415+437. For these objects, we derived rotation curves following a procedure similar to Swaters2009 . As a first step, we obtained initial estimates of the geometrical parameters and of the rotation curve by fitting a tilted-ring model to the VF Begeman1987 .

In Lelli2012a, Lelli2012b , we presented our results for two show-case galaxies: I Zw 18 and UGC 4483. For both objects, we showed that i) the gas forms a compact, rotating disk; ii) the rotation curve rises steeply in the inner parts and flattens in the outer regions; and iii) old stars and atomic gas are dynamically important, since they constitute at least 30 of the total dynamical mass within the last measured point of the rotation curve.

sources.summary: The paper uses I Zw 18 as a low-metallicity dwarf testbed, linking its nebular He II emission and dominant X-ray source to XRB ionizing photons.

quotes.quote: The low-metallicity dwarf galaxy I Zw 18 has been studied in detail. It is known to show nebular \ emission concentrated in the NW region with an integrated intensity of I(4686)/I( ) 0.02-0.04 e.g., izotov98,Kehrig2015The-Extended-He,Lebouteiller2017Neutral-gas-hea . The NW region, a giant \ region, also harbors a point-like X-ray source that dominates the observed X-ray emission of this galaxy and is suggested to be a single XRB Thuan2004Chandra-Observa,Kaaret2013A-State-Transit .

Adopting an intermediate value of q=2 10^ 10 photon/erg, we find that the observed I(4686)/I( ) intensity of I Zw 18 is well reproduced from the L_X/ SFR relation of , as shown in Fig. \ 1. This is indeed expected because I Zw 18 lies perfectly on their mean relation.

sources.summary: The paper uses I Zw 18 as a key low-metallicity He-emitting galaxy where Chandra finds a ULX in the main emitting region, supporting X-ray binaries as an ionizing source.

quotes.quote: \ in the very detailed study of the metal-poor \ emitting galaxy I Zw 18 by . Indeed in this galaxy, Chandra observations have shown the presence of a ULX and demonstrated that it is located in the main \ region (NW region), where the bulk of \ emission originates from. This strongly supports a physical connection between bright X-ray sources and nebular .

This finding, is also confirmed by a new study of , who also show that a well-motivated ULX spectral model is able to quantitatively explain the \ intensity in I Zw 18. In we have used the observed X-ray and \ line luminosities of I Zw 18 to empirically derive q = Q( He ^+)/L_X=(1.0-3.4) 10^ 10 photon/erg and adopt a ``typical'' value of q=2 10^ 10 photon/erg.

sources.summary: The paper reports marginal NOEMA CO(2-1) detection in the metal-poor blue compact dwarf IZw18, finding very low CO content despite vigorous star formation and a larger molecular reservoir than CO traces.

quotes.quote: Based on the observation with the upgraded Northern Extended Millimeter Array (NOEMA), we report a marginal detection of CO J =2-1 emission in IZw18, pushing the detection limit down to L^ _ CO(2-1) = 3.99 10 ^3 K km s ^ 1 pc ^ 2 , which is at least 40 times lower than previous studies. As one of the most metal-poor galaxies, IZw18 shows extremely low CO content despite its vigorous star formation activity. Such low CO content relative to its infrared luminosity, star formation rate, and [ C ii ] luminosity, compared with other galaxies, indicates a significant change in the ISM properties at a few percent of the Solar metallicity. In particular, the high [ C ii ] luminosity relative to CO implies a larger molecular reservoir than the CO emitter in IZw18. We also obtain an upper limit of the 1.3 mm continuum, which excludes a sub-millimetre excess in IZw18.

sources.summary: The paper describes IZw18 as two EMPG clumps with distinct velocities, complex morphokinematics, an H arc linked to IZw18NW, and no clear bulk rotation.

quotes.quote: 2 IZw18 : IZw18 has 2 main blue clumps of IZw18 Northwest (NW) and IZw18 Southeast (SE) , both of which have been confirmed to be EMPGs Izotov1998 . IZw18NW is blueshifted by 40 km s ^ 1 with respect to IZw18SE (at around their flux peaks), consistent with the H i gas kinematics Lelli2012 . We thus think that IZw18NW and IZw18SE are not in the same kinematic structure. IZw18NW shows a complex morphokinematic structure. In the H flux map, we find that the arc-like structure indicated by the gray curve (H arc; Dufour1990 ) has a velocity similar to that of IZw18NW, which indicates that the H arc and IZw18NW belong to the same kinematic structure.

sources.summary: The paper uses I Zw 18 as an extremely metal-poor dwarf prototype, noting its possible newly formed status, small physical diameter, and uncertain ancient stellar population.

quotes.quote: Our calculated abundance can be compared with those for other known XMP dwarfs: 7.17 0.04 for I Zw 18 Skillman2013 , 7.06 0.03 for the Leoncino Dwarf Aver2022 , 6.95 0.06 for AGC 124629 and 7.05 0.05 for AGC 411446 Pustilnik2020 , and 6.98 0.02 for J0811+4730 Izotov2018 .

Their prototype is the famous dwarf system I Zw 18 (= Mrk 116) with extremely low metallicity. Izotov2004 considered it as an example of a newly formed galaxy. With an angular diameter of 9 arcsec and a distance of D = 18.2 Mpc Aloisi2007 , I Zw 18 has a linear diameter about 0.80 kpc. Peekaboo, along with Leoncino and I Zw 18, are XMP galaxies with HST imaging observations that bring into question whether they contain any ancient population of significance.

sources.summary: The paper presents NICMOS evidence that IZw18 contains red supergiants and AGB stars, requiring stars older than 1 Gyr and ruling out a genuinely young galaxy.

quotes.quote: I present the first results from a HST/NICMOS imaging study of the most metal-poor blue compact dwarf galaxy IZw18. The near infrared color--magnitude diagram (CMD) is dominated by two populations, one 10-20 Myr population of red super giants and one 0.5-5 Gyr population of asymptotic giant branch stars. Stars older than 1 Gyr are required to explain the observed CMD at the adopted distance of 12.6 Mpc, showing that IZw18 is not a young galaxy. The results hold also if the distance to IZw18 is significantly larger. This rules out the possibility that IZw18 is a truly young galaxy formed recently in the local universe.

sources.summary: The paper recovers I Zw 18 in SDSS spectroscopy, confirms component oxygen abundances against previous measurements, and treats it as an extremely metal-deficient star-forming galaxy with unresolved age questions.

quotes.quote: The published oxygen abundances of the famous XMPG I Zw 18 range from 7.05 to 7.2 in the northwest (NW) component along its major axis see the Figure 5 from Izot99 , and from 7.2 to 7.3 in the southeast (SE) component. Both of these components were targeted by SDSS spectroscopy, and our measured oxygen abundances agree within the cited uncertainties with the previous results (see Table tbl:main_par ).
None of our new XMPGs is as metal-poor as I Zw 18 (which is among the XMPGs recovered in the SDSS data) or SBS 0335 052, which remain the most metal-deficient, star-forming galaxies known to-date.

Deep HST imaging of nearby BCGs (typically at distances >3 Mpc) shows that star formation has progressed for at least several Gyr e.g., Dol01,IT02 , but for more distant BCGs such as the famous I Zw 18, the age question remains unsolved compare, e.g., Ost00,Hunt03 . However, the older populations in I Zw 18 as well as in at least half of the new XMPGs of our sample should be resolvable with the Advanced Camera for Surveys aboard the Hubble Space Telescope, which should allow us to settle the question of delayed star formation soon.

sources.summary: The paper uses I Zw 18 as a comparison case where absorption-line and H II-region abundance tests are complicated by stellar-background sightlines and conflicting results.

quotes.quote: One such example may be the Blue Compact Dwarf (BCD) galaxy I Zw 18, but opinions are still divided as to whether the O/H ratio measured from interstellar absorption lines is lower than the value found in the \ regions (Kunth et al. 1994; Pettini Lipman 1995; Aloisi et al. 2003; Lecavalier des Etangs et al. 2003).

A similar test has recently been performed in I Zw 18, but with conflicting conclusions. While Aloisi et al. (2003) deduced a value of N ( )/ N ( ) which is consistent with (actually higher by a factor of 2 than) the (N/O) ratio in the \ gas, the estimate by Lecavalier des Etangs et al. (2003) is one order of magnitude lower than that of Aloisi et al. (2003).

In any case, I Zw 18 is not ideal for this test, because the interstellar absorption lines are detected against the background of OB stars within the galaxy, so that the line of sight must include ionized, as well as neutral, gas. Furthermore, there may be concerns with interpreting absorption due to the superposition of multiple sight-lines.

sources.summary: The paper models IZw18-like dwarf-galaxy evolution, arguing that gasping star formation better matches its low nitrogen-to-oxygen ratio and observed chemical properties than short bursts.

quotes.quote: We first describe the evolution of a model galaxy resembling IZw18. This object, the most metal-poor galaxy locally known, has been considered in the past as a truly ``young'' galaxy, experiencing star formation for the very first time, since old stars could not be observed. Moreover, the spectral energy distribution is well described assuming a single, recent burst of SF (Mas-Hesse Kunth 1999; Takeuchi et al. 2003).

Model IZw -- 4 exceeds the nitrogen of the most metal-poor galaxies (in particular the nitrogen of IZw18), whereas model IZw -- 3 (solid line) matches the low log (N/O). It is also worth noticing that for this model it takes 100 Myr to reach the O abundance of the most metal-poor galaxies (see Fig. 1).

sources.summary: The paper reports a Chandra X-ray source coincident with I Zw 18's optical counterpart, located on an H superbubble rim, with no stellar counterpart and no evidence for extended source structure.

quotes.quote: Chandra observations revisited In addition to five background sources in the ACIS-S3 field of view, the Chandra observations show a source coinciding with the optical counterpart of I Zw 18 (see Fig. fig:nodetect_allfig , upper left). This source is located on the rim of a central expanding H superbubble described by mar96 , north-west to the main optical body. Based on the HST /WFPC2 data shown in Fig. fig:nodetect_allfig , no stellar counterpart can be assigned to this X-ray point source. We checked whether or not the source may be slightly extended by comparing its shape with a model PSF calculated for the same off-axis angle and the same mean energy as the X-ray source itself. According to our analysis, the PSF and the X-ray source profiles are indistinguishable.

sources.summary: The review says IZw18 has a complicated but resolved CMD, RGB evidence, Cepheid-based distance work, very low metallicity, and star formation beginning several Gyr ago.

quotes.quote: The case of IZw18 left-hand panel in Fig. sbs , from To06 is more complicated, because crowding is more severe and the distance may be higher. Yet we do see in its CMD an increase in the stellar density along the red branch below I 27.5, which is most likely due to the appearance of the RGB, specially once we consider that photometric incompleteness is increasingly affecting the faintest magnitudes see also Mom05 . New HST/ACS time series observations, suitable for the derivation of Cepheids light-curves (Aloisi et al. in preparation) are providing an independent estimate of the galaxy distance and confirming the detection of the RGB. These results show that, contrary to previous claims e.g. IT04 , even the most metal-poor star forming galaxy ever discovered cannot be considered a genuinely young system and is instead active since at least a couple of Gyr. Moreover, its extremely low metallicity (Z=0.0004 for the youngest stars, presumably less for the older ones) significantly reduces the age-metallicity degeneracy on the RGB, and its colour extension therefore traces a significant age spread, suggesting that the onset of the SF activity in IZw18 has occurred several Gyrs ago.

sources.summary: The paper presents deep HST/ACS photometry showing RGB evidence in the metal-poor blue compact dwarf I Zw 18 and uses Cepheids/TRGB to revise its distance upward.

quotes.quote: We present results from new deep HST/ACS photometry of I Zw 18, the most metal-poor blue compact dwarf galaxy in the nearby universe. It has been previously argued that this is a very young system that started forming stars only 500 Myr ago, but other work has hinted that older ( 1 Gyr) red giant branch (RGB) stars may exist in this galaxy. Our deeper data indeed reveal evidence for an RGB. Underlying old ( 1 Gyr) populations are therefore present in even the most metal-poor systems, implying that star formation started at z 0.1 . The RGB tip (TRGB) magnitude and the properties of Cepheid variables identified from our program indicate that I Zw 18 is farther away ( D = 19.0 1.8 Mpc) than previously believed.

sources.summary: The review describes IZw 18 as the most metal-poor known starburst dwarf, unexpectedly showing Ly absorption; weak dust and neutral-gas resonant scattering can suppress detectable Ly emission.

quotes.quote: In a first attempt, Ly \ was observed only in absorption in the starburst dwarf galaxy IZw 18, . Since IZw 18 at Z=1/50 Z_ is the most metal--poor starburst galaxy known at present, it was considered previously a good candidate to show Ly \ in emission.

Therefore, since these galaxies have a low dust content - IZw 18 shows weak signs of reddening and its dust-to-gas ratio is at least 50 times smaller than the Galactic value, , this shows that it remains possible to weaken observationally Ly \ by simple multiple resonant scattering from the neutral gas, and even to produce an absorption feature. The H I cloud surrounding these galaxies might be leaking Ly \ photons through its external surface. The Ly \ line would then become very hard to detect because of its low surface brightness.

sources.summary: The paper uses IZw18 as a structural template for dwarf-galaxy simulations and notes that matching its observed abundance ratios requires yields from Meynet and Maeder rather than Renzini and Voli.

quotes.quote: In two previous papers, we have studied the dynamical and chemical evolution of model galaxies similar to IZw18 (Recchi et al. , hereafter Paper I) and NGC1569 (Recchi et al. , hereafter Paper II).

Model parameters The initial configurations of our models are aimed at reproducing the main structural parameters of two very well studied gas-rich dwarf galaxies: IZw18 and NGC1569. The initial setup is taken from our previous models and described in RMD (for IZw18) and in Paper II (for NGC1569).

However, as we have pointed out in Paper I, the Renzini Voli ( ) yields tend to overestimate the nitrogen production and only the assumption of the Meynet Maeder ( ) set of yields can reconcile the prediction of the models with the observed abundance ratios in IZw18.

sources.summary: The paper contrasts I Zw 18 with SBS 0335--052E, describing its similar low metallicity but passive-mode star formation and little or no WISE mid-infrared color excess.

quotes.quote: This longward rising of MIR emission implies the presence of hot (up to 1000K) dust emission. The properties of SBS 0335--052E in the MIR range are very different from that in another extremely metal-deficient BCD with similar metallicity, I Zw 18.

Stellar (with an effective temperature 3000K) and interstellar ionised gas emission is characterised by W1-W2 colours of 0 - 0.4 mag. In particular, the W1-W2 0.5 mag of I Zw 18, one of the most metal-deficient BCDs, is consistent with that of stellar and ionised gas emission only.

sources.summary: The paper identifies I Zw 18 as extremely low-metallicity and dust-deficient, with weak FIR cooling-line contributions and soft X-ray or non-photoelectric heating affecting SFR-line calibrations.

quotes.quote: The weak trends for [ O i ] _ 63 and [ O iii ] _ 88 are mainly driven by two galaxies of extreme low metal abundance, I Zw 18 and SBS 0335-052, in which the [ O i ] _ 63 and [ O iii ] _ 88 lines do not seem to add significantly to the overall gas cooling. Since the SFR is unlikely to be overestimated for these galaxies based on FUV +MIPS 24 m (see comparison with other SFR tracers in Section DGScompare of the Appendix), the offset of these extremely dust deficient galaxies might suggest that other lines dominate the cooling processes (e.g. Ly ). The gas heating might, alternatively, be dominated by heating mechanisms other than the photoelectric effect (e.g. soft X-ray heating, mechanical heating, cosmic rays), which could disperse the link between the emission of cooling lines and the SFR. For I Zw 18, the heating has indeed been shown to be dominated by soft X-ray heating ( 2008A&A...478..371P and Lebouteiller et al. in prep.), which is likely to also affect the SFR- L_ line relations.

sources.summary: The paper treats I Zw 18 as a heavily disturbed system with an updated H I map and interprets it as an interaction or merger between gas-rich dwarfs.

quotes.quote: Several galaxies have heavily disturbed distributions, characterized by large-scale asymmetries, long filaments, and/or a large optical- offset (NGC 4449, I Zw 18, NGC 1705, UGC 6541, NGC 1569, and I Zw 36).

I Zw 18 has been studied in detail by Lelli2012a . The total map presented here is slightly different from that in Lelli2012a because it was constructed using a mask at 60 '' resolution (instead of 45 '' resolution) for consistency with the other galaxies. The most likely interpretation of this system is an interaction/merger between two (or more) gas-rich dwarfs.

sources.summary: The paper models I Zw 18 as an extremely metal-poor blue compact dwarf, using Herschel lines and dust constraints to assess X-ray heating, molecular gas, and star-formation implications.

quotes.quote: Our objective is to identify the dominant thermal mechanisms in one such galaxy, \ ( 1/30 Z _ ), assess the diagnostic value of fine-structure cooling lines, and estimate the molecular gas content.

Building on a previous photoionization model describing the giant H \ region of NW within a multi-sector topology, we provide additional constraints using, in particular, the [ C ] 157 \ and [ O ] 63 \ lines and the dust mass recently measured with the Herschel Space Telescope. The heating of the H \ region appears to be mainly due to photoionization by radiation from a bright X-ray binary source, while the photoelectric effect is negligible.
Inasmuch as X-ray heating dominates in the H \ gas, the infrared fine-structure lines provide an average X-ray luminosity of order 4 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 over the last few 10^4 years in the galaxy.

sources.summary: The white paper uses I Zw 18 as a blue compact dwarf example where far-UV abundance studies debate oxygen discrepancies between ionized and neutral gas.

quotes.quote: The comparison led to a still ongoing debate, which is well illustrated by several studies of the BCD I Zw 18 ( 18 Mpc, 2 solar metallicity). Early observations with HST/GHRS showed a discrepancy between the oxygen abundance measured in emission and in absorption, leading to the hypothesis of self-enrichment by the current starburst episode Kunth1986a,Kunth1994a but, due to a limited sensitivity, only strong lines were accessible and hidden saturation could not be identified easily Pettini1995a . Later studies of the same galaxy with FUSE, which enabled the observation of several metal lines and of hydrogen, highlighted issues regarding the stellar continuum and the selection of weak lines Aloisi2003a and showed that only a small discrepancy may exist, if any Lecavelierdesetangs2004a . More recently, confirmed, using HST/COS and weak lines such as 1254 S , that a small discrepancy does exist in I Zw 18.

sources.summary: The CLASSY notes identify J0934+5514 with low-metallicity dwarf I Zw 18, mention its NW and SE components, and say the coadd samples the NW region.

quotes.quote: J0934+5514 : This is the famous low-metallicity dwarf galaxy, I Zw 18, which is also known as Mrk 116 or UGCA 116. I Zw 18 has two main components that have been heavily studied: the NW and SE components. The CLASSY coadded spectrum only contains light from the NW region. It also combines data from COS FUV detector lifetime positions 1 and 4 and data taken at position angles that are offset by 67 ^ .

sources.summary: The paper reexamines photoionization models for I Zw 18, describing its low metallicity, NW knot modeling, and the need for additional heating beyond photoionization.

quotes.quote: In particular, models for the well-known HII galaxy, I Zw 18, are discussed, and possible explanations for the discrepancy between model results and observational data are suggested. Due to its low metalicity, I Zw 18 is one of the extragalactic HII regions used to derive the primordial He abundance. Recently, Stasinska Schaerer (1999, hereinafter SS99) presented 1-D models for the NW knot of I Zw 18. They concluded that in addition to photoionization, another heating mechanism is necessary to explain all observed features.

sources.summary: The paper uses I Zw 18 as the passive, very metal-poor blue compact dwarf case, emphasizing its diffuse star-forming regions, moderate extinction, and radio SED behavior.

quotes.quote: They argue that the star-formation modes in the two most metal-poor galaxies, SBS 0335 052 and I Zw 18 , are very different, in spite of their similar metallicities ( 12+ ( O/H )=7.3 and 12+ ( O/H )=7.2 , respectively; Skillman Kennicutt ; Izotov et al. \ ). On the contrary, the star-forming regions in \ are diffuse ( r_ SF 100 pc, n 100 cm ^ 3 ), and contain no SSCs. NIR H _2 emission has not been detected (Hunt et al., private communication), and the dust extinction is moderate ( A_V 0.2 mag; Cannon et al. \ ). We consider a region with such properties as ``passive'' following Hunt et al. \ ( ), while a like star-forming property is considered ``active''.

sources.summary: IZw18 is treated as a blue compact dwarf hosting three ultra-low-metallicity Cepheids; Cepheid and TRGB distances ruled out recent primordial formation and made it a benchmark for low-metallicity pulsation models.

quotes.quote: In the context of the HST ACS GO program 10586 (PI: A. Aloisi) we have identified three Classical Cepheids in the Blue Compact Dwarf galaxy IZw18, having respectively periods of 8.71, 125 and 130.3 days. A new estimate of the distance, and, in turn, of the age of the galaxy stellar populations were obtained both from the Classical Cepheids and from the galaxy's TRGB, which allowed us to rule out the possibility that IZw18 is a truly primordial galaxy of recent formation in the local universe a07 . At the same time this project also provided a first sample of ultra-low metallicity ( 1/50 Z_ ) Classical Cepheids f10 , thus representing an important benchmark for the pulsation models.

sources.summary: The paper presents I Zw 18 as a low-redshift starburst analogue with very low metallicity and little dust, used to calibrate low dust-yield models.

quotes.quote: It is believed that the local starburst galaxy I Zw 18, with a metallicity Z=0.04 Z_ , is a good analogue at low redshift. I Zw 18 is also essentially dust free, with an estimated dust mass of 450-1800 M_ and if Himiko has a similar dust-to-gas mass ratio, it would have a dust mass which falls two orders of magnitude below local SBGs of similar stellar mass Fisher14 .
With the stellar dust yield taken to be just y_ d = 10^ 5 (to match the low M_ d /M_ stars of I Zw 18), almost all of the dust mass has to be due to dust condensation in the ISM.

sources.summary: The paper describes 1 Zw 18 as a vigorously star-forming, very low-metallicity starburst galaxy with weak C III] emission despite its high ionization parameter.

quotes.quote: Perhaps more surprising is the fact that the vigorously star-forming, very low metallicity starburst galaxy 1 Zw 18 is not a strong C III ] emitter; its equivalent width in three different regions varies from 1.3 to 4.4 . Its ionization parameter is high for the local universe ( U 2.5 from Dufour:1988eq ; inferred as 2.2 as the lowest--metallicity object in MoralesLuis:2014bj ). Any hypothesis that explains strong C III ] emission will need to explain why 1 Zw 18 shows relatively weak C III ] emission.
