sources.summary: The paper presents IZw18 as the archetype for young-galaxy selection, but argues its uniform H I/H II metallicity and surface photometry imply earlier star formation.

quotes.quote: The selection criteria has, on purpose (since the common belief has been that a young galaxy should look similar to IZw18), picked out actively star forming galaxies, and will miss dormant post burst objects where N/O may have decreased.

In IZw18 both H i and H ii metallicity is now known to be similar and show no gradient indicating that dispersion and mixing must have operated after one or several star formation episodes, excluding the possibility that the observed metals result from the present burst (van Zee et al., 1998; Legrand et al., 2000, Legrand, 2000).

Similarly, for IZw18, the surface photometry consistently gives a lower limit of 1 Gyr ( Ostlin, this conference) in agreement with the results from CMD analysis (Aloisi et al. 1999; Ostlin 2000), although there may be a serious contribution from ionised gas in the halo (Izotov 2000, private correspondance).

gmw_00000304.name: NGC 474
gmw_00000304.overview: NGC 474 is a lenticular galaxy [arXiv:2106.12164] located in a poor group [arXiv:astro-ph/0606385] that hosts a rich system of tidal shells and streams [arXiv:2111.14886]. It is tidally interacting with the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 470 via an H I gas bridge [arXiv:1204.3879, arXiv:astro-ph/0703588].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 474's prominent outer shell, finding intermediate metallicity, associated planetary nebulae and globular cluster candidates, and possible links to a minor merger and young central stellar population.

quotes.quote: We present MUSE@VLT data of the most prominent outer shell of NGC 474, to constrain its formation history. The stellar shell spectrum is clearly detected, with a signal-to-noise ratio of around 65 pix ^ 1 . We use a full spectral fitting method to determine the line-of-sight velocity and the age and metallicity of the shell and associated point-like sources within the MUSE field of view. We detect six globular cluster (GC) candidates and eight planetary nebula (PN) candidates which are all kinematically associated to the stellar shell.

We show that the shell has an intermediate metallicity, [ M/H ] = 0.83^ +0.12 _ 0.12 and a possible enrichment, [ /Fe ] 0.3. Assuming the material of the shell comes from a lower mass companion, and that the latter had no initial metallicity gradient, such a stellar metallicity would constrain the mass of the progenitor to be around 7.4 10^8 M _ , implying a merger mass ratio of about 1:100.

We show that at least two globular cluster candidates possibly associated with the shell are quite young, with ages below 1.5 Gyr. We also note the presence of a young ( 1 Gyr) stellar population in the center of NGC 474.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 474 as a gas-poor shell S0 interacting with NGC 470, with low X-ray luminosity and shells likely produced by multiple accretion events.

quotes.quote: NGC 474, also known as Arp 227, is interacting with NGC 470, a spiral galaxy at 5.4 \ to the west. The two galaxies have a small (virtually null) velocity difference. The surface photometry of Shombert87 indicates that NGC 474 is a S0 galaxy with a smooth and undistorted inner luminosity profile. Turnbull99 suggests that the colors of the inner shells follow that of the galaxy while the outer shells appear to be bluer.

Using ACS-HST, these latter authors suggest that the morphology of the shell system is probably due to multiple accretion events. NGC 474 is a very gas-poor system. It has not been detected in H and also the hot gas component is poor: it's X-ray emission is very low, and its luminosity, L _x 10^ 39 , is consistent with the low end of the expected emission from discrete sources Rampazzo06 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 474 as a multi-component fast rotator, finding low central velocities, disk-like components separated by a position-angle shift, and an unresolved inner disk.

quotes.quote: Two such examples of these, NGC 474 and NGC 661, are identified in Figure Fig:KinematicsSetGala , which shows radial profiles of k_1 , PA _ kin and k_5 / k_1 as well as 2D kinematic maps for these two galaxies. Very likely these rapid drops in velocity and _R point to small rotating components that are unresolved by our observations. In particular, our observations of NGC 474 and NGC 5982 show low velocities in their central fibers whereas higher resolution SAURON kinematics identify KDC's rotating at 50 \ within 1 kpc.

Another four of the MC FRs are typified by NGC 474, shown in the bottom panel of Figure Fig:KinematicsSetGala . These relatively slowly rotating FRs contain two distinct disk-like components, in the case of NGC 474 separated by a PA shift of about 15^ at 2 kpc. The transition is also marked by a clear hump in k_5 and a minimum of k_1 . SAURON kinematics of NGC 474 also show an additional inner disk-like component at 0.8 kpc Krajnovic2008 that we do not detect, empirically determining that structures with sizes < 7 \ are not discernible in our data.

sources.summary: NGC 474 is treated as a type II shell galaxy in a poor group, with tidal interaction signs, strong dust lanes, peculiar gas/dust kinematics, and evidence favoring externally supplied dust.

quotes.quote: NGC 474 The low v/ ratio (this defines the amount of rotation), of 0.18 ramp is consistent with NGC 474 being either a near face-on disk, or a near spherical galaxy. NGC 474 is part of a small evolving poor group ramp .
In H I , there are signs of tidal interactions with the nearby (at 5.5') regular spiral galaxy NGC 470.

Sarzi et al. report a misalignment of the stars and ionised gas by 74 16 degrees. Comparing the position angle of the dust lanes with the velocity maps of Sarzi et al. show that the dust is aligned with the velocity field of the ionised gas and not with the stars. This probably implies a connection between the dust and the gas and is seen in many other early type galaxies goud2,ferrari,sarzi . The velocity structure of the gas, the dust morphology of NGC 474 (many dust lanes residing on top of the supposed bulge) and the large offset between stars and gas+dust disturb the picture of this galaxy being an S0, and point towards an external origin for the dust.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 474 as a shell galaxy with especially prominent shells, strong interaction signatures, low-end X-ray emission, and UV morphology tracing bulge and central emission.

quotes.quote: We present Galaxy Evolution Explorer ( GALEX ), XMM- Newton Optical Monitor (OM) and XMM-EPIC observations of NGC 474, NGC 7070A, and ESO 2400100, three shell galaxies in the Malin83 catalogue. XMM- Newton observations of NGC 474 are fully discussed in Rampazzo06 Beside the presence of shells, clearly visible in all three, although more spectacular in NGC 474, these galaxies share other properties: they are in low density environments and are interacting systems. However, the details of the interaction are quite different: companions are well separated in NGC 7070A Ramella96 and strongly interacting in NGC 474 as shown by H I observations see e.g. Rampazzo06 .

Rampazzo06 showed that NGC 474 is at the bottom of the X-ray emission distribution of E galaxies: its X-ray emission is consistent with the low end of the expected emission from discrete sources. GALEX data of NGC 474 (Figure fig2 ) show that the NUV emission extends to the galaxy bulge, while the FUV emission shows up only in the central regions of the galaxy. In the NGC 474 image obtained in the UVW1 and UVM2 filters outer shells are visible as in the optical image Rampazzo06 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 474 as Arp 227, interacting with NGC 470 through an H I bridge, with prominent shells, a comma-like structure, possible bar/lens, and infrared residual features.

quotes.quote: NGC 474: This galaxy is also known as Arp 227 ( arp66 ), and is interacting with NGC 470, which is 5.5 ( 50 kpc) to the West. An HI gas bridge connects NGC 474 and NGC 470 ( rampazzo06 ). NGC 474 is famous for its shell structures ( malin83 ; turnbull99 ) and the misalignment between the kinematic and photometric major axis ( hau96 ).

About 3.5 to the South-west of the galaxy center, there is a ``comma-like'' structure ( rampazzo06 ), which is denoted with a letter `a' in Figure ngc0474_2634 . In the 4g data, shells in NGC 474 are clearly visible in the unsharp masked image and in the residual image (Figure ngc0474set6 ) from the GALFIT model. There is also a possible bar feature (PA 33 from North to East), which shows up in the residual image after a plus the exponential disk model has been subtracted (Figure ngc0474_2634 ).

sources.summary: The paper selects NGC 0474 as a bright core-less early-type galaxy that may be developing a depleted core while retaining prominent fine structure.

quotes.quote: We therefore applied this magnitude cut to the core- less galaxies with a measured (or T_c ), resulting in the selection of NGC 0474 and NGC 4125 We excluded NGC 5982 for coherency, being one of the few objects for which richings did not report a distance, although the distance retrieved from NED technically makes this object part of the selection. . Such a small number of matching objects (2 out of the parent 41) was indeed expected since observing a galaxy soon after its formation is a statistically rare occurrence: the vast majority core- less objects are ``pure'' galaxies. These two objects appear in panel B-1, where their mass deficit has been set to an arbitrarily small value. Under the assumption that NGC 0474 and NGC 4152 are in-the-making core galaxies, their location in the plot is consistent with our evolutionary scenario.

Remarkably, galaxies which will eventually develop a core (see NGC 4125 and NGC 0474 in panel B-1 ) consistently present indication of prominent fine structure. This strongly suggests that galaxy relaxation and core excavation are indeed happening in parallel.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 474 as a massive lenticular shell galaxy hosting SN 2017fgc, with metal-rich outskirts and evidence for a past merger.

quotes.quote: SN 2017fgc is a fast-expanding SN Ia that exploded in the nearby shell galaxy NGC 474 at a distance of 29.51 2.09 Mpc Cappellari2011413 . Note that the host is a lenticular galaxy with prominent gas shell and bridge structures at its outskirts, suggestive of the merging process Lim2017835,Alabi2020497,Fensch2020644 .

Using this relation, a supersolar oxygen abundance of 9.08 0.12 can be derived for NGC 474. From Figure fgcHost , one can see that SN 2017fgc is consistent with the finding that HV SNe Ia tend to occur in more massive and metal-rich host galaxies. Also, the spectral survey for NGC 474 by Alabi2020497 shows that this galaxy has a slightly more metal-rich outer shell compared to the galaxy center. Recently, the detailed study by Fensch2020644 showed that NGC 474 merged with a young metal-rich galaxy during its evolution.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 474's bright tidal shells as products of a spiral accretion event, using shell radii and simulations to infer two collisions and one merger-driven origin for the tidal features.

quotes.quote: The lenticular galaxy NGC 474 hosts a rich system of tidal shells and streams, some of which are exceptionally bright.

Here, we add further clues on their formation from the radii of the shells, and we present a scenario for the formation of the tidal features that seems to be unique and can explain all available data. Shell radii were analyzed with the shell identification method, and we ran self-consistent simulations of the formation of the tidal features. We considered Newtonian as well as MOND gravity. Observations suggest that the tidal features originate from the accretion of a spiral galaxy.
According to the shell identification method, the merging galaxies first collided 1.3 Gyr ago and then again 0.9 Gyr ago, thereby forming the shells in two generations.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 474 as a shell early-type galaxy whose globular-cluster system has an unusually weak red population, possibly tied to fewer early mergers and significant rotation.

quotes.quote: NGC 474 - brightest galaxy of LGG20 (4 members). The galaxy is connected with the small spiral NGC 470 via a HI tidal bridge ( ). Often classified as S0; shows some rotation ( ). May be a LINER.

The colour distributions of NGC 474 and NGC 1344 (Figure VIplot ) are similar, and appear either to be unimodal, or to have a very low red (metal-rich) component. Although KMM returns a combination of a blue and red peak as a good description of the distribution, there are very few GCs in the red peak.

sources.summary: The MATLAS paper highlights NGC 474 as a spectacular shell galaxy where deep MegaCam imaging reveals outer shells, radial structures, and shell color variations.

quotes.quote: Several of the galaxies in our sample, in particularly the truly spectacular galaxy NGC 474, were already known to exhibit shells Turnbull99 . Indeed, having not a particularly low surface brightness, they were visible in previous generations of imaging surveys. Our deep imaging survey reveals new shells, located in the outermost regions, as well as so far unknown radial linear structures which may have formed at the same time. These structures might provide new constraints for numerical models. The MegaCam images also disclose a variety of colours from one shell to the other -- see in particular NGC 474 --, which should be taken into account when modeling the collision.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 474 as a prototypical shell galaxy, using simulations to interpret its shell morphology, young outer-shell clusters, nuclear starburst scenario, and post-merger quenching.

quotes.quote: Due to its numerous prominent shells, NGC 474 is a prototypical system of a shell galaxy, often put in the spotlight when studying the formation of shells and shell galaxies.
The recent simulation of Bilek2022 confirms that a shell morphology similar to that of NGC 474 could be explained by a radial interaction.

If our formation scenario applies to NGC 474, it suggests that the young massive star clusters detected in the outer shell of NGC 474 by Fensch2020 do not have an in situ origin. Fensch2020 proposes a formation scenario where the merger event responsible for the formation of the shells, also triggered the formation of massive star clusters via a nuclear starburst. Similarities in age and metallicity with the relatively young stellar population in the nucleus of NGC 474, indicates that this could indeed be the birth place of the young massive clusters.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 474 as a green-valley elliptical undergoing a likely recent disk merger, with bluish merger shells and an anomalously large envelope.

quotes.quote: NGC 474 is an elliptical galaxy in the green valley, but it is involved with a significant (likely recent disk) merger event. The merger shells are bluish on false colour images (see e.g. Duc15 ); this galaxy will likely migrate to the red sequence after the merger event settles. NGC 5746 shows one of the largest envelopes found for a galaxy in the blue cloud.
NGC 474, NGC 772, and NGC 5746 are anomalous in their hosting of large envelopes yet not residing on the red sequence.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 474's center and shell spectroscopy, finding different stellar populations and favoring a major-to-intermediate merger origin for its shell system.

quotes.quote: We present new spectra obtained using Keck/KCWI and perform kinematics and stellar population analyses of the shell galaxy NGC 474, from both the galaxy centre and a region from the outer shell. We show that both regions have similarly extended star formation histories although with different stellar population properties. The central region of NGC 474 is dominated by intermediate-aged stars ( 8.3 0.3 Gyr) with subsolar metallicity ( [ Z /H ] =-0.24 0.07 dex) while the observed shell region, which hosts a substantial population of younger stars, has a mean luminosity-weighted age of 4.0 0.5 Gyr with solar metallicities ( [ Z /H ] = 0.03 0.09 dex). Our results are consistent with a scenario in which NGC 474 experienced a major to intermediate merger with a log (M_*/ ) 10 mass satellite galaxy at least 2 Gyr ago which produced its shell system.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 474 as a shell-bearing lenticular galaxy whose arcs may come from interaction or merger, with old central stars and uncertain LINER/AGN status.

quotes.quote: NGC 474 (Arp 227) is a lenticular galaxy surrounded by arcs or shells of stars Turnbull99 that were possibly formed in an interaction with neighboring NGC 470, or in a past merger event. The central regions of the galaxy are old ( 9 Gyr), while the age of the stellar population decreases towards the galaxy's outskirts Kuntschner10 . The classification of NGC 474 as a type 2 LINER is highly uncertain, and its optical emission lines are weak Ho97 .

gmw_00000305.name: NGC 7070A
gmw_00000305.overview: NGC 7070A is a shell galaxy located in a low-density interacting system with well-separated companions [arXiv:0801.2327]. It features a dust lane likely formed through external accretion [arXiv:0807.0741, arXiv:0801.2327], and its emission is dominated by a low-luminosity nuclear source [arXiv:0807.0741, arXiv:0801.2327].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 7070A as a shell galaxy with decoupled gas, a dust lane likely from external accretion, and X-ray emission dominated by a low-luminosity nucleus.

quotes.quote: A low luminosity nuclear source is the dominant component in NGC 7070A (log L _X =41.7 erg s ^ 1 in the 2-10 keV band).

Rampazzo03 showed indeed that the ionized gas distribution in NGC 7070A is elongated and has an asymmetric structure relative to the stellar body. The nucleus of NGC 7070A is a faint radio emitter at 4.885 GHz Sadler89 . No \ is detected down to the level of M 5 10^7 M _ using the Australia Telescope Compact Array Oosterloo02 .

There is strong evidence that the current interaction is not responsible for the origin of the shell system in either NGC 474 or NGC 7070A, at variance with ESO 2400100. Pierfederici04 have shown that the radial distribution of the shell surface brightness in NGC 474 is inconsistent with the weak interaction hypothesis. There is also kinematical evidence that the strong dust--lane which crosses the entire body of NGC 7070A, with an orientation intermediate between the optical major and minor axes, is the result of external accretion Sharples83 , which could be related with the formation of the shell system.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7070A as a shell galaxy in a low-density interacting system, with separated companions, compact X-ray emission, dominant nuclear emission, and UV extension similar to optical light.

quotes.quote: We present Galaxy Evolution Explorer ( GALEX ), XMM- Newton Optical Monitor (OM) and XMM-EPIC observations of NGC 474, NGC 7070A, and ESO 2400100, three shell galaxies in the Malin83 catalogue. However, the details of the interaction are quite different: companions are well separated in NGC 7070A Ramella96 and strongly interacting in NGC 474 as shown by H I observations see e.g. Rampazzo06 . Furthermore, dust is detected in NGC 474 and NGC 7070A, although only for the latter, there is kinematic evidence of an external acquisition.

In NGC 7070A the emission is rather compact both in the hard and soft bands. Spectral results are in agreement with the spatial picture: the nuclear source of NGC 7070A is the dominant component while in ESO 2400100 the emission is indicative of a low temperature plasma. Both ESO 2400100 (Figure fig3 ) and NGC 7070A have extension similar to that of the optical image in the OM-UVW1 and in the GALEX NUV filter.

gmw_00000306.name: ESO 2400100
gmw_00000306.overview: ESO 2400100 is a shell galaxy containing two interacting nuclei embedded within its envelope [arXiv:0801.2327, arXiv:0807.0741]. The northern nucleus is bluer than the southern one [arXiv:0801.2327, arXiv:0807.0741], likely due to a very recent star formation event [arXiv:0807.0741].

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 2400100 as a shell galaxy with low-temperature X-ray plasma, a blue double nucleus, recent northern star formation, and ongoing interaction/accretion.

quotes.quote: In ESO 2400100, the X-ray emission is due to a low temperature plasma with a contribution from the collective emission of individual sources. In the Optical Monitor image ESO 2400100 shows a double nucleus, one bluer than the other. This probably results from a very recent star formation event in the northern nuclear region.
The presence of the double nucleus, corroborated by the (UV - optical) colors and line strength indices analysis, suggests that ESO 2400100 is accreting a faint companion.

ESO 2400100 Longhetti98b found two distinct nuclei separated by 5'' and with systemic velocities different by 200 km s ^ 1 . Rampazzo03 show that the ionized gas and the stars are completely decoupled. Simulation of inter-penetrating encounters by Combes95 and references therein show that the U-shape and the correlated inverted U-shape velocity profile along the line connecting the two galaxy nuclei is a bona fide signature of the ongoing interaction. Since the gas 2D morphology, the stellar and the gas kinematics suggest that the two nuclei are still interacting, if the development of the shells is connected with these interactions, their formation process is still on-going.

sources.summary: The paper treats ESO 2400100 as a shell galaxy whose envelope contains two interacting nuclei, with extended soft X-ray emission and UV evidence that the nuclei have different stellar populations.

quotes.quote: We present Galaxy Evolution Explorer ( GALEX ), XMM- Newton Optical Monitor (OM) and XMM-EPIC observations of NGC 474, NGC 7070A, and ESO 2400100, three shell galaxies in the Malin83 catalogue. Two nuclei are embedded and interacting within the ESO 2400100 envelope Longhetti98b . In ESO 2400100 the soft band emission is significantly stronger than the hard one and extends further than in NGC 7070A.

Spectral results are in agreement with the spatial picture: the nuclear source of NGC 7070A is the dominant component while in ESO 2400100 the emission is indicative of a low temperature plasma. Both ESO 2400100 (Figure fig3 ) and NGC 7070A have extension similar to that of the optical image in the OM-UVW1 and in the GALEX NUV filter. In Figure fig3 (mid and left panels) the UVW1 and UVM2 images show the two nuclei embedded in ESO 2400100. The northern nucleus is significantly bluer than the southern one.

gmw_00000307.name: Haro 15
gmw_00000307.overview: Haro 15 is a luminous compact blue, Wolf-Rayet starburst galaxy [arXiv:1103.0526] that is also classified as a blue compact dwarf [arXiv:0912.2781, arXiv:1011.5991]. It exhibits a merger-like morphology with two separated nuclei [arXiv:1103.0526] and contains multiple luminous, star-forming knots with complex ionized-gas kinematics [arXiv:0912.2781, arXiv:1011.5991].

sources.summary: The paper describes Haro 15 as a luminous compact blue, Wolf-Rayet starburst galaxy with clumpy star formation, merger-like morphology, and complex ionized-gas kinematics.

quotes.quote: Already in the original list of blue galaxies with emission lines by Haro (1956) who described this object as ``minute cometary nebula'', \ has later been included in several compilations of Blue Compact Galaxies (BCG). Several studied at all frequencies have been carried out over this object. In particular, the optical spectroscopy was analysed by ; ; ; , between others. Owing to the detection of the He ii 4686 emission line by , has classified this galaxy as a Wolf-Rayet (WR) galaxy. also found a blue WR bump supporting the WR nature of .

At a distance of 86.6 Mpc RC3.9 , Haro 15 meets the criteria for a Luminous Compact Blue Galaxy (LCBG) 2004AJ....128.1541H despite the fact of its brightness distribution showing an exponential profile with a scale length of 1.37 kpc. The nature of this type of galaxies is not yet clear since they probably constitute a mixed population of starburst galaxies. Some authors suggest that LCBGs might represent the final outcome of a merger between a dwarf elliptical and a gas rich dwarf galaxy or \ cloud Ostlin98,2008A&A...479..725C . The interactions taking place during the merging process would act as the starburst trigger see e.g. 2004A&A...428..425L,2006A&A...449..997L . The images of \ showing two separated nuclei surrounded by a more regular, roughly elliptical envelope with twisted isophotes, together with different features and faint extensions as reported by give support to this picture. Other galaxy properties like different kinematics and chemical abundances between regions A and B may also indicate that is probably experiencing a minor merger 2010A&A...521A..63L .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Haro 15 as a blue compact dwarf with multiple luminous knots, deriving nebular conditions, chemical abundances, and regional oxygen-abundance differences from long-slit and echelle spectra.

quotes.quote: We present a detailed study of the physical properties of the nebular material in multiple knots of the blue compact dwarf galaxy Haro 15. Using long slit and echelle spectroscopy, obtained at Las Campanas Observatory, we study the physical conditions (electron density and temperature), ionic and total chemical abundances of several atoms, reddening and ionization structure. The latter was derived by comparing the oxygen and sulphur ionic ratios to their corresponding observed emission line ratios (the and ' plots) in different regions of the galaxy.

We obtained high dispersion (Echelle Spectrograph; = 0.148 \ px ^ 1 at 5400 equivalent to 6.72 kms ^ 1 px ^ 1 ) and long-slit low resolution spectra (WFCCD; = 4.2 \ px ^ 1 at 5400 ) of five luminous knots in the BCD galaxy Haro 15 ( Fig. fig ) at the du Pont Telescope. We notice a difference in the O/H ratio between knots A and B. Our results support these differences between the two regions.

sources.summary: The paper studies Haro 15 as a blue compact dwarf galaxy, measuring ionized-gas kinematics, physical conditions, abundances, and complex star-forming knot structure from spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: Abstract We present a detailed study of the kinematic and physical properties of the ionized gas in multiple knots of the blue compact dwarf galaxy Haro 15. Using echelle and long slit spectroscopy data, obtained with different instruments at Las Campanas Observatory, we study the internal kinematic and physical conditions (electron density and temperature), ionic and total chemical abundances of several atoms, reddening and ionization structure. Applying direct and empirical methods for abundance determination, we perform a comparative analysis between these regions and in their different components.

On the other hand, our echelle spectra show complex kinematics in several conspicuous knots within the galaxy. To perform an in-depth 2D spectroscopic study we complete this work with high spatial and spectral resolution spectroscopy using the Integral Field Unit mode on the Gemini Multi-Object Spectrograph instrument at the Gemini South telescope. With these data we are able to resolve the complex kinematical structure within star forming knots in Haro 15 galaxy.

gmw_00000308.name: NGC 3256
gmw_00000308.overview: NGC 3256 is a nearby luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1708.07269] and a late-stage merger of two gas-rich spiral galaxies [arXiv:1104.1177]. The system features two distinct nuclei [arXiv:0904.4816], extended tidal tails [arXiv:0904.4816], and molecular outflows driven by a central starburst [arXiv:2010.05072].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3256 as a luminous nearby merger with tidal tails and dual nuclei, and reports water maser emission split into two star-formation-associated hot spots.

quotes.quote: NGC 3256 is a well studied merging galaxy located at a distance of 37 Mpc ( 1'' 180 pc ; H_ 0 = 75 km s^ 1 Mpc^ 1 ). With an IR luminosity of L_ IR 6 10^ 11 (Lira et al., ), it is one of the most luminous galaxies in the nearby Universe. The galaxy consists of a main body, two extended tidal tails with a total extension of 80 kpc , and two faint external loops (L pari, ). The double tidal tails are characteristic of an interaction between two spiral galaxies of comparable mass (e.g., de Vaucouleurs de Vaucouleurs, ). Two distinct nuclei aligned along a north-south axis have been revealed by high spatial resolution near-IR (e.g. Kotilainen et al., ), MIR (B o ker et al., ), and radio observations (e.g. Norris Forbes, , hereafter NF95; Neff et al., ).

In the ATCA observation, about a third of the total emission is seen to originate from two separate maser spots: a northern one (labelled N) is located at _ 2000 =10^ h 27^ m 51^ s .87 0^ s .01 and _ 2000 =-43^ 54'12'' .4 0'' .1 , and a southern one (labelled S) at _ 2000 =10^ h 27^ m 51^ s .74 0^ s .01 and _ 2000 =-43^ 54'19'' .2 0'' .1 (Fig. ngc3256cont ). Gaussian fits provide peak velocities of 2822 km s^ 1 and 2840 km s^ 1 , peak flux densities of 3.5 mJy and 2.1 mJy , and linewidths of 8 km s^ 1 and 16 km s^ 1 for the northern and southern maser, respectively. Hence both features are redshifted with respect to the systemic velocity, by 23 km s^ 1 (N) and 41 km s^ 1 (S).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3256 as a late-stage LIRG merger whose tidal tails, obscured nucleus, starburst-driven wind, and line-ratio maps reveal widespread shock excitation.

quotes.quote: NGC 3256: NGC 3256 (IRAS designation IRASF10257-4339) is a late-stage merger with nuclei separated by approximately 1 kpc, though it has not yet formed a single nucleus and therefore cannot be classified as a post-merger. Sakamoto06 note that the long tidal tails seen in optical and HI images deVaucouleurs61,English03b suggest ``a prograde-prograde merger of two gas-rich spiral galaxies of similar size'' Toomre72,White79 . The bright face-on galaxy seen in figure fig1 has a separated, heavily obscured secondary nucleus seen in x-ray, IR and radio lying 5 \ south of the bright optical nucleus Boeker97,Neff03,Sakamoto06 .

This supernova activity in the core of NGC 3256 is also driving a galactic wind measured in neutral absorption and ionized emission previously associated with shock heating Armus89,Heckman90,Heckman00,Lipari00 . NGC 3256 lies closer than IC 1623 at z 0.0094 , which means that our data probe a finer spatial scale of 1 185 pc.

NGC 3256: The line ratio maps of NGC 3256 show similar behavior compared to IC 1623. Again the areas of unobscured star formation correspond to lower line ratios, tracing the ongoing star formation as the dominant source of ionizing photons. A key difference is that NGC 3256 shows a region of increasing line ratios in a small central part of the region of low line ratios.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3256 as a luminous nearby merger dominated by a powerful starburst, with X-ray spectra showing ionized iron emission likely tied to supernova activity.

quotes.quote: NGC 3256 It is the most luminous nearby (z < 0.01) merger system. Its energy output is dominated by a powerful starburst. Previous ASCA , Chandra and \ X-ray observations of this galaxy have been analyzed in detail by Moran1999 , Lira2002 and Jenkins2004 , respectively. We used a simple model (absorbed vmekal + power-law) to fit the \ spectrum. It provides an acceptable fit ( ^2_ red 1.7 ) for our analysis.

Jenkins2004 tentatively detected a \ emission line at 6--7 keV. The higher S/N ratio data analyzed here shows clearly an emission line at 6.60 ^ +0.10 _ 0.04 keV (Table tbl_feka ). The energy of the line suggests that it is produced by ionized Fe, possibly related to supernovae activity.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3256 as a dusty merger-remnant starburst with a face-on outflow, dual radio nuclei, and XMM-Newton spectral evidence for charge exchange.

quotes.quote: NGC 3256 is a luminous dusty merger remnant, with a SFR about 10 times larger than M82. In antithesis to M82, NGC 3256 lies face-on, so that the galactic outflow points towards us and is superimposed on the galactic disc. The 3 and 6 cm radio maps norrisforbes95 reveal two distinct, resolved (FWHM 1.2 ) nuclei and some fainter diffuse radio emission. Separated by 5 in declination, the two cores dominate the radio emission, the northern one being slightly (15 ) brighter.

This fitted well the observed spectrum, including the O VIII lines, but it underestimated the O VII triplet at 22 . Thus, a set of three narrow Gaussian lines at the O VII energies was added. The resulting parameters for the line intensity ratios are shown in Fig. fig:OVIIngc3256 : they show that a CE component is detected on top of the thermal emission , notwithstanding the uncertainties on the G parameter. Some residuals are visible at the energies of the neutral Mg and Si lines, though a proper analysis of their significance has not yet been made.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 3256's southern nucleus and argues that infrared colors, SED modeling, NICMOS compact emission, and Chandra spectra indicate a heavily absorbed low-luminosity AGN.

quotes.quote: We investigate signs of Active Galactic Nucleus (AGN) in the luminous infrared galaxy NGC 3256 at both infrared and X-ray wavelengths. NGC 3256 has double, the Northern and Southern, nuclei (hereafter, N and S nuclei, respectively). We show that the Spitzer IRAC colors extracted at the S nucleus are AGN-like, and the Spitzer IRS spectrum is bluer at <6 m than at the N nucleus.

We found a 2.2 m compact source at the S nucleus in a HST NICMOS image and identified its unresolved core (at 0 26 resolution) with the compact core in previous mid-infrared observations at comparable resolution. The flux of the 2.2 m core is consistent with our AGN spectral energy distribution model. We also analyzed a deeper than ever Chandra X-ray spectrum of the unresolved (at 0 5 resolution) source at the S nucleus.

Together with a limit on equivalent width of a fluorescent Fe-K emission line at 6.4 keV, the X-ray spectrum is consistent with a typical Compton-thin Seyfert 2. We therefore suggest that the S nucleus hosts a heavily absorbed low-luminosity AGN.

sources.summary: NGC 3256 is a LIRG merger with spatially extended emission, lower overall CO/CN ratios than VV 114, and a higher southern-nucleus ratio near an AGN.

quotes.quote: This paper presents the results of an archival project using ALMA Cycle 0 data to survey the CO/CN ratio in 17 nearby galaxies. We identified a sample of 8 galaxies with distances < 200 Mpc for which both the CN N=1-0 and CO J=1-0 lines were detected. These galaxies range from nearby starbursts (M83, NGC 253) to ultra/luminous infrared galaxies (AM 1300-233, AM 2246-490, AM 2055-425, NGC 3256, VV 114) to an S0 galaxy with an unusual far-infrared excess (NGC 1377). We measure S/N-matched CO/CN line ratios as well as ratios calculated from the total flux detected in each line with ALMA.

NGC 3256 The CO/CN line ratio varies substantially across this galaxy. The minimum value of 8 corresponds to the northern nucleus neff2003 while the ratio at the peak of the CO emission in the southern nucleus is 26. These values have been measured in a single beam but are constant over areas of a few beams around each nucleus (Fig. fig-ngc3256 ). Images and global spectra for NGC 3256 are shown in Fig. fig-ngc3256 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3256 as a nearby galaxy merger template, modeling its core magnetic field and radio/X-ray emission with a one-zone cosmic-ray scenario.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we demonstrate that these emissions can explain the radio and X-ray fluxes of merging galaxies such as NGC 660 and NGC 3256. Using our model in combination with the observations, we can constrain the gas mass, shock velocity, magnetic field and the CR spectral index s of these systems. For NGC 660 a single-zone model with a spectral index 2.1 s 2.2 is able to reproduce simultaneously the radio and X-ray observations, while a simple one-zone scenario with s 2 can describe the radio and a large fraction of X-ray observations of NGC 3256.

NGC 3256 is also a galaxy formed by the collision of two galaxies and the redshift of NGC 3256 is z 0.009364 meyer2004hipass . In a CDM universe with _m=0.286 and H_0=69.6 \ km \ s^ 1 \ Mpc^ 1 , the luminosity distance to us is d_L=40.6 \ Mpc . It provides a nearby template for studying the properties of merging galaxies. Nearly infrared observations skrutskie2006two reveal that the major axis and minor axis sizes are a= 1.277 arcmin and b= 1.251 arcmin respectively. In our calculation, we assume an equivalent angular size _ g = ab =1.264 arcmin and the corresponding radius R=14.92 \ kpc .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3256 as a gas-rich merger with two nuclei, known outflows, complex inner morphology, differing H- and K-band velocity components, and nucleus-linked kinematic features.

quotes.quote: NGC 3256 : This object is a gas-rich merger where the secondary nucleus is located at 1 kpc S. Gas outflows in both nuclei had been previously reported see and references therein Emonts+14 . Despite the fact that the NICMOS image reveals a main spherical nucleus (i.e. north nucleus), the abundant, bright, spatially resolved structures of the inner regions complicate the photometric analysis within <1 kpc. From analysis of the outer parts, the interacting system seems to be slightly elongated (i.e. 45 ) with PA 60 .

The SINFONI data do not cover the same spatial regions in both bands, precluding the kinematic analysis. The H-band velocity map shows the rotation pattern of the main nucleus with similar orientation to the photometric PA (i.e. 80 ) while the K-band reveals a different velocity component, likely related with the secondary nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper finds superlinear HCN ratio trends in NGC 3256, lower CN ratios than comparison galaxies, and excitation variations associated with its two nuclei and merger-driven starburst environment.

quotes.quote: We use both new and archival ALMA data of three energy lines each of CN and HCN to explore intensity ratios in dense gas in NGC 3256, NGC 7469, and IRAS 13120-5453. The HCN (3-2)/HCN (1-0) intensity ratio varies in NGC 3256 and NGC 7469, with superlinear trends of 1.53 0.07 and 1.55 0.05 , respectively. We find an offset to higher HCN (3-2)/HCN (1-0) intensity ratios ( 0.8 ) in IRAS 13120-5453 compared to NGC 3256 ( 0.3-0.4 ) and NGC 7469 ( 0.3-0.5 ). The HCN (4-3)/HCN (3-2) intensity ratio in NGC 7469 has a slope of 1.34 0.05 . We attribute the variation within NGC 3256 to excitation associated with the northern and southern nuclei.

NGC 3256 is a nearby luminous galaxy merger that can be separated into northern and southern nuclear regions. The northern nucleus is fairly face-on and has a nuclear disk with significant starburst activity Sakamoto2014 . The southern nucleus is mostly edge-on and thought to host an embedded dormant AGN Sakamoto2014 . NGC 3256 is a complicated system containing molecular outflows from both nuclei Sakamoto2014 and high fractions of shocked and dense gas Harada2018 .

sources.summary: The paper reports H91 and H92 radio recombination-line detections from NGC 3256's nuclear starburst, describing it as a merging pair of disk galaxies with tidal tails and constrained ionized gas conditions.

quotes.quote: NGC 3256 is a pair of colliding disk galaxies 37 Mpc distant (for H_0 = 75 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 ) that are merging and display spectacular tidal tails (e.g. ). The FIR luminosity is 1.9 10^ 11 L_ following the method of , and the molecular gas mass, from CO emission, is large ( 3 10^ 10 M_ , ). The Br and [ Fe II ] luminosities imply a total star formation rate of 3.9 M_   yr ^ 1 . VLA observations at 6 cm with 4" resolution by show emission over 30" and arms of diffuse emission extending out towards the giant tidal arms seen in H I ( ). At higher resolution (2"), resolve the nucleus into two equal components; the nuclei of the progenitor galaxies are both undergoing starbursts.

We used the observed line strength (1.0 mJy), line width (160 km s ^ 1 ), size of the line-emitting region (630 pc), continuum emission (116 mJy) and spectral index (-0.7) to constrain conditions in the ionized gas. We considered a simple model consisting of a collection of spherical H II regions, all with the same diameter, electron temperature, T_ e , electron density, n_ e , in a volume of uniform synchrotron emission, following . Models with 10 to 300 H II regions, all with T_ e 5000  K, n_ e (10^ 3 to 10^ 4 ) cm ^ 3 and size of (5 to 600) pc produced good matches to the line and continuum emission.

sources.summary: The paper reports radio recombination-line detections from the starburst nucleus of NGC 3256 and describes it as a partly merged pair with tidal tails, abundant molecular gas, and nuclear star formation.

quotes.quote: We have detected the radio recombination lines H91 and H92 with rest frequencies of 8.6 GHz and 8.3 GHz from the starburst nucleus NGC 3256 at an angular resolution of 16.4'' 9.6'' using the Australia Telescope Compact Array and at an angular resolution of 12.0'' 2.9'' using the VLA. The line was detected at 1 mJy beam ^ 1 peak with a width of 160 km s ^ 1 with the ATCA and at 0.5 mJy beam ^ 1 peak with a width of 114 km s ^ 1 with the VLA. Modelling the line emitting region as a collection of H II regions, we derive constraints on the required number of H II regions, their temperature, density, and distribution. We find that a collection of 10 to 300 H II regions with temperatures of 5000 K, densities of 1000 cm ^ 3 to 5000 cm ^ 3 and diameters of 15 pc produced good matches to the line and continuum emmission.

NGC 3256 is a pair of colliding disk galaxies that are partly merged and display spectacular tidal tails and disrupted morphologies (e.g. Schweizer 1986). At an inferred distance of 37 Mpc ( v_ = (2781 24) km s ^ 1 from optical emission lines; de Vaucouleurs et al. 1991), the FIR luminosity is 1.9 10^ 11 L_ following the method of Helou et al. (1985), making it one of the most luminous galaxies with a recession velocity less than 3000 km s ^ 1 . The molecular gas mass, inferred from CO emission, is extremely large ( 3 10^ 10 M_ ; Sargent et al. 1989). The far-infrared (FIR) colours (Rowan-Robinson Crawford 1989), and the near-infrared colours (Glass Moorwood 1985) are typical of starburst galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3256 as an extreme-star-formation supernova factory, a localized hyperflare search target, and a possible site of very young magnetars.

quotes.quote: These are Arp 299 n2004 and NGC 3256 l2004 , two galaxies with extreme star formation rate (``supernova factories''). The total star formation rate in these galaxies is few times lower than that in the Virgo cluster, therefore they are a less probable source of HFs in the BATSE data. Nevertheless, these galaxies are of great interest since they are well-localized and can lead to measurements with a better angular resolution. A number of candidates for HFs from these galaxies is given by Popov (private communication See the e-print astro-ph/0502391.

In the four near-by star-forming galaxies there should be SGRs 10 times younger than the Galactic ones; in galaxies like Arp 299 and NGC 3256 we expect to find magnetars with ages about few tens of years. If they produce frequent bursts, then non-detection should mean that their luminosities are lower than those exhibited by the galactic sources. As it is noted by h2005 , Swift gives an excellent opportunity to observe extragalactic GFs and HFs of SGRs.
Arp 299 and NGC 3256 can be good targets for such observations.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3256 as a peculiar spiral at 37 Mpc with tidal tails, a knotty central morphology, two nuclei, and evidence that its merger is not yet complete.

quotes.quote: NGC 3256 General description The galaxy is a peculiar spiral galaxy located at a distance of 37 Mpc lipari00 . The angular scale at this distance is 1 = 179 pc. This galaxy consists of a main body of about 60 , two extended tidal tails that can be traced as far as 8 \ and two faint external loops lipari00 . The central ( 30 ) region shows a knotty structure with a very unusual triple asymmetrical spiral arm morphology. The double tidal tails are characteristic of an interaction between two spiral galaxies of comparable mass e.g. deVaucouleurs61 .

High spatial resolution near-IR e.g. moorwood94,kotilainen96 , MIR boker97 and radio observations e.g. norris95,neff03 reveal two distinct nuclei aligned in the north-south direction and separated by 5 . The southern nucleus has no optical counterpart due to the high extinction in this region. The presence of these two nuclei, which at radio wavelengths have approximately similar size, brightness and spectral index, suggests that the merger is not yet completed.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3256 as a starburst comparison spectrum, noting its high SFR, hot-gas soft X-ray emission, and cold-ISM absorption that can obscure HMXB soft X-rays.

quotes.quote: Several such spectra, measured with Chandra and NuSTAR , have been presented by lehetal15 , of which the most interesting is that of the starburst galaxy NGC 3256. This galaxy has a very high total SFR of 36 M_ yr ^ 1 , which is similar to the combined SFR of all 27 galaxies making up our sample ( 32 M_ yr ^ 1 ). Therefore, NGC 3256 should be as representative of the local luminous HMXB population as our sample of galaxies. As shown in Fig. fig:avspec_obs , the SFR-normalized spectrum of NGC 3256 nearly matches our observed collective spectrum of HMXBs at energies above 3 keV, confirming that the total emission of actively star forming galaxies at these energies is dominated by ULXs.

It is more difficult to compare the collective spectrum of HMXBs and the NGC 3256 spectrum below 3 keV. First, as discussed by lehetal15 and evident from the strong X-ray line emission observed from NGC 3256, thermal emission from hot interstellar gas provides a strong contribution to the soft X-ray flux from this galaxy. Secondly, soft X-ray emission from point sources in NGC 3256 can be significantly suppressed by absorption in the cold component of its ISM, in fact much stronger than for typical galaxies in our sample, which are characterized by much lower SFRs (at most 3 M_ yr ^ 1 ) and smaller amounts of cold gas.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 3256 as a merging ultraluminous starburst with two molecular outflows; the southern nucleus has a collimated likely AGN-driven outflow.

quotes.quote: An example of both is provided by the galaxy merger NGC 3256, an ultra-luminous starburst at z=0.01. ALMA observations of the molecular gas (through the CO(3-2) line) have revealed high-velocity wings in both nuclei, the face-on N3256N and almost edge-on N3256S (Sakamoto et al 2014). For the latter, the outflow is highly collimated, and likely due to an AGN (cf Figure fig1 ). The derived maximum velocity is 2000 km/s out to 300 pc, and corresponds to 50 M _ /yr. For the northern galaxy, the maximum velocity is 750 km/s, and the outflow rate of 60 M _ /yr.
In both cases, these rates are comparable to the star formation rate, showing that the implied quenching is significant. The time-scale to develop these outflows is 1 Myr (Sakamoto et al 2014).

sources.summary: NGC 3256 is treated as a LIRG merger with hundreds of clusters in clumps, B-band color measurements, and a dense molecular nuclear disk with outflows.

quotes.quote: For this paper, we used multi-wavelength observations of 7 merging systems in the survey: Arp 220, ESO 185--IG13, Haro 11 (ESO 350--IG038), NGC 1614, NGC 2623, NGC 3256, and NGC 3690. Arp 220 is an Ultraluminous Infrared Galaxy (ULIRG), NGC 1614, NGC 2623, NGC 3256, and NGC 3690 (Arp 299) are LIRGS, and ESO 185--IG13 and Haro 11 are blue compact galaxies. Their distances range from 38 to 90 Mpc.

Arp 220 is anomalous in having very few clusters within the clumps, whereas NGC 3256 and NGC 3690 have hundreds. As the figure illustrates, the distributions of clusters within the clumps are fairly uniform, although the concentration of clusters increases in regions within a clump where there are brighter arcs and substructure, such as in NGC 3256 and NGC 3690.
NGC 3256 has a molecular nuclear disk with a gas density > 10^3 M_ pc ^ 2 saka , with HCN and HCO ^+ outflows mich .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3256 ALMA CO data, notes incomplete zero-spacing correction, finds its molecular lines exceptionally bright, and reports several species detected only in that galaxy.

quotes.quote: In addition, we used the CO (1--0) data of NGC 3256 obtained with the ALMA 12 m (TM2) array and the ACA 7 m array as part of 2016.2.00042.S, 2016.2.00094.S, and 2018.1.00223.S. The data were obtained using a single pointing.

The data of NGC 3256 were not corrected with the zero-spacing information because the TP array data are not available. The maximum recoverable angular scale calculated from the minimum baseline of the 7 m array ( 9 m) is 35 8 at the observing frequency. We compared the flux density measured in the 12m+7m image of NGC 3256 with the literature single-dish measurement 1992A&A...264...49C . The recovered flux is 90 in the central 44 region.

The molecular lines in NGC 3256 are roughly five times brighter than the galaxy with second brightest lines. Of the 21 galaxies detected in one or more molecular lines, all but UGC 10675 was detected in the ^ 13 CO (1--0) line. Seventeen sources were detected in either the HCN (1--0) or HCO ^ + (1--0) lines and 15 were detected in both lines. While UGC 10675 was detected with S/N 5 in the HCN line, it was not detected in the HCO ^ + line. On the other hand, NGC 6052 was detected with S/N > 6 in the HCO ^ + line, but it was not detected in the HCN line. Six out of 15 sources have the HCN luminosity higher than the HCO ^ + luminosity. The HC _ 3 N lines were detected in UGC 5101 and UGC 8058, both of which host X-ray detected AGNs e.g., 2011A&A...529A.106I . Three molecular species (-C _ 3 H _ 2 , CH _ 3 OH, and CH _ 3 CCH) were only detected in NGC 3256 and N _ 2 H ^ + was only detected in UGC 8058.

sources.summary: NGC 3256 is a late-stage merger with a starburst northern component and Compton-thick AGN southern component; the paper finds buried, hot, obscured dust in NGC 3256S.

quotes.quote: NGC 3256 is a late-stage merger consisting of a face-on spiral galaxy NGC 3256 N Sakamoto2014 with a bright nuclear starburst Lira2008 . The southern component of the merger (NGC 3256 S) is an edge on galaxy hosting a highly obscured, Compton Thick AGN e.g. Ohyama2015 which drives a highly collimated outflow e.g. Sakamoto2014, Pereira-Santaella2023 .

NGC 3256 NGC 3256S hosts a low luminosity, Compton Thick AGN e.g. Ohyama2015 which has a collimated molecular outflow Sakamoto2014, Emonts2014, Pereira-Santaella2023 detected through CO, HCN, HCO+ and H _2 transitions. From the dust distribution we see a clear signature of a buried continuum source which shows a gradient of extinction, with the extinction increasing with temperature in the warm component. This suggests a smooth torus rather than a clumpy one, as the hot dust is buried and not leaking through low density lines of sight. In addition, the dust distribution shows an isolated hot dust component that is also highly obscured. This hot dust, as shown in Fig. fig:Results , dominates the continuum between 2 - 10 m.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3256 as a merger-induced starburst with luminous X-rays, thermal soft emission, possible buried AGN, diffuse disk emission, and two hard X-ray nuclei.

quotes.quote: NGC 3256 NGC 3256 is a merger-induced starburst system (Moran, Lehnert Helfand 1999) with an X-ray luminous starburst having a luminosity of 1.6 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 . ASCA data indicate that the soft X-rays are primarily thermal in origin, with k T = 0.3 and 0.8 keV, while the hard X-rays are best described as a power law. It has been suggested that NGC 3256 harbors two nuclei, one being a buried AGN The Chandra image (Fig. \ 14) shows diffuse emission extended throughout the disk of the galaxy. At soft energies there is a single nuclear source, but at hard energies, two nuclei are clearly visible.

The crosses mark the position of the optical nucleus (which has an IR and radio counterpart) and an IR and radio point source that is offset from the optical nucleus by about 1 kpc (Kotilainen et al. \ 1996). The two IR and radio nuclei are of comparable strength. The Chandra image shows that this is true in hard X-rays as well, confirming the possibility of a buried AGN with N_ H 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 .

sources.summary: NGC 3256 shows one optical nucleus plus a hidden second nucleus in other bands; its H emission follows spiral structure and the primary nucleus is regarded as starburst-like.

quotes.quote: NGC 3256 Our optical HST images show one obvious nucleus located in the center of Figure f:n3256 (see also zepf99 ). This nucleus is surrounded by a relatively symmetric spiral morphology. Near-infrared, radio continuum, and X-ray observations e.g., nor95,kot96,lira02 have discovered a second nucleus about 5 (885 pc) south of the primary nucleus (marked with an ``X'' in Fig. f:n3256 ), presumably hidden beneath the dark (red) dust feature directly south of the primary nucleus in the V-I image of Fig. f:n3256 (see also eng03 ). We do not see an optical source at the position of the second near-infrared, radio, and X-ray source.

The H emission is extended and follows the spiral structure, but the brightest emission occurs at the position of the primary nucleus. Most previous studies regard this nucleus to be of starburst type, although ver86 were unable to resolve its true nature. While the primary nucleus has a blue V-I color near 0.7, the dusty region covering the secondary nucleus to the south of the primary nucleus has a V-I color of 2.5 or redder.

sources.summary: For NGC 3256 the paper analyzes archival NICMOS data, identifies a smooth northern nucleus, and discusses multiwavelength evidence that both nuclei may be low-luminosity AGN.

quotes.quote: Our analysis of the late-stage merger galaxy NGC 3256 is based on archival NICMOS images in H and F222M (hereafter referred to as K ; see Figure f:thirtytwofiftysixvk ); no J band data exists. The nuclear region (NGC 3256 N) appears as a concentrated and smooth source. Radio imaging at 3 cm and 6 cm, performed by nor95 , identified a second nucleus 5 south of the primary (NGC 3256 S).

Based on radio and X-ray power and their ratios nef03 argue that both nuclei may be low-luminosity active galactic nuclei (LLAGNs). In our NICMOS data there is a compact source at the position of the secondary nucleus, but with fainter intensity than the primary nucleus. The source appears projected onto the middle of a spiral arm, lying in a highly reddened region corresponding to a local maximum in the V-K image.

sources.summary: NGC 3256 is described as a nearby advanced merger with an obscured component, extensive gas and tidal tails, central star formation, and widespread shocks.

quotes.quote: IRAS F10257-4339 (NGC 3256) This advanced merger is quite nearby and well studied. As with IRAS F01053-1746, one of the galaxies in IRAS F10257-4339 is very extinguished. The second system and its nucleus are revealed at longer wavelengths, south of the main optical nucleus Alonso02,Rothberg10 . Deprojected radii are calculated using the main optical nucleus.

The gas and tidal tails in this system extend quite far (e.g. Rothberg10 ) our IFU mosaic covers only the central 6 kpc, though this appears to be the physical extent of most of the ongoing star formation in this system. NGC 3256 also shows evidence for widespread shocks in our data and is discussed alongside IC 1623 in Rich11 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 3256 as the oldest merger in its sample, emphasizing its eastern tidal tail, partial ACS coverage, and broad cluster-age distribution.

quotes.quote: We study the stellar content in the tidal tails of three nearby merging galaxies, NGC 520, NGC 2623, and NGC 3256, using BVI imaging taken with the Advanced Camera for Surveys on board the Hubble Space Telescope . The tidal tails in all three systems contain compact and fairly massive young star clusters, embedded in a sea of diffuse, unresolved stellar light.

NGC 3256 is on the right in Figure sample . While it has two tails (east and west), only part of the eastern tail was observed. The three mergers are next to each other on the Toomre Sequence, in the order NGC 520, NGC 2623, and NGC 3256 (Toomre 1977).

The oldest merger, NGC 3256, contains clusters in the eastern tail with ages _ clus 260 Myr, although we caution that the broad age distribution in NGC 3256E suggests that cluster formation likely began earlier than 260 Myr ago.

sources.summary: NGC 3256 is described as a powerful starburst major merger whose 0.3-30 keV emission combines diffuse thermal plasma, point-source power-law emission, and a northern nucleus powered mainly by star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 3256 NGC 3256 (35.4 Mpc) is a major-merger system composed of two gas-rich galaxies with comparable masses (total M_ 10^ 11   ) that are in a nearly coalescent phase of the merger (L pari \ 2000; Alonso-Herrero \ 2002). Two discrete nuclei have been identified 5 arcsec apart (850 pc; Zenner Lenzen 1993; Norris Forbes 1995) and remarkably long (200 kpc) H  I tidal features have been identified (e.g., Graham \ 1984; English \ 2003). The system has the highest 8--1000  m infrared luminosity ( L_ IR   4 10^ 11   L_ ; Sanders \ 2003) for galaxies that reside within z 0.01 , and studies at radio (e.g., Norris Forbes 1995), submm (e.g., Sakamoto \ 2006, 2014), infrared (Graham \ 1984; Doyon \ 1994a, 1994b; Moorwood Olivia 1994; Lira \ 2008; Alonso-Herrero \ 2013), optical (e.g., Heckman \ 2000; L pari \ 2000; Alonso-Herrero \ 2002), and UV (e.g., Kinney \ 1993; Leitherer \ 2013) wavelengths all support a scenario in which the majority of the galaxy's power output is produced by starburst-related activity. X-ray studies with \ and \ have shown that the 0.3--10 keV spectrum of NGC 3256 can be modeled well as a multiphase thermal plasma with harder power-law emission from a population of discrete point sources, the majority of which are ULXs (see, e.g., Lira \ 2002; Jenkins \ 2004; Pereira-Santaella \ 2011).

For NGC 3256, we find that the sum of the diffuse component plus a single absorbed ( N_ H 3 10^ 22  cm ^ 2 ) power-law ( 2.6 ) and an absorbed hot ( 1 keV) plasma component were sufficient to obtain a good fit to the 0.3--30 keV spectrum.

This result is consistent with previous studies that have concluded that the northern nucleus in NGC 3256 is powered almost exclusively by star-formation activity (e.g., Lira \ 2002; Jenkins \ 2004; Sakamoto \ 2014).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3256 as a late-stage luminous infrared merger with dual molecular outflows and ALMA chemical variations tracing shocked regions.

quotes.quote: NGC 3256 is a late-stage merger with nuclear separation of 850 pc. Because of merger-induced star formation, NGC 3256 has a high infrared luminosity, making it a luminous-infrared galaxy (LIRG).

Harada et al. (in preparation) conducted multi-species observations of NGC 3256 with ALMA in the 3-mm and 1.3-mm band. As we compare the column density ratios over ^ 13 CO or CS in several locations within NGC 3256, most variations are seen for SiO and CH _ 3 OH (Fig. fig:n3256 middle panels). Those species are likely to be tracing the shocked regions.
Other molecules such as CCH, CN, HC _3 N, and N _2 H ^+ do not have significant variations within NGC 3256.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3256 as a powerful starburst merger with unclear X-ray-emission origin, no obvious AGN signature, and a complex multi-component X-ray spectrum.

quotes.quote: NGC 3256: Powerful starburst galaxy studied by 2015ApJ...806..126L with Chandra and Nustar data. Nature of X-ray emission is unclear as no obvious AGN signature was found. This galaxy was studied by 2004MNRAS.352.1335J and was referred to as starburst merger galaxy, and a hard X-ray bolometric correction was estimated to be 10^ 5 . The 10^ 40 has been estimated mostly from the ULXs and crowded X-ray sources, and not an AGN.

In our study we found that the X-ray spectrum is complex. It required two thermal components in the soft X-rays ( APEC ), one neutral intrinsic absorber, and three Gaussian emission lines for three Fe K emission lines at different ionisation states.

sources.summary: The paper presents a new ALMA GMC catalog for the merging NGC 3256 system, a star-forming LIRG whose cutoff remains unconstrained.

quotes.quote: New Catalogs We produce new catalogs of GMCs for NGC 3627 and NGC 3256, since no published catalogs currently exist for these galaxies. After downloading the pipeline calibrated datasets for NGC 3627 (project 2015.1.00956.S PI: A. Leroy) and NGC 3256 (project 2015.1.00714.S, PI: K. Silwa) from the ALMA archive, we perform our data reduction and imaging using the CASA software package CASA . We first perform continuum subtraction for the individual interferometric datasets and then combine the results from the 12-meter and 7-meter (ACA) arrays for each galaxy. To image the dataset, we use the non-interactive cleaning process in the tclean routine. We select the area of interest in the two galaxies, using the line-free regions in the datacube to estimate the noise, and then run the tclean process until it reaches a clean threshold of twice the measured rms noise.
The two interacting galaxies in our sample, the Antennae and NGC 3256, are both LIRGs and have high rates of star formation.
We also present a new catalog of GMCs in the merging NGC 3256 system based on archival ALMA observations.

sources.summary: The paper studies Chandra observations of the merger NGC3256, finding bright compact X-ray sources, detected nuclei, no southern active nucleus, and soft diffuse thermal emission in the starburst system.

quotes.quote: We present a detailed analysis of high-resolution Chandra observations of the merger system NGC3256, the most IR luminous galaxy in the nearby universe. The X-ray data show that several discrete sources embedded in complex diffuse emission contribute 20 of the total emission ( L_ x ^ tot 8 10^ 41 ergs s ^ 1 in the 0.5-10 keV energy range). The compact sources are hard and extremely bright and their emission is probably dominated by accretion driven processes. Both galaxy nuclei are detected with L_ x 3-10 10^ 40 ergs s ^ 1 .

No evidence is found for the presence of an active nucleus in the southern nucleus, contrary to previous speculation. Once the discrete sources are removed, the diffuse component has a soft spectrum which can be modelled by the superposition of 3 thermal plasma components with temperatures kT = 0.6, 0.9 and 3.9 keV.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 3256's two nuclei in the near and mid infrared, finding similar obscuration and star formation estimates but distinct infrared emission requiring extra warm dust in the northern nucleus.

quotes.quote: We present new resolved near and mid-IR imaging and N-band spectroscopy of the two nuclei in the merger system NGC 3256, the most IR luminous galaxy in the nearby universe. The results from the SED fit to the data are consistent with previous estimates of the amount of obscuration towards the nuclei and the nuclear star formation rates. However, we also find substantial differences in the infrared emission from the two nuclei which cannot be explained by obscuration alone. We conclude that the northern nucleus requires an additional component of warm dust in order to explain its properties. This suggests that local starforming conditions can vary significantly within the environment of a single system.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3256 as a two-nucleus merger whose southern nucleus is highly obscured, while PAH, continuum, and most atomic emission peak at the northern nucleus.

quotes.quote: NGC 3256 It is galaxy merger with two nuclei. The southern nucleus, located 5 arcsec away from the north nucleus, is highly obscured, although it is visible in the 5.5 \ continuum map. It has one of the deepest silicate absorption feature in our sample, S_ Si 1.4 . The silicate feature of the rest of the galaxy is similar to that found in the rest of the galaxies of our sample. The PAH, continuum and atomic emission (except ) peak at the north nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA and SMA observations of NGC 3256, resolving its two molecular-gas-rich merger nuclei and finding powerful bipolar molecular outflows that significantly affect its gas budget.

quotes.quote: We report ALMA and SMA observations of the luminous infrared merger NGC 3256, the most luminous galaxy within z=0.01 . Our data show that both of the two merger nuclei separated by 5 \ (0.8 kpc) on the sky have a compact concentration of molecular gas. We identify them as nuclear disks with molecular gas surface densities over 10^3 \ and determine that while one at the northern nucleus is nearly face-on the other at the southern nucleus is almost edge-on.

The sum of these outflow rates, although subject to the uncertainty in the molecular mass estimate, either exceeds or compares to the total star formation rate in NGC 3256. The feedback from nuclear activities in the form of molecular outflows is therefore significant in the gas consumption budget, and hence evolution, of this luminous infrared galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes this nearby advanced merger as dusty, double-nucleated at longer wavelengths, tidally extended, centrally star-forming, and widely shock-excited.

quotes.quote: IRAS F10257-4339 (NGC 3256) This advanced merger is the nearest galaxy in our sample (z 0.0094 ) and is well studied. As with IRAS F01053-1746, one of the galaxies in IRAS F10257-4339 is very extinguished. The second, buried system and its nucleus are revealed at longer wavelengths, south of the main optical nucleus Alonso02,Rothberg10 . The gas and tidal tails in this system extend 10's of kpc from the nuclear regions (e.g. Rothberg10 ).

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 3256 as a dusty, high-SFR merging pair with a dense young cluster population, measuring cluster luminosity functions in its inner and outer regions.

quotes.quote: The main body of NGC 3256 contains a dense population of clusters, many of which are embedded in the galaxy's dusty interstellar medium. The galaxy's intense star formation makes it an extreme environment in which to study cluster formation and destruction.

Luminosity Function We present the LFs for clusters in NGC 3256 in Figure dndl . The LF is best described by a power law, dN/dL L^ , where is determined from a linear fit to log (dN/d(m_V)) . The m_V values have been aperture-corrected and corrected for foreground extinction in the Galaxy. The three panels of Figure dndl show the LF for the entire sample, as well as for clusters in the inner and outer regions of NGC 3256, separately. We find = -2.23 0.07 for the combined sample with m_V 21.5 .

sources.summary: The paper observes the luminous infrared merger NGC 3256 with ALMA, comparing chemistry across its nuclei, tidal arms, and outflow-influenced regions and finding strong SiO and methanol variations from shocks.

quotes.quote: To study such molecular chemistry, we observed the luminous-infrared galaxy and merger NGC 3256 using the Atacama Large Millimeter/sub-millimeter Array. We covered most of the 3-mm and 1.3-mm bands for a multi-species, multi-transition analysis. We first analyzed intensity ratio maps of selected lines such as HCN/HCO ^+ , which shows no enhancement at an AGN. We then compared the chemical compositions within NGC 3256 at the two nuclei, tidal arms, and positions with influence from galactic outflows. We found the largest variation in SiO and CH _3 OH, species that are likely to be enhanced by shocks.

sources.summary: NGC 3256 is an advanced merger with northern and southern components; high-resolution MIR and Chandra data identify the northern peak as the brighter star-forming and X-ray source.

quotes.quote: [ 65 ] NGC 3256 : This source is assumed to be in an advanced merger stage, with a northern brighter component (the central peak) and a southern component at about 10 '', elongated in the E W direction. The possibility of this being a merging, obscured, companion galaxy was first suggested by MooOli1994 , and radio observations by NorFor1995 supported this theory by detecting two equally bright knots. However, high-resolution MIR imaging shows that the northern peak is actually 20 times brighter than the southern region, suggesting that most of the star formation in the galaxy is originated there LirWar2002 . They also find the northern peak to be brighter in X-rays, using Chandra data. The images shown in this work mark the two hard-band peaks mentioned by them, the southern clearly falling in an obscured region, with dimmer soft band emission.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC3256 as an earlier-stage merger with very young clusters, a powerful starburst-driven nuclear outflow, and enhanced massive cluster formation.

quotes.quote: The remaining systems, NGC3256, NGC3690, and NGC6052 have increasing number of clusters with ages younger than 100 Myr and peaked below 10 Myr, suggesting a recent enhancement in their SFR. Very similar conclusions have been reached by different studies of the cluster population of NGC3256 goddard10, mulia2016 and of the NGC3690 system by randria19 . NGC3256 is well know to host a very powerful nuclear outflow powered by a nuclear starburst sakamoto14 . We see a clear enhancement of massive and very young cluster formation towards the inner regions of this galaxy, although the nuclear starburst and associated molecular outflow remain hidden at the wavelengths probed in our study.

It is interesting to notice that our optical study is biased against the extreme nuclear starburst arbored in major merger systems like NGC3690 and NGC3256, suggesting that star and cluster formation in these systems will rapidly evolve.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 3256 as a luminous merger LIRG with twin molecular outflows from its nuclei, including a starburst superwind, likely AGN-powered bipolar jet, and shock-enhanced chemistry.

quotes.quote: Examples of well studied LIRG molecular outflows include the twin outflows of the luminous merger NGC 3256 sakamoto06,sakamoto14,michiyama18, harada18a . The high-velocity molecular gas in the system consists of two molecular outflows from the nuclei (Fig. fig:N3256 ). The one from the northern nucleus is part of a starburst-driven superwind ( v_ out >750 km s ^ 1 ) seen nearly pole-on, while the outflow from the southern nucleus is a highly collimated bipolar jet seen nearly edge-on and likely powered by a (now dormant) AGN. The southern outflow shows increasing velocity out to 300 pc from the nucleus where the velocity peaks at a staggering v_ out =2000 km s ^ 1 . The mass outflow rate, from the combined outflows, exceeds 100 \ yr ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper uses newer NGC 3256 ALMA data, finds a lower global CN/CO ratio than Wilson2023 but matching spatial average, and notes an embedded southern-nucleus AGN.

quotes.quote: We removed the only Cycle 0 project which met our selection criteria, project code 2011.0.00525.S, with observations of NGC 3256. The corresponding Cycle 6 observations for NGC 3256 are of higher spatial resolution and sensitivity and can be better calibrated than the Cycle 0 data. measured the (CN bright)/CO ratio for NGC 3256 using the archival Cycle 0 data and we compare with her results in Section subsubsec:cn_co_prev_obs .

Our global ratios of 0.17 and 0.10 agree with their results for IRAS 13120 and NGC 7469, but our global ratio in NGC 3256 is lower ( 0.02 ). However, our spatially averaged (CN bright)/CO intensity ratio of 0.05 is in agreement for NGC 3256. , and found higher (CN bright)/CO ratios toward regions with higher SFRs in U/LIRGs.

The southern nucleus of NGC 3256 has an embedded, dormant AGN Sakamoto2014 , with evidence from both infrared and X-ray emission Ohyama2015 and observations of a jet-driven outflow Sakamoto2014, Brunetti2021 .

sources.summary: The paper separates NGC 3256 MIR emission into core and extended components, adopts the larger source size, and derives central extinction from Pa/Br measurements.

quotes.quote: NGC 3256 - The MIR emission can be separated into two components of different sizes, a core of 8 \ superposed onto a source of 20 . Therefore, a single gaussian does not provide a good fit to the surface brightness profile. We adopt the size of the larger source as our aperture. Kawara87 report a Br flux of 14.8 10^ 17 W m ^ 2 in a smaller aperture of 9 18 , almost identical to the total Br flux of 15 10^ 17 W m ^ 2 measured by Moo94 in a field of view of 34 34 . We derive the extinction in the central 3.5 3.5 from the Pa /Br ratio of Doy94 and assume that it does not vary inside our larger aperture.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3256 as a comparison LIRG with hot and cold bi-conical molecular outflows sharing morphology, kinematics, and a derived outflow rate.

quotes.quote: Considering the molecular gas, the nearby LIRG NGC 3256 was the first galaxy where we imaged a bi-conical molecular outflow both in the hot and cold molecular phase at 100 pc resolution see also sak14 emo14 . The hot and cold components share a similar morphology and kinematics, which suggests that the hot and cold phase trace the same gas features. In NGC 3256 we derived a hot-to-cold molecular gas mass ratio of 6 10 ^ 5 , which is similar to that found in another nearby LIRG per16 . The total molecular mass outflow rate of at least M _ H_2 20 M _ yr ^ 1 in the LIRG NGC 3256 is similar to that of the H _ 2 outflows that we identified in the ULIRGs studied in this paper.

sources.summary: The paper calls AM 1045-433 a Toomre-sequence galaxy, finding center-coordinate changes from the innermost region, high A2 peaking at 20 arcseconds, and ordered Fourier amplitudes.

quotes.quote: AM 1045-433: This is a Toomre sequence galaxy. Here the co-ordinates of the centre (x0 and y0) show a change from the innermost region itself. The value of A _2 is high with a peak value of 0.5 at 20". This galaxy shows the following values for the Fourier amplitudes: A _2 > A _3 > A _4 > A _1 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3256 as a merger LIRG with two bright nuclei whose near-IR, X-ray, and mid-IR properties favor star formation over dominant AGN emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 3256 is a well-studied merger galaxy, with two bright nuclei (north and south, see Fig. 2 for location). Kotilainen et al. (1996) and Neff, Ulvestad, Campion (2003) suggested that the nuclei may host low luminosity AGN. The NIR (L pari et al. 2000), X-ray (Lira et al. 2002), and MIR spectroscopic (see Mart n-Hern andez et al. 2006) properties of both nuclei are generally consistent with star formation. The extended ( FWHM 60 pc) nature of the NIR and MIR continuum emission of both nuclei further corroborates that such AGN, if present, are not dominant in the MIR.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3256 as a well-studied merger LIRG with two bright nuclei, extended near- and mid-infrared nuclear emission, and no dominant obscured AGN contribution.

quotes.quote: Three LIRGs (NGC 5135, IC 4518W, and NGC 7130) were observed with the N broad-band filter (central wavelength _ =10.36 m) and NGC 3256 with the Si-2 narrow-band filter ( _ =8.74 m).

NGC 3256 is a well-studied merger galaxy, with two bright nuclei, which are also the brightest mid-IR sources in this galaxy. Although it has been argued that this merger may contain obscured AGN, the extended ( FWHM 60 pc) nature of the near and mid-IR continuum emission of both nuclei suggests that such AGN, if present, are not dominant in the mid-IR.
The LIRG nuclei (except the south nucleus of NGC 3256) show elevated mid-IR/Pa ratios when compared to H ii regions.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 3256 as a merger/starburst galaxy with possible hidden AGN activity, using multi-cloud models to reproduce its observed infrared line ratios.

quotes.quote: A possible hidden AGN has also been reported in the merger galaxy NGC 3256 .

However, previous investigations on the physical conditions in the ISM of NGC 253 , NGC 3256 , and M 82 found relatively low electronic densities on the basis of the [ ] 33 m/18 m and [ ] 88 m/52 m line ratios, and low velocities derived from the FWHM of the IR line profiles. Multi-cloud models Many different conditions coexist in the ISM of single galaxies. Therefore, we performed multi-cloud model calculations to reproduce all the observed IR line ratios available in the literature for the starburst galaxies NGC 253, NGC 3256, and M 82.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3256's dust reradiation with an AGN-like component, notes free-free continuum absorption, and identifies it as a luminous infrared colliding pair with compact starburst clouds.

quotes.quote: Fig. 5 shows that the IR reradiation by dust of NGC 253, IC 342, NGC 3256, NGC 3690, NGC 5236, and NGC 7552 can be also modelled by a unique model corresponding to a AGN with \ = 1000 , 0 \ = 1000 3, and a power-law flux with log \ = 11 (model m7) as was found in Arp 220 (Contini et al. 2004, Fig. A1).
Free-free continuum absorption is evident in a few objects (NGC 3256, NGC 3690, NGC 7552).

The luminous IR galaxies of the Verma et al sample, with L _ IR > 10^ 11 L _ , NGC 3256 and NGC 3690 which are identified as a colliding pair and a peculiar merger, respectively, show starburst cloud distances from the centre 100 pc.

sources.summary: The paper reports an unresolved mid-infrared core in the IR-luminous merger NGC 3256 and contrasts this with an earlier resolved, featureless image.

quotes.quote: NGC 3256 Our image (not presented here) of this IR-luminous merger system shows an unresolved core, In contrast to who present a resolved, yet featureless image, taken with shorter exposure time. The difference may be explained by a change in seeing between the calibrator, from which the PSF is determined, and NGC 3256.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3256 as a starbursting ULIRG nearing merger, with optical tidal tails formed about 5e8 years ago and central radio emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 3256 -- A kinematic study of this ULIRG system (English et al. ) indicates that it is currently experiencing a starburst just prior to the merging. The two tidal tails visible in optical images probably formed about 5 10^8 yr ago and the coalescence is estimated to take place in 2 10^8 yr. The radio data at 4.86 GHz reveal only total emission peaked at the galactic centre, the site of intense infrared and X-ray emission (Brassington et al. ).

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 3256 as a U/LIRG, uses matched channel imaging for its HCN data, and reports a northern starburst-nucleus CN/HCN enhancement absent in the southern AGN nucleus.
