quotes.quote: For IRAS 13120, NGC 3256, and VV114, star formation rate calculated from L_ IR using the relation from kennicutt2012 and stellar masses from 2MASS K-band luminosity e.g., howell2010ApJ...715..572H .

When only low spectral resolution data were available for CN (NGC 1808, NGC 3351) or HCN (NGC 3256), that line was imaged in channel space with no binning; the other two lines were then imaged using velocity widths matched to the output from the low spectral resolution cube.

Interestingly, the northern (starburst) nucleus of NGC 3256 shows a 15 enhancement in the CN/HCN line ratio compared to its global average, while the southern (AGN) nucleus does not show an enhancement.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3256 as the dominant southern-sky LIRG prediction: a merging super-starburst and luminous X-ray starburst expected to yield Auger events.

quotes.quote: The most conspicuous object is the galaxy NGC 3256. It is a merging system - "super starburst" galaxy [ 5 ] in a very vigorous episode of star formation, also the most luminous X-ray starburst galaxy currently known. If the Arp 299 in the northern hemisphere is responsible for the three events then Auger could register about 4 showers from NGC 3256 for each 100 events above 4 10^ 19 eV .

gmw_00000309.name: UGC 4703
gmw_00000309.overview: UGC 4703 is an interacting dwarf galaxy located near the isolated spiral galaxy NGC 2718 [arXiv:1710.09040]. It is actively interacting with its smaller companion, UGC 4703B, forming a stellar bridge that contains star-forming regions and neutral hydrogen emission [arXiv:1710.09040].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes UGC 4703 as an interacting dwarf near NGC 2718, with a smaller companion, stellar bridge, HI interaction evidence, and star-forming bridge regions.

quotes.quote: We present an analysis of physical and morphological properties of an interacting pair of dwarf galaxies UGC 4703 located at the vicinity of an isolated Milky-Way (MW) type spiral galaxy NGC 2718. Based on the comparison of physical and morphological properties with that of the Large and Small Magellanic Clouds (LMC and SMC), we report that the UGC 4703 pair-NGC 2718 system is probably an LMC-SMC-MW analogue. Located at a sky projected distance of 81 kpc from NGC 2718, we find that UGC 4703 is clearly interacting with its nearby smaller mass companion UGC 4703B forming a bridge of stellar stream between them. Total B-band luminosity of UGC 4703 and its companion is -17.75 and -16.25 mag, respectively.

We obtained HI 21-cm line data of UGC 4703 using the GMRT to get a more detailed view of neutral hydrogen (HI) emission. The HI image revealed evidence of interaction between the dwarf galaxy pair but no extended emission, like Magellanic Stream (MS), was detected between NGC 2781 and the UGC 4703 dwarf pair. We also detected star-forming regions along the UGC 4703/ 4703B bridge with stellar mass exceeding 10 ^ 7 M _ .

gmw_00000310.name: UGC 4703B
gmw_00000310.overview: UGC 4703B is a smaller mass companion galaxy located at a projected distance of 81 kpc from NGC 2718 [arXiv:1710.09040]. It is actively interacting with UGC 4703, forming a stellar bridge that contains star-forming regions [arXiv:1710.09040].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 4703B as the smaller companion of UGC 4703, giving its luminosity and linking it to the stellar bridge and star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: Located at a sky projected distance of 81 kpc from NGC 2718, we find that UGC 4703 is clearly interacting with its nearby smaller mass companion UGC 4703B forming a bridge of stellar stream between them. Total B-band luminosity of UGC 4703 and its companion is -17.75 and -16.25 mag, respectively.
We also detected star-forming regions along the UGC 4703/ 4703B bridge with stellar mass exceeding 10 ^ 7 M _ .

gmw_00000311.name: NGC 2718
gmw_00000311.overview: NGC 2718 is an isolated spiral galaxy that is considered a Milky Way analogue [arXiv:1710.09040]. It is located near the interacting dwarf galaxy pair UGC 4703, which lies at a sky-projected distance of 81 kpc [arXiv:1710.09040].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2718 as the isolated Milky-Way-type spiral near the UGC 4703 dwarf pair, making the system a possible Magellanic-Cloud analogue.

quotes.quote: UGC 4703 interacting pair near to an isolated spiral galaxy NGC 2718: A Milky Way Magellanic Cloud analogue Sanjaya Paudel 1 , C. Sengupta 2 ^1 Department of Astronomy Center for Galaxy Evolution Research, Yonsei University, Seoul 03722, Korea ^2 Department of Astronomy, Yonsei University, 50 Yonsei-ro, Seodaemun-gu, Seoul, Republic of Korea 2 Email: sanjpaudel@gmail.com We present an analysis of physical and morphological properties of an interacting pair of dwarf galaxies UGC 4703 located at the vicinity of an isolated Milky-Way (MW) type spiral galaxy NGC 2718. Based on the comparison of physical and morphological properties with that of the Large and Small Magellanic Clouds (LMC and SMC), we report that the UGC 4703 pair-NGC 2718 system is probably an LMC-SMC-MW analogue. Located at a sky projected distance of 81 kpc from NGC 2718, we find that UGC 4703 is clearly interacting with its nearby smaller mass companion UGC 4703B forming a bridge of stellar stream between them.

gmw_00000312.name: DDO 47
gmw_00000312.overview: DDO 47 is an isolated, gas-rich dwarf irregular galaxy located approximately 5.2 Mpc away [arXiv:2006.01735]. It features a cored dark matter halo [arXiv:astro-ph/0510607, arXiv:0811.4289], strong gas flaring [arXiv:2010.07948], and an outer neutral hydrogen disk containing multiple holes and a spiral structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0203133, arXiv:2006.01735].

sources.summary: The paper describes DDO 47 as lacking an obvious stellar bar despite crinkled velocity contours, with a less coherent noncircular H I filament possibly tied to disk structure.

quotes.quote: DDO 47 also seems to have a crinkle pattern in the \ velocity contours as shown in the velocity field (mom1) image in Figure fig-d47crinkle . However, unlike DDO 133 a stellar bar is not obvious. There is a small elongated structure (red in V image in Figure fig-d47crinkle ) near the center of the galaxy, but it is associated with star formation in the FUV image in Figure fig-maps2 and it is not clear that it is a bar structure. According to bosma81 , kinematic patterns like that due to a bar can also be the result of warps in the \ disk. Alternatively, in a study of the gas structure in DDO 47, gentile05 suggest that the filamentary structure seen in the total \ is a spiral structure.

The filament in DDO 47 is less coherent and is delineated by regions 9-12 (and possibly region 8) in Figure fig-maps2 . The gas in the filament is 5.3 10^6 M , which is 1 of the total \ mass of the galaxy, and has median velocities 4-8 \ higher than in the nearby bulk rotation gas. An example of a cut through the \ cube at a point in the middle of region 11 is shown in Figure fig-d47fil .

sources.summary: For DDO 47, the paper measures SHK91 No. 18, derives an oxygen abundance consistent with prior work, detects He II, and excludes O+3 from the comparison abundance.

quotes.quote: DDO 47 obtained spectra for the \ region SHK91 No. 18 in DDO 47 using the IIDS spectrometer on the KPNO 2.1-m telescope. With their \ measurement, they derived an oxygen abundance of 12 + log(O/H) = 7.89 0.20 . In our spectrum, we measured \ and derived an oxygen abundance of 12 + log(O/H) = 7.92 0.06 , in agreement with . We have also measured He II 4686 .

In \ region SHK91 No. 18, the He II 4686 \ to \ flux ratio is about 10 percent, which implies that the resulting contribution by O ^ +3 to the total oxygen abundance could be of order 10 . For comparison with other dI's, we have not included any O ^ +3 contribution in the total oxygen abundance for DDO 47.

sources.summary: The paper uses DDO 47 as one of two detailed rotation-curve test cases, comparing its pseudo-isothermal or Burkert halo density with conservative NFW halo predictions.

quotes.quote: From our sample 1 we select DDO 47 and ESO 287-G13, as the best examples to show individually the outer NFW CDM-data discrepancy. Then, we use the combined samples 1-2 to compare, at the outermost radii, the NFW CDM predictions, obtained by a newly discovered structural CDM relation, with the values of the mass, radius and density of the DM halos around galaxies. We analyse in detail the density profiles of the DM halos for the two best cases in our sample: DDO 47 (Salucci, Walter and Borriello 2003, Gentile et al. 2005) and ESO 287-G13 (Gentile et al. 2004).

Notice that in both objects we have also considered a Burkert halo (Burkert 1995), and found results coincident with the PI halo. NFW halos Let us assume that: M_ NFW (R_ f )=M_ PI (R_ f ) where M_ PI (R_ f ) is the mass inside R_ f we derive from the above mass model. Eq. masseq allows to derive the values for and M_ vir : 18.4 and 6 10^ 10 M_ for DDO 47, and 13.3 and 7 10^ 11 M_ for ESO 287-G13.

sources.summary: The paper applies RGGR to dwarf disk galaxy DDO 47, a dark-matter test case, using observed baryonic matter, rotation-curve data, and gas surface-density choices.

quotes.quote: DDO 47 Here we present part of a new result from a working in progress . The dwarf disk galaxy DDO 47 is cited as a paradigmatic galaxy for testing dark matter effects , and in particular its fits using the NFW profile vary from bad (using the two NFW halo parameters as free parameters) to a disaster (if one of the parameters is fixed in accordance with N-body simulations expectations) . Here we use the same data and conventions used in Ref. for the baryonic matter and observed rotation curve, except for the gas surface density. For the latter, we directly use the gas surface density provided by Ref. , which leads to a gas rotation curve less smooth than the one presented in Ref. . The results are not significantly sensitive to either of the gas profiles and are shown in Fig. DDO47CosmoSul .

sources.summary: The paper describes DDO 47 as an isolated gas-rich dwarf with 19 H I holes, including a supershell and a newly detected large hole.

quotes.quote: DDO 47 DDO 47 (PGC 21600, UGC 3974) is an isolated gas-rich dwarf about 5.2 Mpc away. It has 19 holes, about half of which are Type 1, including a supershell (No. 15), which were found in the previous study done by . However, we detected another large hole (No. \ 4) which is not in their list.

sources.summary: DDO 47 is described with outer HI spiral structure lacking optical emission, low-inclination elliptical HI isophotes, distributed high-column-density regions, and a deep HI hole.

quotes.quote: DDO 47: Spiral structure in the outer HI disk was reported by Puche Westpfahl (1994). No optical emission appears associated with the spiral arms. Regularly shaped, elliptical outer HI isophotes suggest a disk seen at low inclination. A number of high column density regions are distributed evenly over the disk. There is a deep hole in the HI distribution at = 7^h39^m8^s.1, = 16^ 54' 31'' .

sources.summary: The paper uses HI kinematics of DDO 47 to test whether triaxial halo motions could hide a cusp, finding weak non-circular motions and a genuinely cored dark matter halo.

quotes.quote: We present HI data of the dwarf galaxy DDO 47, aimed at testing the hypothesis that dark halo triaxiality might induce non-circular motions resulting in rotation curves best fitted by cored halos, even if the dark matter halo is intrinsically cuspy. We performed a harmonic decomposition of the velocity field in order to search for alleged non-circular motions needed to ``hide'' a cusp: in DDO 47 non-circular motions are globally at a level of 2-3 km s ^ 1 , far from being sufficient to reconcile the observed rotation curve with the CDM predictions. We conclude that the dark matter halo around DDO 47 is truly cored and that a cusp cannot be hidden by non-circular motions.

We have used DDO 47 to explore the possibility that solid-body rotation curves (usually interpreted as a signature of constant density cores) would arise naturally also in NFW halos as a result of a triaxial dark matter mass distribution that hides the steep central density cusp through deviations from axial symmetry in the disk. DDO 47 is one of the most clear cases with kinematical properties that are inconsistent with NFW (Fig. 1, left panel).

sources.summary: The paper describes DDO 47 as a nearby regular dwarf irregular whose gas kinematics and rotation curve favor a cored Burkert dark-matter halo over an NFW cusp.

quotes.quote: DDO 47 is a dwarf irregular galaxy at 4 Mpc distance. The galaxy has a fairly regular structure. The velocity field of the gas looks regular, rendering this galaxy a convenient object for kinematic studies.
It is obvious that the rotation curve of DDO 47 is in conflict with an NFW law, while a Burkert law yields a rather good fit.

sources.summary: The thesis describes DDO 47 as a nearby dwarf whose regular H I kinematics and rotation-curve modeling favor a shallow cored dark halo over an NFW cusp.

quotes.quote: As an example, it is worth to discuss in detail the case of the nearby dwarf galaxy DDO 47 . The HI observations have adequate resolution and sensitivity, showing that the HI 2D kinematics is very regular, with a well-behaved velocity field. The observed velocity along the line of sight V_ los has been decomposed in terms of harmonic coefficients: V_ los = c_0 + _ j=1 ^n [ c_j cos (j ) + s_j sin (j ) ] where is the azimuthal angle, c_0 is the systemic velocity, c_1 is the rotation velocity; it is found that the coefficients s_1 , s_3 j_2 have a small amplitude that excludes significant global elongation and lopsidedness of the potential and detects non-circular motions with amplitude and radial profile very different from that necessary to hide a cuspy density distribution in the observed RC. The RC mass modeling, shown in Fig. fig:ddo47 , finds that the DDO 47 dark halo has a core radius of about 7 kpc and a central density _0 = 1.4 10^ 24 g cm ^ 3 , i.e. a much shallower distribution than that predicted by the NFW profile.

sources.summary: The paper describes DDO 47 as having approximately constant HI velocity dispersion, strong gas flaring, and literature HWHM trends that differ from the authors' scale-height profile.

quotes.quote: We note that the velocity dispersion is approximately constant for some galaxies (e.g. WLM, DDO 47), but in general _ HI (R) declines with increasing galactocentric radius.
The gas distribution flares of a factor ranging from a minimum of 1.1-1.6 (but with large uncertainty) for DDO 101 and DDO 87 to a maximum of 4-4.5 (e.g. DDO 52, DDO 47).

For some galaxies in common with our sample, the HWHM calculated by is also compatible within the uncertainties with those in Fig. fig:hHI_all (e.g. DDO 50, DDO 87, DDO 133), but others are significantly different (e.g. WLM, DDO 47, DDO 101, DDO 126). For example, the flaring of the scale height is much steeper than ours in some disc outskirts (e.g. WLM) and the radial trend is clearly not monotonic with the galactocentric radius (e.g. DDO 47).

sources.summary: The review uses DDO 47 as a dwarf spiral test case where rotation-curve modeling favors a large cored halo and rejects an NFW cusp.

quotes.quote: As a test case, we consider the nearby dwarf spiral galaxy DDO 47 (see Fig.(8)). Its RC modelling finds that in this galaxy the dark halo density must have a core of 7 kpc and a central density of _0 = 1.4 10^ 24 g cm ^ 3 The NFW halo profile, instead, is totally unable to fit the RC see Fig (8). It is important to stress that the cusped halo distributions do fail, not (only) because they fit poorly the RCs, but also because (see e.g. ): they often imply implausibly values for the stellar mass-to-light ratio and/or the halo mass they often do not follow the CDM concentration vs halo mass relationship
